english.author: brahmali
english.text: The settling of legal issues

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo satta adhikaraṇasamathā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, these seven principles for the settling of legal issues come up for recitation.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the settling and resolving of legal issues whenever they arise there is:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Resolution face-to-face to be applied;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on relinquishment

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on almsbowls

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 1. The training rule on collecting almsbowls

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo tiṁsa nissaggiyā pācittiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these thirty rules on relinquishment and confession come up for recitation.

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo bahū patte sannicayaṁ karonti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns from the group of six had collected a large number of almsbowls.

segments.pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized those nuns,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo bahū patte sannicayaṁ karissanti, pattavāṇijjaṁ vā bhikkhuniyo karissanti, āmattikāpaṇaṁ vā pasāressantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns collect a large number of bowls? Will they start up as bowl merchants or set up a bowl shop?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized those nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six collect bowls?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo pattasannicayaṁ karontī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nuns from the group of six do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo pattasannicayaṁ karissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six collect bowls?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī pattasannicayaṁ kareyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun collects almsbowls, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An almsbowl:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are two kinds of bowls: the iron bowl and the ceramic bowl.

segments.pali: Tayo pattassa vaṇṇā— ukkaṭṭho patto, majjhimo patto, omako patto.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And there are three sizes of bowls: the large bowl, the medium bowl, and the small bowl.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The large bowl:

segments.pali: aḍḍhāḷhakodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it holds half an āḷhaka measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The medium bowl:

segments.pali: nāḷikodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it holds a nāḷika measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The small bowl:

segments.pali: patthodanaṁ gaṇhāti catubhāgaṁ khādanaṁ tadupiyaṁ byañjanaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it holds a pattha measure of cooked rice, a fourth part of fresh food, and a suitable amount of curry.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Anything larger than this is not a bowl, nor anything smaller.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Collects:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: almsbowls that have neither been determined nor assigned to another.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing relinquishment:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: entailing relinquishment at dawn.

segments.pali: Nissajjitabbo saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The bowl should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this.

segments.pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After approaching the Sangha, that nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

segments.pali: “ayaṁ me, ayye, patto rattātikkanto nissaggiyo, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, this almsbowl, which I have kept for more than one day, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After relinquishing it, she should confess the offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The confession should be received by a competent and capable nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The relinquished bowl is then to be given back:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ patto itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā nissaggiyo saṅghassa nissaṭṭho.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This almsbowl, which was to be relinquished by the nun so-and-so, has been relinquished to the Sangha.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho imaṁ pattaṁ itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā dadeyyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should give this bowl back to nun so-and-so.”

segments.pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā sambahulā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassu vacanīyā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Or: after approaching several nuns, that nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

segments.pali: “ayaṁ me, ayyāyo, patto rattātikkanto nissaggiyo, imāhaṁ ayyānaṁ nissajjāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this almsbowl, which I have kept for more than one day, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to you.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After relinquishing it, she should confess the offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The confession should be received by a competent and capable nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The relinquished bowl is then to be given back:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please, venerables, I ask you to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ patto itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā nissaggiyo ayyānaṁ nissaṭṭho.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This almsbowl, which was to be relinquished by the nun so-and-so, has been relinquished to you.

segments.pali: Yadi ayyānaṁ pattakallaṁ, ayyāyo, imaṁ pattaṁ itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā dadeyyun”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the venerables are ready, you should give this bowl back to nun so-and-so.”

segments.pali: Tāya bhikkhuniyā ekaṁ bhikkhuniṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Or: after approaching a single nun, that nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder, squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “This almsbowl, which I have kept for more than one day, is to be relinquished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I relinquish it to you.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After relinquishing it, she should confess the offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The confession should be received by that nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The relinquished bowl is then to be given back:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I give this almsbowl back to you.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is more than one day and she perceives it as more, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is more than one day, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is more than one day, but she perceives it as less, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been determined, but she perceives that it has, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been assigned to another, but she perceives that it has, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been given away, but she perceives that it has, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been not lost, but she perceives that it has …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been destroyed, but she perceives that it has …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been broken, but she perceives that it has …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been stolen, but she perceives that it has, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Nissaggiyaṁ pattaṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she uses an almsbowl that should be relinquished without first relinquishing it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is less than one day, but she perceives it as more, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is less than one day, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is less than one day and she perceives it as less, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: antoaruṇe adhiṭṭheti, vikappeti, vissajjeti, nassati, vinassati, bhijjati, acchinditvā gaṇhanti, vissāsaṁ gaṇhanti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, before dawn, it has been determined, assigned to another, given away, lost, destroyed, broken, stolen, or taken on trust;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo nissaṭṭhapattaṁ na denti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the nuns from the group of six did not give back a relinquished bowl.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the Buddha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, a relinquished almsbowl should be given back.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun doesn’t give it back, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on relinquishment

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on almsbowls

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 2. Distributing out-of-season robe-cloth

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo gāmakāvāse vassaṁvuṭṭhā sāvatthiṁ agamaṁsu vattasampannā iriyāpathasampannā duccoḷā lūkhacīvarā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time, after completing the rainy-season residence in a village monastery, a number of nuns were traveling to Sāvatthī. They were perfect in conduct and deportment, but poorly dressed in shabby robes.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Some lay followers who saw them thought,

segments.pali: “imā bhikkhuniyo vattasampannā iriyāpathasampannā duccoḷā lūkhacīvarā, imā bhikkhuniyo acchinnā bhavissantī”ti bhikkhunisaṅghassa akālacīvaraṁ adaṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “These nuns are perfect in conduct and deportment, but poorly dressed in shabby robes; they must’ve been robbed,” and they gave out-of-season robe-cloth to the Sangha of nuns.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because they had performed the robe-making ceremony, the nun Thullanandā

english.author: brahmali
english.text: determined it as in-season robe-cloth, and then distributed it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The lay followers asked those nuns whether

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they had obtained any robe-cloth.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They replied that they hadn’t

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and told them what had happened.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those lay followers then complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpessatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā determine out-of-season robe-cloth as ‘in-season’, and then distribute it?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ upāsakānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those lay followers,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpessatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā do this?”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those nuns then told the monks,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who in turn told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks:

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī akālacīvaraṁ “kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun determines out-of-season robe-cloth as “in-season”, and then distributes it, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Out-of-season robe-cloth:

segments.pali: anatthate kathine ekādasamāse uppannaṁ, atthate kathine sattamāse uppannaṁ, kālepi ādissa dinnaṁ, etaṁ akālacīvaraṁ nāma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is robe-cloth given during the eleven months. For one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is robe-cloth given during the seven months. Also, if it is given in the robe season, but the cloth is designated, it is called “out-of-season robe-cloth”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she distributes it after determining the out-of-season robe-cloth as “in-season”, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment.

segments.pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (To be expanded as in Bi NP 1:2.1.21–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.)

segments.pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, akālacīvaraṁ ‘kālacīvaran’ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this out-of-season robe-cloth, which I distributed after determining it as “in-season”, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “the Sangha should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “you should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I give this robe-cloth back to you.”

segments.pali: Akālacīvare akālacīvarasaññā “kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is out-of-season robe-cloth and she perceives it as such, and she distributes it after determining it as “in-season”, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Akālacīvare vematikā “kālacīvaran”ti adhiṭṭhahitvā bhājāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is out-of-season robe-cloth, but she is unsure of it, and she distributes it after determining it as “in-season”, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is out-of-season robe-cloth, but she perceives it as in-season robe-cloth, and she distributes it after determining it as “in-season”, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is in-season robe-cloth, but she perceives it as out-of-season robe-cloth, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is in-season robe-cloth, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is in-season robe-cloth and she perceives it as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she distributes out-of-season robe-cloth that she perceives as in-season;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she distributes in-season robe-cloth that she perceives as in-season;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on relinquishment

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 3. Taking back a traded robe

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī aññatarāya bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattetvā paribhuñji.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā was using a robe she had received after trading with another nun.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ saṅgharitvā nikkhipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But the other nun folded up the robe she had received and put it aside.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā then said to her,

segments.pali: “yaṁ te, ayye, mayā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattitaṁ, kahaṁ taṁ cīvaran”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, where’s that robe that I traded with you?”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ nīharitvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā dassesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She brought out the robe and showed it to her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and Thullanandā said,

segments.pali: “handāyye, tuyhaṁ cīvaraṁ, āhara metaṁ cīvaraṁ, yaṁ tuyhaṁ tuyhamevetaṁ, yaṁ mayhaṁ mayhamevetaṁ, āhara metaṁ, sakaṁ paccāharā”ti acchindi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Here’s your robe and give me that robe of mine. That which is yours is yours and that which is mine is mine. Give me that and take back what’s yours.” And she just took it.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun then told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā trade a robe with a nun and then take it back?”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those nuns then told the monks,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who in turn told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks:

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā trade a robe with a nun and then take it back?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ parivattetvā sā pacchā evaṁ vadeyya—‘handāyye, tuyhaṁ cīvaraṁ āhara, metaṁ cīvaraṁ, yaṁ tuyhaṁ tuyhamevetaṁ, yaṁ mayhaṁ mayhamevetaṁ, āhara metaṁ, sakaṁ paccāharā’ti acchindeyya vā acchindāpeyya vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun trades robes with a nun and then says, “Here’s your robe; give me that robe of mine. That which is yours is yours, and that which is mine is mine. Give me that, and take back what’s yours;” and she just takes it or has it taken, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: With a nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: with another nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Robes:

segments.pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Trades:

segments.pali: parittena vā vipulaṁ, vipulena vā parittaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: much with little or little with much.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just takes it:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she just takes it herself, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has it taken:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asks another, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Sakiṁ āṇattā bahukampi acchindati, nissaggiyaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she only asks once, then even if the other takes back many, it becomes subject to relinquishment.

segments.pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The robe-cloth should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (To be expanded as in Bi NP 1:2.1.21–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.)

segments.pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, cīvaraṁ bhikkhuniyā saddhiṁ parivattetvā acchinnaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this robe-cloth, which I took back after trading it with a nun, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “the Sangha should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “you should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I give this robe-cloth back to you.”

segments.pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained and she perceives her as such, and after trading robe-cloth with her she takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya vematikā cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and after trading robe-cloth with her she takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā cīvaraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and after trading robe-cloth with her she takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she trades another requisite, and then takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Anupasampannāya saddhiṁ cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ parivattetvā acchindati vā acchindāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she trades robe-cloth or another requisite with someone who is not fully ordained, and then takes it back or has it taken back, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if the other nun gives it back;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she takes it on trust from her;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 4. Asking for something else

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī gilānā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Thullanandā was sick.

segments.pali: Atha kho aññataro upāsako yena thullanandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A lay follower went to her and asked,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, what’s wrong with you? What may I get you?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I need ghee.”

segments.pali: Atha kho so upāsako aññatarassa āpaṇikassa gharā kahāpaṇassa sappiṁ āharitvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā adāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That lay follower then brought back from a shop a kahāpaṇa’s worth of ghee and gave it to Thullanandā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I don’t need ghee;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I need oil.”

segments.pali: Atha kho so upāsako yena so āpaṇiko tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ āpaṇikaṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The lay follower returned to the shopkeeper and said,

segments.pali: “na kirāyyo, ayyāya sappinā attho, telena attho.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It seems the nun doesn’t need ghee, but oil.

segments.pali: Handa te sappiṁ, telaṁ me dehī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Here’s your ghee; please give me oil.”

segments.pali: “Sace mayaṁ, ayyo, vikkītaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ puna ādiyissāma, kadā amhākaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ vikkāyissati;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sir, if we were to take back goods that have been sold, when would our goods be sold?

segments.pali: sappissa kayena sappi haṭaṁ, telassa kayaṁ āhara, telaṁ harissasī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When ghee is bought, ghee is taken away. Buying oil, you receive that, and you’ll take that away.”

segments.pali: Atha kho so upāsako ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That lay follower then complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpessatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā ask for one thing and then for something else?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa upāsakassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of that lay follower,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her …

segments.pali: atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those nuns then told the monks,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who in turn told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks:

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā ask for one thing and then for something else?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun asks for one thing and then for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Asks for one thing:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whatever she asks for.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then for something else:

segments.pali: taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: apart from that thing, if she asks for something else, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment.

segments.pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (To be expanded as in Bi NP 1:2.1.21–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.)

segments.pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññaṁ viññāpetvā aññaṁ viññāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this thing, which I asked for after asking for something else, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “the Sangha should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “you should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I give this back to you.”

segments.pali: Aññe aññasaññā aññaṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is something else and she perceives it as such, and she asks for that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññe vematikā aññaṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is something else, but she is unsure of it, and she asks for that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññe anaññasaññā aññaṁ viññāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is something else, but she does not perceive it as such, and she asks for that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Anaññe aññasaññā anaññaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not something else, but she perceives it as such, and she asks for that, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Anaññe vematikā anaññaṁ viññāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not something else, but she is unsure of it, and she asks for that, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not something else, and she does not perceive it as such, and she asks for that, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asks for both at the same time;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she can show a benefit in asking;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 5. Exchanging for something else

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī gilānā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Thullanandā was sick.

segments.pali: Atha kho aññataro upāsako yena thullanandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A lay follower went to her and asked,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I hope you’re bearing up, venerable, I hope you’re getting better?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I’m not bearing up, and I’m not getting better.”

segments.pali: “Amukassa, ayye, āpaṇikassa ghare kahāpaṇaṁ nikkhipissāmi, tato yaṁ iccheyyāsi taṁ āharāpeyyāsī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “We’ll deposit a kahāpaṇa coin in such-and-such a shop. Please get whatever you wish from there.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā then said to a trainee nun,

segments.pali: “gaccha, sikkhamāne, amukassa āpaṇikassa gharā kahāpaṇassa telaṁ āharā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Go to such-and-such a shop and bring back a kahāpaṇa’s worth of oil.”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā sikkhamānā tassa āpaṇikassa gharā kahāpaṇassa telaṁ āharitvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā adāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That trainee nun did just that and gave it to Thullanandā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā said,

segments.pali: “na me, sikkhamāne, telena attho, sappinā me attho”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I don’t need oil; I need ghee.”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā sikkhamānā yena so āpaṇiko tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ āpaṇikaṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The trainee nun returned to the shopkeeper and said,

segments.pali: “na kira, āvuso, ayyāya telena attho, sappinā attho, handa te telaṁ, sappiṁ me dehī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It seems the nun doesn’t need oil, but ghee. Here’s your oil; please give me ghee.”

segments.pali: “Sace mayaṁ, ayye, vikkītaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ puna ādiyissāma, kadā amhākaṁ bhaṇḍaṁ vikkāyissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, if we were to take back goods that have been sold, when would our goods be sold?

segments.pali: Telassa kayena telaṁ haṭaṁ, sappissa kayaṁ āhara, sappiṁ harissasī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When oil is bought, oil is taken away. Buying ghee, you receive that, and you’ll take that away.”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā sikkhamānā rodantī aṭṭhāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The trainee nun started to cry.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns asked her

english.author: brahmali
english.text: why,

segments.pali: Atha kho sā sikkhamānā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and she told them what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā get one thing in exchange and then something else?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā get one thing in exchange and then something else?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gets one thing in exchange and then something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gets one thing in exchange:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whatever she gets in exchange.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then something else:

segments.pali: taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ cetāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: apart from that thing, if she gets something else in exchange, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment.

segments.pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (To be expanded as in Bi NP 1:2.1.21–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.)

segments.pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññaṁ cetāpetvā aññaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this thing, which I got in exchange, having first gotten something else in exchange, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “the Sangha should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “you should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I give this back to you.”

segments.pali: Aññe aññasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is something else and she perceives it as such, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññe vematikā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is something else, but she is unsure of it, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññe anaññasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is something else, but she does not perceive it as such, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Anaññe aññasaññā anaññaṁ cetāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not something else, but she perceives it as such, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Anaññe vematikā anaññaṁ cetāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not something else, but she is unsure of it, and she gets that in exchange, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not something else, and she does not perceive it as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets both in exchange at the same time;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she can show a benefit in doing the exchange;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 6. Exchanging designated Sangha property

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena upāsakā bhikkhunisaṅghassa cīvaratthāya chandakaṁ saṅgharitvā aññatarassa pāvārikassa ghare parikkhāraṁ nikkhipitvā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the lay followers collected voluntary contributions to supply the Sangha of nuns with robes. They stored the requisites in a cloth merchant’s shop, and then went to the nuns and said,

segments.pali: “amukassa, ayye, pāvārikassa ghare cīvaratthāya parikkhāro nikkhitto, tato cīvaraṁ āharāpetvā bhājethā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, requisites to be used for robes are stored in such-and-such a cloth merchant’s shop. Please have someone get cloth from there and share it out.”

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo tena parikkhārena bhesajjaṁ cetāpetvā paribhuñjiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But the nuns exchanged those requisites for tonics, which they then used.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the lay followers found out about this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpessantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When requisites belonging to the Sangha are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ upāsakānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those lay followers,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When requisites belonging to the Sangha are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When requisites belonging to the Sangha are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘When a requisite belonging to the Sangha is designated for a specific purpose, if a nun exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A requisite is designated for a specific purpose:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it was given for a specific purpose.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Belonging to the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: belonging to the Sangha, not to a group, not to an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Exchanges it for something else:

segments.pali: yaṁatthāya dinnaṁ, taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ cetāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, apart from the purpose for which it was given, she exchanges it for something else, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment.

segments.pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (To be expanded as in Bi NP 1:2.1.21–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.)

segments.pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena saṅghikena aññaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this thing, which I got in exchange for a requisite belonging to the Sangha that was designated for a specific purpose, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “the Sangha should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “you should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I give this back to you.”

segments.pali: Aññadatthike aññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is for a specific purpose and she perceives that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññadatthike vematikā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññadatthike anaññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is for a specific purpose, but she does not perceive that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she receives in return what had been relinquished, it is to be used in accordance with the intention of the donors.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not for a specific purpose, but she perceives that it is, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not for a specific purpose, and she does not perceive that it is, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses the remainder;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses it after getting permission from the owners;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 8. Exchanging designated collective property

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa pūgassa pariveṇavāsikā bhikkhuniyo yāguyā kilamanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns staying in the yard belonging to a certain association were weak because of a lack of congee.

segments.pali: Atha kho so pūgo bhikkhunīnaṁ yāguatthāya chandakaṁ saṅgharitvā aññatarassa āpaṇikassa ghare parikkhāraṁ nikkhipitvā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after collecting voluntary contributions to supply the nuns with congee, that association stored the ingredients in a shop. They then went to the nuns and said,

segments.pali: “amukassa, ayye, āpaṇikassa ghare yāguatthāya parikkhāro nikkhitto, tato taṇḍulaṁ āharāpetvā yāguṁ pacāpetvā paribhuñjathā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, congee ingredients are stored in such-and-such a shop. Please have someone get rice from there, have congee cooked, and then eat it.”

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo tena parikkhārena bhesajjaṁ cetāpetvā paribhuñjiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But the nuns exchanged those ingredients for tonics, which they then used.

segments.pali: So pūgo jānitvā ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When that association found out about this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena aññaṁ cetāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When collective ingredients are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena aññaṁ cetāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena aññaṁ cetāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When collective ingredients are designated for a specific purpose, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena aññaṁ cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘When a collective requisite is designated for a specific purpose, if a nun exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A requisite is designated for a specific purpose:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it was given for a specific purpose.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Collective:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: belonging to a group, not to the Sangha, not to an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Exchanges it for something else:

segments.pali: yaṁatthāya dinnaṁ taṁ ṭhapetvā aññaṁ cetāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, apart from the purpose for which it was given, she exchanges it for something else, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment.

segments.pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (To be expanded as in Bi NP 1:2.1.21–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.)

segments.pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena aññaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this thing, which I got in exchange for a collective requisite that was designated for a specific purpose, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “the Sangha should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “you should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I give this back to you.”

segments.pali: Aññadatthike aññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is for a specific purpose and she perceives that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññadatthike vematikā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

segments.pali: Aññadatthike anaññadatthikasaññā aññaṁ cetāpeti, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is for a specific purpose, but she does not perceive that it is, and she exchanges it for something else, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she receives in return what had been relinquished, it is to be used in accordance with the intention of the donors.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not for a specific purpose, but she perceives that it is, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not for a specific purpose, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not for a specific purpose, and she does not perceive that it is, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses the remainder;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses it after getting permission from the owners;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 9. Exchanging designated collective property that was asked for

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa pūgassa pariveṇavāsikā bhikkhuniyo yāguyā kilamanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns staying in the yard belonging to a certain association were weak because of a lack of congee.

segments.pali: Atha kho so pūgo bhikkhunīnaṁ yāguatthāya chandakaṁ saṅgharitvā aññatarassa āpaṇikassa ghare parikkhāraṁ nikkhipitvā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after collecting voluntary contributions to supply the nuns with congee, that association stored the ingredients in a shop. They then went to the nuns and said,

segments.pali: “amukassa, ayye, āpaṇikassa ghare yāguatthāya parikkhāro nikkhitto.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, congee ingredients are stored in such-and-such a shop.

segments.pali: Tato taṇḍule āharāpetvā yāguṁ pacāpetvā paribhuñjathā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please have someone get rice from there, have congee cooked, and then eat it.”

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo tena ca parikkhārena sayampi yācitvā bhesajjaṁ cetāpetvā paribhuñjiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But even though they had asked for them, the nuns exchanged those ingredients for tonics, which they then used.

segments.pali: So pūgo jānitvā ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When that association found out about this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When collective ingredients are designated for a specific purpose and were asked for, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena mahājanikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When collective ingredients are designated for a specific purpose and were asked for, how could the nuns exchange them for something else?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 10. Exchanging designated personal property that was asked for

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Many people visited her.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā pariveṇaṁ undriyati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then the yard of Thullanandā’s dwelling was deteriorating.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People asked her why,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and she replied, “I have neither donor nor worker.”

segments.pali: Atha kho te manussā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā pariveṇatthāya chandakaṁ saṅgharitvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā parikkhāraṁ adaṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after collecting voluntary contributions for the yard of Thullanandā’s dwelling, the people gave the collected requisites to Thullanandā.

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī tena ca parikkhārena sayampi yācitvā bhesajjaṁ cetāpetvā paribhuñji.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But even though she had asked for them, Thullanandā exchanged those requisites for tonics, which she then used.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the people found out about this, they complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena puggalikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When personal requisites are designated for a specific purpose and were asked for, how could the nun Thullanandā exchange them for something else?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena puggalikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññadatthikena parikkhārena aññuddisikena puggalikena saññācikena aññaṁ cetāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When personal requisites are designated for a specific purpose and were asked for, how could the nun Thullanandā exchange them for something else?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 11. Getting an expensive heavy cloak in exchange

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings.

segments.pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo sītakāle mahagghaṁ kambalaṁ pārupitvā yena thullanandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On one occasion when the weather was cold, King Pasenadi of Kosala put on an expensive woolen cloak and went to Thullanandā. He bowed and sat down.

segments.pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And Thullanandā instructed, inspired, and gladdened him with a teaching.

segments.pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He then said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, please say what you need.”

segments.pali: “Sace me tvaṁ, mahārāja, dātukāmosi, imaṁ kambalaṁ dehī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Great king, if you wish to give me something, then give me this woolen cloak.”

segments.pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā kambalaṁ datvā uṭṭhāyāsanā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The king gave her his cloak. He then got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated her with his right side toward her, and left.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “These nuns have great desires; they are not content.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: How can they ask the king for his woolen cloak?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā rājānaṁ kambalaṁ viññāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā ask the king for his woolen cloak?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī rājānaṁ kambalaṁ viññāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā asked for this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī rājānaṁ kambalaṁ viññāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā ask the king for his woolen cloak?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Garupāvuraṇaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā cetāpentiyā catukkaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpetabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun carries out an exchange to get a heavy cloak, it is to be worth at most four kaṁsa coins. If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A heavy cloak:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whatever cloak is used in cold weather.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Carries out an exchange to get:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: asks for.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It is to be worth at most four kaṁsa coins:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it may be worth sixteen kahāpaṇa coins.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asks for one worth more than that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she gets it, it becomes subject to relinquishment.

segments.pali: Nissajjitabbaṁ saṅghassa vā gaṇassa vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It should be relinquished to a sangha, a group, or an individual nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, it should be relinquished like this. …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (To be expanded as in Bi NP 1:2.1.21–2.1.43, with appropriate substitutions.)

segments.pali: “idaṁ me, ayye, garupāvuraṇaṁ atirekacatukkaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpitaṁ nissaggiyaṁ, imāhaṁ saṅghassa nissajjāmī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this heavy cloak worth more than four kaṁsa coins, which I got in exchange, is to be relinquished. I relinquish it to the Sangha.” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “the Sangha should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “you should give …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I give this back to you.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is worth more than four kaṁsa coins, and she perceives it as such, and she gets it in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is worth more than four kaṁsa coins, but she is unsure of it, and she gets it in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is worth more than four kaṁsa coins, but she perceives it as being worth less, and she gets it in exchange, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is worth less than four kaṁsa coins, but she perceives it as being worth more, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is worth less than four kaṁsa coins, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is worth less than four kaṁsa coins, and she perceives it as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she carries out an exchange for one worth at most four kaṁsa coins;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she carries out an exchange for one worth less than four kaṁsa coins;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is from relatives;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is from those who have given an invitation;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is for the benefit of someone else;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is by means of her own property;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets one in exchange that has little value from someone who wants to exchange one of great value;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eleventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 12. Getting an expensive light cloak in exchange

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings.

segments.pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo uṇhakāle mahagghaṁ khomaṁ pārupitvā yena thullanandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On one occasion when the weather was warm, King Pasenadi of Kosala put on an expensive linen cloak and went to Thullanandā. He bowed and sat down.

segments.pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And Thullanandā instructed, inspired, and gladdened him with a teaching.

segments.pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He then said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, please say what you need.”

segments.pali: “Sace me tvaṁ, mahārāja, dātukāmosi, imaṁ khomaṁ dehī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Great king, if you wish to give me something, then give me this linen cloak.”

segments.pali: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā khomaṁ datvā uṭṭhāyāsanā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The king gave her his cloak. He then got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated her with his right side toward her, and left.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “These nuns have great desires; they are not content.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: How can they ask the king for his linen cloak?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā rājānaṁ khomaṁ viññāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā ask the king for his linen cloak?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī rājānaṁ khomaṁ viññāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā asked for this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī rājānaṁ khomaṁ viññāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā ask the king for his linen cloak?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Lahupāvuraṇaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā cetāpentiyā aḍḍhateyyakaṁsaparamaṁ cetāpetabbaṁ. Tato ce uttari cetāpeyya, nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun carries out an exchange to get a light cloak, it is to be worth at most two-and-a-half kaṁsa coins. If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.’”

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It is to be worth at most two-and-a-half kaṁsa coins:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it may be worth ten kahāpaṇa coins.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she gets one in exchange that is worth more than that:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asks for one worth more than that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she carries out an exchange for one worth at most two-and-a-half kaṁsa coins;

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
