english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is for the benefit of someone else;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is by means of her own property;

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The twelfth training rule is finished.

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, tiṁsa nissaggiyā pācittiyā dhammā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, the thirty rules on relinquishment and confession have been recited.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing relinquishment in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on garlic

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 1. The training rule on eating garlic

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo chasaṭṭhisatā pācittiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these one hundred and sixty-six rules on confession come up for recitation.

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarena upāsakena bhikkhunisaṅgho lasuṇena pavārito hoti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a lay follower had invited the Sangha of nuns to ask for garlic:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If any of the nuns need garlic, I’ll supply it.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And he had told his field-keeper,

segments.pali: “sace bhikkhuniyo āgacchanti, ekamekāya bhikkhuniyā dvetayo bhaṇḍike dehī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If the nuns come, give two or three bulbs to each nun.”

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyaṁ ussavo hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On that occasion they were holding a celebration in Sāvatthī,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the garlic was used up as soon as it arrived there.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then the nuns went to that lay follower and said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “We need garlic.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, there isn’t any.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: As soon as the garlic arrives, it’s used up.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please go to the field.”

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī khettaṁ gantvā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ lasuṇaṁ harāpesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun Thullanandā went to the field, and not having any sense of moderation she took a large amount of garlic.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The field-keeper complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ lasuṇaṁ harāpessantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns not have any sense of moderation and take a large amount of garlic?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa khettapālassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of that field-keeper,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ lasuṇaṁ harāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā not have any sense of moderation and take a large amount of garlic?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ lasuṇaṁ harāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her,

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ lasuṇaṁ harāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā not have any sense of moderation and take a large amount of garlic?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and after giving a teaching he addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī aññatarassa brāhmaṇassa pajāpati ahosi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Once upon a time, monks, Thullanandā was the wife of a brahmin.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They had three daughters,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nandā, Nandavatī, and Sundarīnandā.

segments.pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, so brāhmaṇo kālaṁ katvā aññataraṁ haṁsayoniṁ upapajji.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When that brahmin eventually died, he was reborn as a goose,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whose feathers were all made of gold.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And he gave his former family members one feather at a time.

segments.pali: Atha kho, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī ‘ayaṁ haṁso amhākaṁ ekekaṁ pattaṁ detī’ti taṁ haṁsarājaṁ gahetvā nippattaṁ akāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā considered this. She then grabbed hold of that king of geese and plucked him.

segments.pali: Tassa puna jāyamānā pattā setā sampajjiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But when his feathers regrew they were white.

segments.pali: Tadāpi, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī atilobhena suvaṇṇā parihīnā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time too, monks, Thullanandā lost her gold because she was too greedy.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Now she will lose her garlic.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “One should be content with what one gets,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because excessive greed is bad.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After grabbing hold of the king of geese,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The gold came to an end.”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after rebuking the nun Thullanandā in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun eats garlic, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Garlic:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: from Magadha is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is garlic and she perceives it as such, and she eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is garlic, but she is unsure of it, and she eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is garlic, but she does not perceive it as such, and she eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not garlic, but she perceives it as such, and she eats it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not garlic, but she is unsure of it, and she eats it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not garlic and she does not perceive it as such, and she eats it, there is no offense.

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying in the Sakyan country in the Banyan Tree Monastery at Kapilavatthu,

segments.pali: Atha kho mahāpajāpati gotamī yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā adhovāte aṭṭhāsi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Mahāpajāpati Gotamī went to him and bowed. Standing downwind from him, she said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sir, women smell.”

segments.pali: “ādiyantu kho bhikkhuniyo udakasuddhikan”ti, mahāpajāpatiṁ gotamiṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Well then, the nuns should clean themselves with water.” And the Buddha instructed, inspired, and gladdened her with a teaching,

segments.pali: Atha kho mahāpajāpati gotamī bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassitā samādapitā samuttejitā sampahaṁsitā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: after which she bowed down, circumambulated him with her right side toward him, and left.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, I allow the nuns to clean themselves with water.”

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Being aware that the Buddha had allowed cleaning with water,

segments.pali: “bhagavatā udakasuddhikā anuññātā”ti atigambhīraṁ udakasuddhikaṁ ādiyantī muttakaraṇe vaṇaṁ akāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun did it too deeply, causing a sore in her vagina.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ āroceti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun clean herself too deeply with water?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī atigambhīraṁ udakasuddhikaṁ ādiyatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her,

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī atigambhīraṁ udakasuddhikaṁ ādiyissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun clean herself too deeply with water?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Udakasuddhikaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā ādiyamānāya dvaṅgulapabbaparamaṁ ādātabbaṁ. Taṁ atikkāmentiyā pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun is cleaning herself with water, she may insert two finger joints at the most. If she goes further than that, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: rinsing the vagina is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Cleaning:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: rinses.

english.text: She may insert two finger joints at the most:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she may insert two joints of two fingers at the most.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she goes further than that:

segments.pali: samphassaṁ sādiyantī antamaso kesaggamattampi atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she consents to the contact and goes further even by a hair’s breadth, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena ārohanto nāma mahāmatto bhikkhūsu pabbajito hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a government official called Ārohanta became a monk

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and his ex-wife a nun.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena so bhikkhu tassā bhikkhuniyā santike bhattavissaggaṁ karoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On one occasion that monk was having his meal in the presence of that nun.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī tassa bhikkhuno bhuñjantassa pānīyena ca vidhūpanena ca upatiṭṭhitvā accāvadati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: While he was eating, she attended on him with drinking water and a fan, and she flirted with him.

segments.pali: Atha kho so bhikkhu taṁ bhikkhuniṁ apasādeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But he dismissed her, saying,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Don’t do that;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it’s not allowable.”

segments.pali: “Pubbe maṁ tvaṁ evañca evañca karosi, idāni ettakaṁ na sahasī”ti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Before you did such and such to me, but now you can’t even take this much.”

segments.pali: pānīyathālakaṁ matthake āsumbhitvā vidhūpanena pahāraṁ adāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she dropped the water vessel on his head and struck him with the fan.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun hit a monk?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī bhikkhussa pahāraṁ adāsī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her,

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī bhikkhussa pahāraṁ dassati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun hit a monk?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhussa bhuñjantassa pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭheyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If, when a monk is eating, a nun attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monk:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: fully ordained.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Is eating:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: is eating any of the five cooked foods.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any kind of drink.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A fan:

english.author: brahmali

english.text: Attends on:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stands within arm’s reach, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññā pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is fully ordained, and she perceives him as such, and she attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampanne vematikā pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññā pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is fully ordained, but she does not perceive him as such, and she attends on him with drinking water or a fan, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she attends on him from beyond arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she attends on him when he is eating fresh food, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she attends on one who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is not fully ordained, but she perceives him as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive him as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives something;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has someone else give something;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asks someone who is not fully ordained to do it;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 7. Asking for raw grain

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo sassakāle āmakadhaññaṁ viññāpetvā nagaraṁ atiharanti dvāraṭṭhāne—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it was the harvest season. At that time the nuns had asked for raw grain, which they then carried to town. At the town gate they were detained and told,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, give a share.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After being released,

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo upassayaṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they went to the nunnery and told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns ask for raw grain?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them,

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo āmakadhaññaṁ viññāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī āmakadhaññaṁ viññatvā vā viññāpetvā vā bhajjitvā vā bhajjāpetvā vā koṭṭetvā vā koṭṭāpetvā vā pacitvā vā pacāpetvā vā bhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun asks for or has someone else ask for raw grain, or she roasts it or has it roasted, or she pounds it or has it pounded, or she cooks it or has it cooked, and she then eats it, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Raw grain:

segments.pali: sāli vīhi yavo godhumo kaṅgu varako kudrusako.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: rice, barley, wheat, millet, wild gram, kodo millet.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Asks for:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she asks herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has someone ask for:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she gets someone else to ask.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Roasts:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she roasts it herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has it roasted:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she gets someone else to roast it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Pounds:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she pounds it herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has it pounded:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she gets someone else to pound it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Cooks:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she cooks it herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has it cooked:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she gets someone else to cook it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does it because she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asks for vegetables;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on garlic

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 8. Disposing of waste over a wall

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro brāhmaṇo nibbiṭṭharājabhaṭo “taññeva bhaṭapathaṁ yācissāmī”ti sīsaṁ nahāyitvā bhikkhunupassayaṁ nissāya rājakulaṁ gacchati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a brahmin who had earned money by working for the king thought, “I’ll ask for my wages.” After washing his hair, he walked past the nunnery on his way to the king’s residence.

segments.pali: Aññatarā bhikkhunī kaṭāhe vaccaṁ katvā tirokuṭṭe chaḍḍentī tassa brāhmaṇassa matthake āsumbhi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then, after defecating in a pot, a nun disposed of the feces over a wall, and it landed on the head of that brahmin.

segments.pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He complained and criticized the nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “They’re not monastics, these shaven-headed sluts!

english.author: brahmali
english.text: How can they dump shit on my head?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I’m gonna burn down the nunnery!”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And he got hold of a firebrand and entered the nunnery.

segments.pali: Aññataro upāsako upassayā nikkhamanto addasa taṁ brāhmaṇaṁ ummukaṁ gahetvā upassayaṁ pavisantaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then a lay follower who was coming out from the nunnery saw that brahmin with a firebrand on his way in,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and he said to him,

segments.pali: “kissa tvaṁ, bho, ummukaṁ gahetvā upassayaṁ pavisasī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sir, why are you entering the nunnery with a firebrand?”

segments.pali: “Imā maṁ, bho, muṇḍā bandhakiniyo gūthakaṭāhaṁ matthake āsumbhiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “These shaven-headed sluts dumped shit on my head.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I’m gonna burn down the nunnery!”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “But this is auspicious, brahmin!

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You’ll get your wages and a thousand coins in addition.”

segments.pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo sīsaṁ nahāyitvā rājakulaṁ gantvā sahassaṁ alattha tañca bhaṭapathaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That brahmin then washed his hair, went to the king’s residence, and he got his wages and a thousand coins in addition.

segments.pali: Atha kho so upāsako upassayaṁ pavisitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocetvā paribhāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But that lay follower returned to the nunnery, told them what had happened, and then scolded them.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns dispose of feces over a wall?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo uccāraṁ tirokuṭṭe chaḍḍentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them,

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo uccāraṁ tirokuṭṭe chaḍḍessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns dispose of feces over a wall?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā saṅkāraṁ vā vighāsaṁ vā tirokuṭṭe vā tiropākāre vā chaḍḍeyya vā chaḍḍāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun disposes of feces or urine or trash or food scraps over a wall or over an encircling wall, or she has it disposed of in this way, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Feces:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: excrement is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Urine:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: pee is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Trash:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: refuse is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Food scraps:

segments.pali: calakāni vā aṭṭhikāni vā ucchiṭṭhodakaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: food remnants or bones or wastewater.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A wall:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are three kinds of walls: walls made of bricks, walls made of stone, walls made of wood.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An encircling wall:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are three kinds of encircling walls: encircling walls made of bricks, encircling walls made of stone, encircling walls made of wood.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Over a wall:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: to the other side of the wall.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Over an encircling wall:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: to the other side of the encircling wall.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Disposes of:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she disposes of it herself, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has it disposed of:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in asking another, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Sakiṁ āṇattā bahukampi chaḍḍeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she only asks once, then even if the other disposes of such things many times, she commits one offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she disposes of it after having looked;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she disposes of it at a place where no-one passes by;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 9. Disposing of waste on plants

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa brāhmaṇassa bhikkhunupassayaṁ nissāya yavakhettaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a brahmin had a barley field next to the nunnery.

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo uccārampi passāvampi saṅkārampi vighāsampi khette chaḍḍenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns disposed of feces, urine, trash, and food scraps in that field.

segments.pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The brahmin complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns spoil my barley field?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa brāhmaṇassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of that brahmin,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo uccārampi passāvampi saṅkārampi vighāsampi harite chaḍḍessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns dispose of feces, urine, trash, and food scraps on cultivated plants?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo uccārampi passāvampi saṅkārampi vighāsampi harite chaḍḍentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them,

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo uccārampi passāvampi saṅkārampi vighāsampi harite chaḍḍessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā saṅkāraṁ vā vighāsaṁ vā harite chaḍḍeyya vā chaḍḍāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun disposes of feces or urine or trash or food scraps on cultivated plants, or she has it disposed of in this way, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Feces:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: excrement is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Urine:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: pee is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Trash:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: refuse is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Food scraps:

segments.pali: calakāni vā aṭṭhikāni vā ucchiṭṭhodakaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: food remnants or bones or wastewater.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Cultivated plants:

segments.pali: pubbaṇṇaṁ aparaṇṇaṁ yaṁ manussānaṁ upabhogaparibhogaṁ ropimaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: grain, vegetables, whatever cultivated plants people consider valuable or useful.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Disposes of:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she disposes of it herself, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has it disposed of:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in asking another, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Sakiṁ āṇattā bahukampi chaḍḍeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she only asks once, then even if the other disposes of such things many times, she commits one offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Harite haritasaññā chaḍḍeti vā chaḍḍāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they are cultivated plants, and she perceives them as such, and she disposes of it or has it disposed of, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Harite vematikā chaḍḍeti vā chaḍḍāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they are cultivated plants, but she is unsure of it, and she disposes of it or has it disposed of, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Harite aharitasaññā chaḍḍeti vā chaḍḍāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they are cultivated plants, but she perceives them as uncultivated, and she disposes of it or has it disposed of, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they are uncultivated plants, but she perceives them as cultivated, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they are uncultivated plants, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they are uncultivated plants, and she perceives them as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she disposes of it after having looked;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she disposes of it at the edge of the field;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she disposes of it after asking and getting permission from the owners;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on garlic

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 10. Watching entertainment

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena rājagahe giraggasamajjo hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time in Rājagaha there was a hilltop fair,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns from the group of six went to see it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo naccampi gītampi vāditampi dassanāya gacchissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns go to see dancing, singing, and music? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo naccampi gītampi vāditampi dassanāya gacchissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns from the group of six go to see dancing, singing, and music?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo naccampi gītampi vāditampi dassanāya gacchantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns did that?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them,

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo naccampi gītampi vāditampi dassanāya gacchissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī naccaṁ vā gītaṁ vā vāditaṁ vā dassanāya gaccheyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun goes to see dancing or singing or music, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Dancing:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any kind of dancing.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Singing:

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Music:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any kind of music.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is on her way to see it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Yattha ṭhitā passati vā suṇāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Wherever she stands to see it or hear it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati vā suṇāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Every time she goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it or hears it again, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is on her way to see any one of the three, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Yattha ṭhitā passati vā suṇāti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali

english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: ārāme ṭhitā passati vā suṇāti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sees it or hears it while remaining in the monastery;

segments.pali: bhikkhuniyā ṭhitokāsaṁ vā nisinnokāsaṁ vā nipannokāsaṁ vā āgantvā naccanti vā gāyanti vā vādenti vā,

english.text: if the dancing, singing, or music comes to the place where the nun is standing, sitting, or lying down;

segments.pali: paṭipathaṁ gacchantī passati vā suṇāti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sees it or hears it while walking in the opposite direction;

segments.pali: sati karaṇīye gantvā passati vā suṇāti vā,

english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī rathikāyapi byūhepi siṅghāṭakepi purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhatipi sallapatipi nikaṇṇikampi jappeti dutiyikampi bhikkhuniṁ uyyojeti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā stood and talked alone with men on streets, in cul-de-sacs, and at intersections, and she whispered in their ears and dismissed her companion nun.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā rathikāyapi byūhepi siṅghāṭakepi purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhissatipi sallapissatipi nikaṇṇikampi jappissati dutiyikampi bhikkhuniṁ uyyojessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can Venerable Thullanandā do such things?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī rathikāyapi byūhepi siṅghāṭakepi purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhatipi sallapatipi nikaṇṇikampi jappeti dutiyikampi bhikkhuniṁ uyyojetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā does this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī rathikāyapi byūhepi siṅghāṭakepi purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhissatipi sallapissatipi nikaṇṇikampi jappissati dutiyikampi bhikkhuniṁ uyyojessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nun Thullanandā do such things?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī rathikāya vā byūhe vā siṅghāṭake vā purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭheyya vā sallapeyya vā nikaṇṇikaṁ vā jappeyya dutiyikaṁ vā bhikkhuniṁ uyyojeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun stands or talks alone with a man on a street or in a cul-de-sac or at an intersection, or she whispers in his ear or dismisses her companion nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A street:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a carriage road is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A cul-de-sac:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one departs the same way one enters.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An intersection:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a crossroads is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A man:

segments.pali: manussapuriso na yakkho na peto na tiracchānagato viññū paṭibalo santiṭṭhituṁ sallapituṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of standing together and talking.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: With:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: together.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Alone:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: just the man and the nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Stands with:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stands within arm’s reach of the man, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Talks with:

segments.pali: purisassa hatthapāse ṭhitā sallapati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stands talking within arm’s reach of the man, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Whispers in his ear:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she speaks into the ear of a man, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Dismisses her companion nun:

segments.pali: anācāraṁ ācaritukāmā dutiyikampi bhikkhuniṁ uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, wanting to misbehave, she dismisses her companion nun, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vā savanūpacāraṁ vā vijahantiyā āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the companion nun is in the process of going beyond the range of sight or the range of hearing, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the companion nun has gone beyond, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Hatthapāsaṁ vijahitvā santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stands or talks outside of arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Yakkhena vā petena vā paṇḍakena vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahena vā saddhiṁ santiṭṭhati vā sallapati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stands or talks with a male spirit, a male ghost, a paṇḍaka, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has a companion who understands;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is not seeking privacy;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stands or talks thinking of something else;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not want to misbehave;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she dismisses her companion nun when there is something to be done;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on the dark of the night

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 15. Departing without taking leave

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī aññatarassa kulassa kulūpikā hoti niccabhattikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a certain nun was associating with a family from which she received a regular meal.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after robing up one morning, she took her bowl and robe and went to that family where she sat down on a seat. She then departed without informing the owners.

segments.pali: Tassa kulassa dāsī gharaṁ sammajjantī taṁ āsanaṁ bhājanantarikāya pakkhipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A slave-woman who was sweeping the house put that seat in between some vessels.

segments.pali: Manussā taṁ āsanaṁ apassantā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Not seeing the seat, soon afterwards the people there asked that nun,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, where’s that seat?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I don’t know.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Give back the seat, venerable.” And after scolding her, they made an end of her regular meal.

segments.pali: Atha kho te manussā gharaṁ sodhentā taṁ āsanaṁ bhājanantarikāya passitvā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ khamāpetvā niccabhattaṁ paṭṭhapesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, while those people were cleaning the house, they saw that seat in between those vessels. They asked that nun for forgiveness and restored her regular meal.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun then told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkamissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun visit a family before the meal, sit down on a seat, and then depart without informing the owners?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkamatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave bhikkhunī purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkamissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun act like this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkameyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun visits families before the meal, sits down on a seat, and then departs without informing the owners, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Before the meal:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: from dawn until midday.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A family:

segments.pali: cattāri kulāni— khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ, suddakulaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Visits:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: goes there.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A seat:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a place for sitting cross-legged is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sits down:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: sits down on that seat.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Departs without informing the owners:

segments.pali: yo tasmiṁ kule manusso viññū taṁ anāpucchā anovassakaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, without informing a person who understands in that family, she goes beyond the roof cover of that house, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is out in the open and she goes beyond the vicinity of the seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not informed, and she does not perceive that she has, and she departs, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not informed, but she is unsure of it, and she departs, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not informed, but she perceives that she has, and she departs, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not a place for sitting cross-legged, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has informed, but she does not perceive that she has, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has informed, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has informed, and she perceives that she has, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she departs after informing someone;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if the seat is not movable;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 16. Sitting down without permission

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdatipi abhinipajjatipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā visited families after the meal, and she sat down and lay down on the seats without asking permission of the owners.

segments.pali: Manussā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ hirīyamānā āsane neva abhinisīdanti na abhinipajjanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because of Thullanandā, the people there had qualms,  neither sitting nor lying down.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They then complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdissatipi abhinipajjissatipī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā visit families after the meal, and then sit down and lie down on the seats without asking the owners for permission?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdissatipi abhinipajjissatipī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā act in this way?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdatipi abhinipajjatipī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdissatipi abhinipajjissatipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā act in this way?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī pacchābhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā āsane abhinisīdeyya vā abhinipajjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun visits families after the meal, and then sits down or lies down on a seat without asking permission of the owners, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the meal:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: when the middle of the day has passed, until sunset.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A family:

segments.pali: cattāri kulāni— khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ, suddakulaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Visits:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: goes there.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without asking permission of the owners:

segments.pali: yo tasmiṁ kule manusso sāmiko dātuṁ, taṁ anāpucchā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: not having asked permission of a person in that family who is an owner and who has the authority to give.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A seat:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a place for sitting cross-legged is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sits down:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sits down on that seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lies down:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she lies down on that seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite anāpucchitasaññā āsane abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, and she does not perceive that she has, and she sits down or lies down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite vematikā āsane abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, but she is unsure of it, and she sits down or lies down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite āpucchitasaññā āsane abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, but she perceives that she has, and she sits down or lies down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not a place for sitting cross-legged, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, but she does not perceive that she has, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, and she perceives that she has, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: āpucchā āsane abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sits down or lies down after asking permission;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if a dedicated seat is permanently ready for her;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 17. Putting out bedding without permission

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchantiyo sāyaṁ aññataraṁ gāmaṁ upagantvā aññataraṁ brāhmaṇakulaṁ upasaṅkamitvā okāsaṁ yāciṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a number of nuns were traveling through the Kosalan country on their way to Sāvatthī, when one evening they arrived at a certain village. There they went to a brahmin family and asked for a place to stay.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā brāhmaṇī tā bhikkhuniyo etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The brahmin woman told them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please wait, venerables, until my husband returns.”

segments.pali: “yāva brāhmaṇo āgacchatī”ti seyyaṁ santharitvā ekaccā nisīdiṁsu ekaccā nipajjiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: While they were waiting, the nuns put out bedding, and some sat down on it while others lay down.

segments.pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo rattiṁ āgantvā taṁ brāhmaṇiṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the husband returned at night, he said to his wife,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Who are they?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “They are nuns.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Throw out these shaven-headed sluts!”, and he had them thrown out of the house.

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those nuns then went to Sāvatthī, where they told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo vikāle kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā abhinisīdissantipi abhinipajjissantipī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns visit families at the wrong time, put out bedding without asking permission of the owners, and then sit down and lie down on it?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vikāle kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā abhinisīdantipi abhinipajjantipī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vikāle kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā abhinisīdissantipi abhinipajjissantipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns act like this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī vikāle kulāni upasaṅkamitvā sāmike anāpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdeyya vā abhinipajjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun visits families at the wrong time, puts out bedding without asking permission of the owners, or has it put out, and then sits down or lies down on it, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The wrong time:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: from sunset until dawn.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A family:

segments.pali: cattāri kulāni— khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ suddakulaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Visits:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: goes there.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without asking permission of the owners:

segments.pali: yo tasmiṁ kule manusso sāmiko dātuṁ, taṁ anāpucchā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: not having asked permission of a person in that family who is an owner and who has the authority to give.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Bedding:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: even a spread of leaves.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Puts out:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she puts it out herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has it put out:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she has someone else put it out.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sits down:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sits down on it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lies down:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she lies down on it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite anāpucchitasaññā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, and she does not perceive that she has, and she sits down or lies down on bedding after putting it out or having it put out, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite vematikā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, but she is unsure of it, and she sits down or lies down on bedding after putting it out or having it put out, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite āpucchitasaññā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, but she perceives that she has, and she sits down or lies down on bedding after putting it out or having it put out, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, but she does not perceive that she has, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, and she perceives that she has, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: āpucchā seyyaṁ santharitvā vā santharāpetvā vā abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she first asks permission, and then, after putting out bedding or having it put out, sits down or lies down on it;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 18. Complaining without reflection

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddāya kāpilāniyā antevāsinī bhikkhunī bhaddaṁ kāpilāniṁ sakkaccaṁ upaṭṭheti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a nun who was a pupil of Bhaddā Kāpilānī attended on her with care.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Bhaddā Kāpilānī said to the nuns,

segments.pali: “ayaṁ maṁ, ayye, bhikkhunī sakkaccaṁ upaṭṭheti, imissāhaṁ cīvaraṁ dassāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, this nun is attending on me with care. I’ll give her a robe.”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, because of a misunderstanding and a lack of proper reflection, that nun complained about Bhaddā Kāpilānī,

segments.pali: “ahaṁ kirāyye, ayyaṁ na sakkaccaṁ upaṭṭhemi, na kira me ayyā cīvaraṁ dassatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, if I didn’t attend on her with care, she wouldn’t give me a robe.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun complain about someone else because of a misunderstanding and a lack of proper reflection?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun act in this way?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī duggahitena dūpadhāritena paraṁ ujjhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun complains about someone else because of a misunderstanding and a lack of proper reflection, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because of a misunderstanding:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: because of wrong understanding.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because of a lack of proper reflection:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: because of wrong reflection.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Someone else:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she complains about one who is fully ordained, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives them as such, and she complains about them, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she complains about them, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive them as such, and she complains about them, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she complains about someone who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives them as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive them as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
