english.author: brahmali
english.text: 19. Cursing

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo attano bhaṇḍakaṁ apassantiyo caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: some nuns were unable to find their possessions. They said to the nun Caṇḍakāḷī,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, have you seen our things?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Caṇḍakāḷī complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “ahameva nūna corī, ahameva nūna alajjinī, yā ayyāyo attano bhaṇḍakaṁ apassantiyo tā maṁ evamāhaṁsu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Why are you asking me if I’ve seen your things? Am I a thief? Am I shameless?

segments.pali: Sacāhaṁ, ayye, tumhākaṁ bhaṇḍakaṁ gaṇhāmi, assamaṇī homi, brahmacariyā cavāmi, nirayaṁ upapajjāmi;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, if I took your things I wouldn’t be a monastic anymore. I would fall from the spiritual life and be reborn in hell.

segments.pali: yā pana maṁ abhūtena evamāha sāpi assamaṇī hotu, brahmacariyā cavatu, nirayaṁ upapajjatū”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: May anyone who speaks such an untruth about me depart from monasticism, fall from the spiritual life, and be reborn in hell!”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā caṇḍakāḷī attānampi parampi nirayenapi brahmacariyenapi abhisapissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Caṇḍakāḷī curse herself and others, referring to hell and the spiritual life?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī attānampi parampi nirayenapi brahmacariyenapi abhisapatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī attānampi parampi nirayenapi brahmacariyenapi abhisapissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Caṇḍakāḷī act like this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī attānaṁ vā paraṁ vā nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun curses herself or someone else, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Herself:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: oneself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Someone else:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who is fully ordained.

segments.pali: Nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she curses, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives them as such, and she curses them, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya vematikā nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, yet she curses them, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā nirayena vā brahmacariyena vā abhisapati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive them as such, yet she curses them, referring to hell or the spiritual life, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tiracchānayoniyā vā pettivisayena vā manussadobhaggena vā abhisapati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she curses, referring to the animal realm, the ghost realm, or human misfortune, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she curses someone who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives them as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive them as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at something beneficial;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at giving a teaching;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at giving an instruction;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 20. Crying and beating oneself

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ bhaṇḍitvā attānaṁ vadhitvā vadhitvā rodati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Caṇḍakāḷī quarreled with the nuns and then cried after repeatedly beating herself.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā caṇḍakāḷī attānaṁ vadhitvā vadhitvā rodissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Caṇḍakāḷī cry after repeatedly beating herself?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī attānaṁ vadhitvā vadhitvā rodatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī attānaṁ vadhitvā vadhitvā rodissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Caṇḍakāḷī act like this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī attānaṁ vadhitvā vadhitvā rodeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun cries after repeatedly beating herself, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Herself:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: oneself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she cries after repeatedly beating herself, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she beats herself, but does not cry, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she cries, but does not beat herself, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: ñātibyasanena vā bhogabyasanena vā rogabyasanena vā phuṭṭhā rodati na vadhati,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she cries, but does not beat herself, because of loss of relatives, loss of property, or loss of health;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The tenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second subchapter on the dark of the night is finished.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 21. Bathing naked

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo aciravatiyā nadiyā vesiyāhi saddhiṁ naggā ekatitthe nahāyanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a number of nuns were bathing naked at a ford in the river Aciravatī together with sex workers.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sex workers teased them,

segments.pali: “kiṁ nu kho nāma tumhākaṁ, ayye, daharānaṁ brahmacariyaṁ ciṇṇena, nanu nāma kāmā paribhuñjitabbā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, why practice the spiritual life when you’re still young? Why not enjoy worldly pleasures?

segments.pali: Yadā jiṇṇā bhavissatha tadā brahmacariyaṁ carissatha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You can practice the spiritual life when you’re old.

segments.pali: Evaṁ tumhākaṁ ubho atthā pariggahitā bhavissantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way you’ll get the benefits of both.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns felt humiliated.

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo upassayaṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They then went to the nunnery and told the nuns what had happened.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns told the monks,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who in turn told the Buddha.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the nuns for the following ten reasons:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: for the well-being of the Sangha …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: for supporting the training.

segments.pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun bathes naked, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Bathes naked:

segments.pali: anivatthā vā apārutā vā nahāyati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she bathes without wearing a sarong or an upper robe, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At the end of the bath, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if her robes have been stolen or lost;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 22. Making an oversize bathing robe

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ udakasāṭikā anuññātā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the Buddha had allowed bathing robes for the nuns.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Knowing this, the nuns from the group of six wore bathing robes of inappropriate size.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: As they were walking about, they were dragging them along, both in front and behind.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo appamāṇikāyo udakasāṭikāyo dhāressantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six wear such bathing robes?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo appamāṇikāyo udakasāṭikāyo dhārentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nuns from the group of six do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo appamāṇikāyo udakasāṭikāyo dhāressanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six wear such bathing robes?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Udakasāṭikaṁ pana bhikkhuniyā kārayamānāya pamāṇikā kāretabbā. Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ—dīghaso catasso vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ dve vidatthiyo. Taṁ atikkāmentiyā chedanakaṁ pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun is having a bathing robe made, it should be made the right size. This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide. If it exceeds that, it is to be cut down, and she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A bathing robe:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: wearing it as a sarong, she bathes.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Is having made:

segments.pali: karontiyā vā kārāpentiyā vā. Pamāṇikā kāretabbā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: making it herself or having someone else make it, it should be made the right size.

segments.pali: Tatridaṁ pamāṇaṁ— dīghaso catasso vidatthiyo, sugatavidatthiyā; tiriyaṁ dve vidatthiyo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the right size: four standard handspans long and two wide.

segments.pali: Taṁ atikkāmetvā karoti vā kārāpeti vā, payoge dukkaṭaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she makes one or has one made that exceeds that, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she gets it, it is to be cut down, and she is then to confess an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Attanā vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she finishes what she began herself, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Attanā vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has others finish what she began herself, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Parehi vippakataṁ attanā pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she finishes herself what was begun by others, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Parehi vippakataṁ parehi pariyosāpeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has others finish what was begun by others, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Aññassatthāya karoti vā kārāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she makes one or has one made for the benefit of someone else, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Aññena kataṁ paṭilabhitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she gets one that was made by someone else and then uses it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she makes it the right size;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she makes it smaller than the right size;

segments.pali: aññena kataṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ paṭilabhitvā chinditvā paribhuñjati,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets one made by someone else that exceeds the right size and she cuts it down before using it;

segments.pali: vitānaṁ vā bhūmattharaṇaṁ vā sāṇipākāraṁ vā bhisiṁ vā bibbohanaṁ vā karoti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she makes a canopy, a floor cover, a cloth screen, a mattress, or a pillow;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 23. Unstitching a robe

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarissā bhikkhuniyā mahagghe cīvaradusse cīvaraṁ dukkaṭaṁ hoti dussibbitaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a robe belonging to a certain nun had been badly made and badly sewn from expensive robe-cloth.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun Thullanandā said to her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, this robe-cloth is beautiful,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: but the robe has been badly made and badly sewn.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If I unstitch it, will you sew it back together?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sure.”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ visibbetvā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā adāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then that nun unstitched the robe and gave it to Thullanandā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Yet although Thullanandā

segments.pali: “sibbissāmi sibbissāmī”ti neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: repeatedly said she would sew it, she neither sewed it herself nor did she make any effort to have someone else do it.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun then told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized Thullanandā,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ visibbāpetvā neva sibbissati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā have a nun’s robe unstitched, and then neither sew it herself nor make any effort to have someone else do it?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ visibbāpetvā neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karotī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ visibbāpetvā neva sibbissati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā act like this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbeyya na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ kareyya, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun, after unstitching a nun’s robe or having it unstitched, neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A nun’s:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: another nun’s.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Robe:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one of the six kinds of robes.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Unstitching:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she unstitches it herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Having it unstitched:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she has someone else unstitch it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And there were no obstacles:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: when there is no obstacle.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She neither sews:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not sew it herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not ask anyone else.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Except if it was no more than four or five days:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: unless it was no more than four or five days.

segments.pali: “Neva sibbissāmi na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I’ll neither sew it nor make any effort to have someone else sew it,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā cīvaraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she unstitches her robe or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya vematikā cīvaraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she unstitches her robe or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā cīvaraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she unstitches her robe or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she unstitches another requisite or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Anupasampannāya cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ visibbetvā vā visibbāpetvā vā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva sibbati na sibbāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, aññatra catūhapañcāhā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she unstitches her robe or another requisite, or has it unstitched, and then neither sews it herself nor makes any effort to have someone else sew it, and there were no obstacles, then, except if it was no more than four or five days, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches for someone to sew, but does not find anyone;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is doing it, but she takes longer than four or five days;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 24. Moving robes

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns stored one of their robes with other nuns and then left to wander the country in a sarong and an upper robe.

segments.pali: Tāni cīvarāni ciraṁ nikkhittāni kaṇṇakitāni honti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because they were stored for a long time, the robes became moldy.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the nuns put them out to sun them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: other nuns asked them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Whose moldy robes are these?”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they told them what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns store one of their robes with other nuns and then go wandering the country in a sarong and an upper robe?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīnaṁ hatthe cīvaraṁ nikkhipitvā santaruttarena janapadacārikaṁ pakkamissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī pañcāhikaṁ saṅghāṭicāraṁ atikkāmeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun does not move her robes for more than five days, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Does not move her robes for more than five days:

segments.pali: pañcamaṁ divasaṁ pañca cīvarāni neva nivāseti na pārupati na otāpeti, pañcamaṁ divasaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not wear or sun her five robes on the fifth day, then, when the fifth day has passed, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is more than five days, and she perceives it as more, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is more than five days, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is more than five days, but she perceives it as less, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is less than five days, but she perceives it as more, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is less than five days, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is less than five days and she perceives it as less, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: pañcamaṁ divasaṁ pañca cīvarāni nivāseti vā pārupati vā otāpeti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she wears or suns the five robes on the fifth day;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.text: The subchapter on nakedness

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 25. Borrowing a robe

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī piṇḍāya caritvā allacīvaraṁ pattharitvā vihāraṁ pāvisi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there was a nun who, after walking for almsfood, spread out her damp robe and entered her dwelling.

segments.pali: Aññatarā bhikkhunī taṁ cīvaraṁ pārupitvā gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Another nun put on that robe and went to the village for alms.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the first nun came out and asked the nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, have you seen my robe?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns told her what had happened.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She then complained and criticized the other nun,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī mayhaṁ cīvaraṁ anāpucchā pārupissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun put on my robe without asking permission?”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She told the nuns what had happened,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ anāpucchā pārupissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun put on another nun’s robe without asking permission?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ anāpucchā pārupatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ anāpucchā pārupissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun wears a robe taken on loan, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A robe taken on loan:

segments.pali: upasampannāya pañcannaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ tassā vā adinnaṁ taṁ vā anāpucchā nivāseti vā pārupati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she wears any of the five robes belonging to a fully ordained nun, and it has not been given to her, nor has she asked permission to use it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she takes on loan a robe belonging to her and then wears it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya vematikā cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she takes on loan a robe belonging to her and then wears it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā cīvarasaṅkamanīyaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she takes on loan a robe belonging to her and then wears it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she takes on loan a robe belonging to her and then wears it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if the robe has been given to her;

segments.pali: taṁ vā āpucchā nivāseti vā pārupati vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she wears it after asking permission;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if her own robe has been stolen;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if her own robe has been lost;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on nakedness

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 26. Hindering robe-cloth for the community

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā upaṭṭhākakulaṁ thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a family that was supporting the nun Thullanandā said to her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, we’re going to give robe-cloth to the Sangha of nuns.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She replied,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “You’re very busy,” and she created an obstacle for them.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena tassa kulassa gharaṁ ḍayhati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the house of that family burned down.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They then complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā amhākaṁ deyyadhammaṁ antarāyaṁ karissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā create an obstacle for our gift?

segments.pali: Ubhayenāmha paribāhirā bhogehi ca puññena cā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Now we have neither possessions nor merit.”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā gaṇassa cīvaralābhaṁ antarāyaṁ karissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā create an obstacle for the community to get robe-cloth?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī gaṇassa cīvaralābhaṁ antarāyaṁ akāsī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī gaṇassa cīvaralābhaṁ antarāyaṁ karissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī gaṇassa cīvaralābhaṁ antarāyaṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun creates an obstacle for the community to get robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The community:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the Sangha of nuns is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Robe-cloth:

segments.pali: channaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ vikappanupagaṁ pacchimaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one of the six kinds of robe-cloth, but not smaller than what can be assigned to another.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Creates an obstacle:

segments.pali: “kathaṁ ime cīvaraṁ na dadeyyun”ti antarāyaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she creates an obstacle with the thought, “What can be done so that these people don’t give robe-cloth?”, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Aññaṁ parikkhāraṁ antarāyaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she creates an obstacle for another requisite, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Sambahulānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ vā ekabhikkhuniyā vā anupasampannāya vā cīvaraṁ vā aññaṁ vā parikkhāraṁ antarāyaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she creates an obstacle for a number of nuns or for a single nun or for someone who is not fully ordained to get robe-cloth or another requisite, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she can show a benefit in obstructing them;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on nakedness

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 27. Blocking robe-cloth distribution

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhunisaṅghassa akālacīvaraṁ uppannaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time out-of-season robe-cloth had been given to the Sangha of nuns,

segments.pali: Atha kho bhikkhunisaṅgho taṁ cīvaraṁ bhājetukāmo sannipati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the Sangha gathered to share it out.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā antevāsiniyo bhikkhuniyo pakkantā honti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then the nuns who were pupils of the nun Thullanandā were away.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā said to the nuns:

segments.pali: “ayye, bhikkhuniyo pakkantā, na tāva cīvaraṁ bhājīyissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, there are nuns who are away. We cannot distribute the robe-cloth until they get back.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she blocked the distribution of robe-cloth.

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo na tāva cīvaraṁ bhājīyissatīti pakkamiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Since the robe-cloth could not be distributed until those nuns returned, other nuns left.

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī antevāsinīsu bhikkhunīsu āgatāsu taṁ cīvaraṁ bhājāpesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, when her pupils returned, Thullanandā had that robe-cloth distributed.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā dhammikaṁ cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāhissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā block a legitimate distribution of robe-cloth?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāhatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāhissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ cīvaravibhaṅgaṁ paṭibāheyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun blocks a legitimate distribution of robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A legitimate distribution of robe-cloth:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it is distributed by a unanimous Sangha of nuns.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Blocks:

segments.pali: kathaṁ imaṁ cīvaraṁ na bhājeyyāti paṭibāhati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she blocks it with the thought, “What can be done so that this robe-cloth isn’t distributed?”, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she blocks it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she blocks it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she blocks it, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she blocks it after demonstrating a benefit;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession
