english.text: The subchapter on nakedness

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 28. Giving a robe to a non-monastic

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī naṭānampi naṭakānampi laṅghakānampi sokajjhāyikānampi kumbhathūṇikānampi samaṇacīvaraṁ deti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā was giving monastic robes to actors, dancers, acrobats, magicians, and musicians, saying,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please praise me in public.”

segments.pali: Naṭāpi naṭakāpi laṅghakāpi sokajjhāyikāpi kumbhathūṇikāpi thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā parisati vaṇṇaṁ bhāsanti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they did:

segments.pali: “ayyā thullanandā bahussutā bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable Thullanandā is a learned reciter; she’s confident and skilled at giving teachings.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Give to her; work for her!”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā … tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā agārikassa samaṇacīvaraṁ dassatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can Venerable Thullanandā give monastic robes to householders?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī agārikassa samaṇacīvaraṁ detī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā does this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī agārikassa samaṇacīvaraṁ dassati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī agārikassa vā paribbājakassa vā paribbājikāya vā samaṇacīvaraṁ dadeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives a monastic robe to a householder or a male wanderer or a female wanderer, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A householder:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: anyone who lives at home.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A male wanderer:

segments.pali: bhikkhuñca sāmaṇerañca ṭhapetvā yo koci paribbājakasamāpanno.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any male wanderer apart from Buddhist monks and novice monks.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A female wanderer:

segments.pali: bhikkhuniñca sikkhamānañca sāmaṇeriñca ṭhapetvā yā kāci paribbājikasamāpannā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any female wanderer apart from Buddhist nuns, trainee nuns, and novice nuns.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monastic robe:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a mark has been made is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she gives it away, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives one to her mother or father;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she lends one out;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on nakedness

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 29. Letting the robe season expire

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā upaṭṭhākakulaṁ thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a family that was supporting the nun Thullanandā said to her,

segments.pali: “sace mayaṁ, ayye, sakkoma, bhikkhunisaṅghassa cīvaraṁ dassāmā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If we’re able, venerable, we’ll give robe-cloth to the Sangha of nuns.”

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo cīvaraṁ bhājetukāmā sannipatiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then the nuns who had completed the rainy-season residence had gathered to distribute robe-cloth.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But Thullanandā said to them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please wait, venerables. The Sangha is expecting more cloth.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns said to her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Go and find out what’s happening.”

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te manusse etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She then went to that family and said to them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please give the robe-cloth to the Sangha of nuns.”

segments.pali: “Na mayaṁ, ayye, sakkoma bhikkhunisaṅghassa cīvaraṁ dātun”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “We’re sorry, but we’re not able to give robe-cloth to the Sangha.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā dubbalacīvarapaccāsāya cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā allow the robe season to expire because of an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dubbalacīvarapaccāsāya cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dubbalacīvarapaccāsāya cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dubbalacīvarapaccāsāya cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun lets the robe season expire because of an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An uncertain expectation of robe-cloth:

segments.pali: “sace mayaṁ sakkoma, dassāma karissāmā”ti vācā bhinnā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they have said, “If we’re able, then we’ll give, then we’ll act.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Robe season:

segments.pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimo māso, atthate kathine pañca māsā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is the last month of the rainy season; for one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, it is the five month period.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lets the robe season expire:

segments.pali: anatthate kathine vassānassa pacchimaṁ divasaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: for one who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony, if she goes beyond the last day of the rainy season, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Atthate kathine kathinuddhāradivasaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For one who has participated in the robe-making ceremony, if she goes beyond the day on which the robe season ends, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dubbalacīvare dubbalacīvarasaññā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, and she perceives it as such, and she lets the robe season expire, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dubbalacīvare vematikā cīvarakālasamayaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, but she is unsure of it, and she lets the robe season expire, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, but she does not perceive it as such, and she lets the robe season expire, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, but she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is not an uncertain expectation of robe-cloth, and she does not perceive it as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she can show a benefit in hindering it;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on nakedness

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 30. Blocking the ending of the robe season

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarena upāsakena saṅghaṁ uddissa vihāro kārāpito hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a lay follower had built a dwelling for the Sangha.

segments.pali: So tassa vihārassa mahe ubhatosaṅghassa akālacīvaraṁ dātukāmo hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He wanted to give out-of-season robe-cloth to both Sanghas at the presentation ceremony for that dwelling,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena ubhatosaṅghassa kathinaṁ atthataṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: but both Sanghas had already performed the robe-making ceremony.

segments.pali: Atha kho so upāsako saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā kathinuddhāraṁ yāci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That lay follower then went to the Sangha and asked it to make an end of the robe season.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the Buddha.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, I allow you to end the robe season.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And it should be done like this.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho kathinaṁ uddhareyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should end the robe season.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha ends the robe season.

segments.pali: Yassāyasmato khamati kathinassa uddhāro, so tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any monk who approves of ending the robe season should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

segments.pali: Ubbhataṁ saṅghena kathinaṁ, khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has made an end of the robe season. The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

segments.pali: Atha kho so upāsako bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā kathinuddhāraṁ yāci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That lay follower then went to the Sangha of nuns and asked them to end the robe season.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But the nun Thullanandā, aiming to get robe-cloth for herself, blocked the Sangha from doing it.

segments.pali: Atha kho so upāsako ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That lay follower complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo amhākaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ na dassantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns not end the robe season for us?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa upāsakassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of that lay follower.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā dhammikaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāhissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā block a legitimate ending of the robe season?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāhatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāhissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dhammikaṁ kathinuddhāraṁ paṭibāheyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun blocks a legitimate ending of the robe season, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A legitimate ending of the robe season:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a unanimous Sangha of nuns brings it to an end.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Blocks:

segments.pali: “kathaṁ idaṁ kathinaṁ na uddhareyyā”ti paṭibāhati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she blocks it with the thought, “What can be done so that the robe season doesn’t end?”, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she blocks it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she blocks it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she blocks it, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she can show a benefit in blocking it;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The tenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third subchapter on nakedness is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on lying down

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 31. Lying on the same bed

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo dve ekamañce tuvaṭṭenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two nuns were lying down on the same bed.

segments.pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo dve ekamañce tuvaṭṭessanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can two nuns lie down on the same bed? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve ekamañce tuvaṭṭentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve ekamañce tuvaṭṭessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhuniyo dve ekamañce tuvaṭṭeyyuṁ, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If two nuns lie down on the same bed, they commit an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nuns:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: fully ordained is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If two lie down on the same bed:

segments.pali: ekāya nipannāya aparā nipajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, when one is lying down, the other lies down, they commit an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If both lie down together, they commit an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Every time they get up and then lie down again, they commit an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, when one is lying down, the other sits down;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if both sit down together;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they are insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they are the first offenders.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on lying down

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 32. Lying on the same sheet

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two nuns were lying down on the same sheet and under the same cover.

segments.pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭessanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can two nuns lie down on the same sheet and under the same cover? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhuniyo dve ekattharaṇapāvuraṇā tuvaṭṭeyyuṁ, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If two nuns lie down on the same sheet and under the same cover, they commit an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nuns:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: fully ordained is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If two lie down on the same sheet and under the same cover:

segments.pali: taññeva attharitvā taññeva pārupanti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they spread out just the one and cover themselves with just the one, they commit an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is the same sheet and the same cover, and they perceive them as such, and they lie down, they commit an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is the same sheet and the same cover, but they are unsure of it, and they lie down, they commit an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is the same sheet and the same cover, but they perceive them as different, and they lie down, they commit an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is the same sheet but different covers, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is different sheets but the same cover, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is different sheets and different covers, but they perceive them as the same, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is different sheets and different covers, but they are unsure of it, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is different sheets and different covers, and they perceive them as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they make a partition and then lie down;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they are insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they are the first offenders.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on lying down

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 33. Making a nun ill at ease

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings.

segments.pali: Bhaddāpi kāpilānī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ uḷārasambhāvitā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Bhaddā Kāpilānī, too, was a learned reciter who was confident and skilled at giving teachings, and she was respected for her excellence.

segments.pali: “ayyā bhaddā kāpilānī bahussutā bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ uḷārasambhāvitā”ti bhaddaṁ kāpilāniṁ paṭhamaṁ payirupāsitvā, pacchā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ payirupāsanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because of this, people visited Bhaddā Kāpilānī first and then Thullanandā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Overcome by jealousy, Thullanandā thought,

segments.pali: “imā kira appicchā santuṭṭhā pavivittā asaṁsaṭṭhā yā imā saññattibahulā viññattibahulā viharantī”ti bhaddāya kāpilāniyā purato caṅkamatipi tiṭṭhatipi nisīdatipi seyyampi kappeti uddisatipi uddisāpetipi sajjhāyampi karoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “These ones, who are supposedly contented and have few desires, who are supposedly secluded and not socializing, are always persuading and convincing people.” And in front of Bhaddā Kāpilānī, she walked back and forth, stood, sat down, and lay down, and she recited and had others recite, and she rehearsed.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā ayyāya bhaddāya kāpilāniyā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā intentionally make Bhaddā Kāpilānī ill at ease?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karotī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā sañcicca aphāsuṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun intentionally makes a nun ill at ease, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: another nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Intentionally:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: knowing, perceiving, having intended, having decided, she transgresses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Makes ill at ease:

segments.pali: “iminā imissā aphāsu bhavissatī”ti anāpucchā purato caṅkamati vā tiṭṭhati vā nisīdati vā seyyaṁ vā kappeti uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā sajjhāyaṁ vā karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, without asking permission, but thinking, “In this way she will be ill at ease,” she walks back and forth in front of her, or she stands, sits down, or lies down in front of her, or she recites, has others recite, or rehearses in front of her, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she intentionally makes her ill at ease, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya vematikā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she intentionally makes her ill at ease, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā sañcicca aphāsuṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she intentionally makes her ill at ease, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anupasampannāya sañcicca aphāsuṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she intentionally makes her ill at ease, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: na aphāsuṁ kattukāmā āpucchā purato caṅkamati vā tiṭṭhati vā nisīdati vā seyyaṁ vā kappeti uddisati vā uddisāpeti vā sajjhāyaṁ vā karoti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, after asking permission and not desiring to make her ill at ease, she walks back and forth in front of her, or she stands, sits down, or lies down in front of her, or she recites, has others recite, or rehearses in front of her;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on lying down

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 34. Not nursing a disciple

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭheti na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a disciple of the nun Thullanandā was suffering, but Thullanandā neither nursed her nor made any effort to have someone else nurse her.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭhessati na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā not nurse a suffering disciple, nor make any effort to have someone else nurse her?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭheti na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karotī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā didn’t do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭhessati na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā not do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dukkhitaṁ sahajīviniṁ neva upaṭṭheyya na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun neither nurses a suffering disciple, nor makes any effort to have someone else nurse her, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suffering:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: sick is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Disciple:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a student is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Neither nurses:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: does not herself nurse her.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nor makes any effort to have someone else nurse her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not ask anyone else.

segments.pali: “Neva upaṭṭhessāmi, na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I will neither nurse her nor make any effort to have someone else nurse her,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Antevāsiniṁ vā anupasampannaṁ vā neva upaṭṭheti, na upaṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she neither nurses nor makes any effort to have someone else nurse a pupil or one who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches but does not find a nurse;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on lying down

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 35. Evicting a nun

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddā kāpilānī sākete vassaṁ upagatā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time Bhaddā Kāpilānī had entered the rainy-season residence at Sāketa.

segments.pali: Sā kenacideva karaṇīyena ubbāḷhā thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But because she was disturbed by a certain matter, she sent a message to the nun Thullanandā:

segments.pali: “sace me ayyā thullanandā upassayaṁ dadeyya āgaccheyyāmahaṁ sāvatthin”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If you would give me a dwelling place, I would come to Sāvatthī.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā replied,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please come; I’ll give you one.”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhaddā kāpilānī sāketā sāvatthiṁ agamāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Bhaddā Kāpilānī then traveled from Sāketa to Sāvatthī,

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā upassayaṁ adāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and Thullanandā gave her a dwelling place.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time Thullanandā was a learned reciter, and she was confident and skilled at giving teachings.

segments.pali: Bhaddāpi kāpilānī bahussutā hoti bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ uḷārasambhāvitā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Bhaddā Kāpilānī, too, was a learned reciter who was confident and skilled at giving teachings, and she was respected for her excellence.

segments.pali: “ayyā bhaddā kāpilānī bahussutā bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ uḷārasambhāvitā”ti bhaddaṁ kāpilāniṁ paṭhamaṁ payirupāsitvā pacchā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ payirupāsanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because of this, people visited Bhaddā Kāpilānī first and then Thullanandā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Overcome by jealousy, Thullanandā thought,

segments.pali: “imā kira appicchā santuṭṭhā pavivittā asaṁsaṭṭhā yā imā saññattibahulā viññattibahulā viharantī”ti kupitā anattamanā bhaddaṁ kāpilāniṁ upassayā nikkaḍḍhi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “These ones, who are supposedly contented and have few desires, who are supposedly secluded and not socializing, are always persuading and convincing people.” And in anger she threw Bhaddā Kāpilānī out of that dwelling place.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā ayyāya bhaddāya kāpilāniyā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā give a dwelling place to Venerable Bhaddā Kāpilānī and then throw her out in anger?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhaddāya kāpilāniyā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍheyya vā nikkaḍḍhāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives a dwelling place to a nun, and then, in anger, throws her out or has her thrown out, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: To a nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: to another nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A dwelling place:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one that has a door is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she gives it herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In anger:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: discontent, having hatred, hostile.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Throws out:

segments.pali: gabbhe gahetvā pamukhaṁ nikkaḍḍhati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she takes hold of her in a room and throws her out to the entryway, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Pamukhe gahetvā bahi nikkaḍḍhati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she takes hold of her in the entryway and throws her outside, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Ekena payogena bahukepi dvāre atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Even if she makes her go through many doors with a single effort, she commits one offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has thrown out:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asks another, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Sakiṁ āṇattā bahukepi dvāre atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she only asks once, then even if the other makes her go through many doors, she commits one offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she gives her a dwelling place, and she then throws her out in anger or has her thrown out, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya vematikā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she gives her a dwelling place, and she then throws her out in anger or has her thrown out, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā upassayaṁ datvā kupitā anattamanā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she gives her a dwelling place, and she then throws her out in anger or has her thrown out, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she throws out one of her requisites, or she has it thrown out, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Akavāṭabaddhā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she throws her out or has her thrown out from a dwelling place without a door, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she throws out one of her requisites from a dwelling place without a door, or she has it thrown out, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Anupasampannaṁ kavāṭabaddhā vā akavāṭabaddhā vā nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she throws out one who is not fully ordained, or she has her thrown out, from a dwelling place with or without a door, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she throws out one of her requisites from that place, or she has it thrown out, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she throws out, or has thrown out, one who is shameless;

segments.pali: tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she throws out, or has thrown out, a requisite belonging to that person;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she throws out, or has thrown out, one who is insane;

segments.pali: tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she throws out, or has thrown out, a requisite belonging to that person;

segments.pali: bhaṇḍanakārikaṁ kalahakārikaṁ vivādakārikaṁ bhassakārikaṁ saṅghe adhikaraṇakārikaṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she throws out, or has thrown out, one who is quarrelsome and argumentative, and who creates legal issues in the Sangha;

segments.pali: tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she throws out, or has thrown out, a requisite belonging to that person;

segments.pali: antevāsiniṁ vā saddhivihāriniṁ vā na sammā vattantiṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she throws out, or has thrown out, a pupil or student who is not conducting herself properly;

segments.pali: tassā parikkhāraṁ nikkaḍḍhati vā nikkaḍḍhāpeti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she throws out, or has thrown out, a requisite belonging to that person;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 36. Socializing with a householder

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Caṇḍakāḷī was socializing with householders and their offspring.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā caṇḍakāḷī saṁsaṭṭhā viharissati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can Venerable Caṇḍakāḷī socialize with householders and their offspring?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī does this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharissati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nun Caṇḍakāḷī do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā vihareyya gahapatinā vā gahapatiputtena vā sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun is socializing with a householder or a householder’s offspring, the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: ‘māyye, saṁsaṭṭhā vihari gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi. Viviccāyye; vivekaññeva bhaginiyā saṅgho vaṇṇetī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, don’t socialize with householders or householders’ offspring. Be secluded, venerable. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”

segments.pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ; no ce paṭinissajjeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then stops, all is well. If she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Socializing:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she socializes with improper bodily and verbal actions.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A householder:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: anyone who lives at home.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A householder’s offspring:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever is an offspring or a sibling.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun who is socializing.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns:

segments.pali: aññāhi bhikkhunīhi. Yā passanti yā suṇanti tāhi vattabbā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct her like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, don’t socialize with householders or householders’ offspring.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerable.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they should correct her a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun, even if she has to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, don’t socialize with householders or householders’ offspring.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerable.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They should correct her a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Should press her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, she should be pressed like this.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so is socializing with householders and their offspring.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she keeps on doing it.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī saṁsaṭṭhā viharati gahapatināpi gahapatiputtenapi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so is socializing with householders and their offspring.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she keeps on doing it.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

segments.pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has not been pressed;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stops;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 37. Wandering within the country

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ caranti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns went wandering without a group of travelers where it was considered risky and dangerous within their own country.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Scoundrels raped them.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns go wandering without a group of travelers where it’s considered risky and dangerous within their own country?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ carissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī antoraṭṭhe sāsaṅkasammate sappaṭibhaye asatthikā cārikaṁ careyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun goes wandering without a group of travelers where it is considered risky and dangerous within her own country, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Within her own country:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in the country where she is living.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Risky:

segments.pali: tasmiṁ magge corānaṁ niviṭṭhokāso dissati, bhuttokāso dissati, ṭhitokāso dissati, nisinnokāso dissati, nipannokāso dissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a place has been seen along that road where criminals are camping, eating, standing, sitting, or lying down.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Dangerous:

segments.pali: tasmiṁ magge corehi manussā hatā dissanti, viluttā dissanti, ākoṭitā dissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: criminals have been seen along that road, injuring, robbing, or beating people.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without a group of travelers:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in the absence of a group of travelers.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Goes wandering:

segments.pali: kukkuṭasampāte gāme gāmantare gāmantare āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: when the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Agāmake araññe addhayojane addhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she travels with a group;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she travels where it is safe and free from danger;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali

english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Outside her own country:

segments.pali: yassa vijite viharati, taṁ ṭhapetvā aññassa raṭṭhe.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in any country apart from the one where she is living.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Risky:

segments.pali: tasmiṁ magge corānaṁ niviṭṭhokāso dissati, bhuttokāso dissati, ṭhitokāso dissati, nisinnokāso dissati, nipannokāso dissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a place has been seen along that road where criminals are camping, eating, standing, sitting, or lying down.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Dangerous:

segments.pali: tasmiṁ magge corehi manussā hatā dissanti, viluttā dissanti, ākoṭitā dissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: criminals have been seen along that road, injuring, robbing, or beating people.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without a group of travelers:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in the absence of a group of travelers.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Goes wandering:

segments.pali: kukkuṭasampāte gāme gāmantare gāmantare, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: when the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Agāmake araññe addhayojane addhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she travels with a group;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she travels where it is safe and free from danger;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 39. Wandering during the rainy season

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo antovassaṁ cārikaṁ caranti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns went wandering during the rainy season.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo antovassaṁ cārikaṁ carissanti haritāni tiṇāni ca sammaddantā, ekindriyaṁ jīvaṁ viheṭhentā, bahū khuddake pāṇe saṅghātaṁ āpādentā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns go wandering during the rainy season? They are trampling down the green grass, harming one-sensed life, and destroying many small beings.”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns go wandering during the rainy season?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo antovassaṁ cārikaṁ carantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo antovassaṁ cārikaṁ carissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī antovassaṁ cārikaṁ careyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun goes wandering during the rainy season, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: During the rainy season:

segments.pali: purimaṁ vā temāsaṁ pacchimaṁ vā temāsaṁ avasitvā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: not having stayed put for the first three or the last three months of the rainy season.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Goes wandering:

segments.pali: kukkuṭasampāte gāme gāmantare gāmantare āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: when the inhabited areas are a chicken’s flight apart, then for every next inhabited area she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Agāmake araññe addhayojane addhayojane āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it is an uninhabited area, a wilderness, then for every six kilometers she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she goes on seven-day business;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she goes because something is disturbing her;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 40. Not wandering after the rainy season

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Rājagaha in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrel sanctuary.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo tattheva rājagahe vassaṁ vasanti, tattha hemantaṁ, tattha gimhaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were staying right there at Rājagaha for the rainy season, the winter, and the summer.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “āhundarikā bhikkhunīnaṁ disā andhakārā, na imāsaṁ disā pakkhāyantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “The nuns are leaving the districts in darkness and obscurity. They don’t brighten them up by their presence.”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They then told the monks,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who in turn told the Buddha.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the nuns for the following ten reasons:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: for the well-being of the Sangha …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training.

segments.pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī vassaṁvuṭṭhā cārikaṁ na pakkameyya antamaso chappañcayojanānipi, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun who has completed the rainy-season residence does not go wandering at least 65 to 80 kilometers, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has completed the rainy-season residence:

segments.pali: purimaṁ vā temāsaṁ pacchimaṁ vā temāsaṁ vuṭṭhā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has completed the first three or the last three months of the rainy-season residence.

segments.pali: “Cārikaṁ na pakkamissāmi antamaso chappañcayojanānipī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I won’t go wandering, not even 65 to 80 kilometers,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches for a companion nun, but is unable to find one;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The tenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth subchapter on lying down is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
