english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on galleries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 41. Visiting places of pleasure

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena rañño pasenadissa kosalassa uyyāne cittāgāre paṭibhānacittaṁ kataṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: artwork had been installed in the gallery in King Pasenadi of Kosala’s park.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Many people visited the gallery,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: as did the nuns from the group of six.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns visit a gallery? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns from the group of six do this?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo cittāgāraṁ dassanāya gacchissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī rājāgāraṁ vā cittāgāraṁ vā ārāmaṁ vā uyyānaṁ vā pokkharaṇiṁ vā dassanāya gaccheyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun visits a royal house or a gallery or a park or a garden or a lotus pond, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A royal house:

segments.pali: yattha katthaci rañño kīḷituṁ ramituṁ kataṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: wherever one has been built for a king to entertain and enjoy himself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A gallery:

segments.pali: yattha katthaci manussānaṁ kīḷituṁ ramituṁ kataṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: wherever one has been built for people to entertain and enjoy themselves.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A park:

segments.pali: yattha katthaci manussānaṁ kīḷituṁ ramituṁ kato hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: wherever one has been made for people to entertain and enjoy themselves.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A garden:

segments.pali: yattha katthaci manussānaṁ kīḷituṁ ramituṁ kataṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: wherever one has been made for people to entertain and enjoy themselves.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A lotus pond:

segments.pali: yattha katthaci manussānaṁ kīḷituṁ ramituṁ katā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: wherever one has been made for people to entertain and enjoy themselves.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is on her way to visit them, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Wherever she stands to see them, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Every time she goes beyond the range of sight and then sees them again, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is on her way to visit any one of them, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Wherever she stands to see it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Dassanūpacāraṁ vijahitvā punappunaṁ passati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Every time she goes beyond the range of sight and then sees it again, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sees it while remaining in a monastery;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sees it while coming or going;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she goes when there is something to be done and then sees it;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 42. Using luxurious furniture

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns were using high and luxurious couches.

segments.pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns use high and luxurious couches? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo āsandimpi pallaṅkampi paribhuñjissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī āsandiṁ vā pallaṅkaṁ vā paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun uses a high or luxurious couch, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A high couch:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one that is oversize is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A luxurious couch:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one decorated with images of predatory animals.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Uses:

segments.pali: tasmiṁ abhinisīdati vā abhinipajjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sits down or lies down on it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses a high couch after cutting off the legs;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses a luxurious couch after removing the decorations with images of predatory animals;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on galleries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 43. Spinning yarn

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo suttaṁ kantanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns from the group of six were spinning yarn.

segments.pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo suttaṁ kantissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns spin yarn? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six spin yarn?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo suttaṁ kantantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo suttaṁ kantissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun spins yarn, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Yarn:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are six kinds of yarn:

segments.pali: khomaṁ, kappāsikaṁ, koseyyaṁ, kambalaṁ, sāṇaṁ, bhaṅgaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: linen, cotton, silk, wool, sunn hemp, and hemp.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Spins:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she spins it herself, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every pull, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she spins yarn that has already been spun;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 44. Serving householders

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo gihiveyyāvaccaṁ karonti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns were providing services for householders.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns provide services for householders?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo gihiveyyāvaccaṁ karissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun provides services for a householder, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Services for a householder:

segments.pali: agārikassa yāguṁ vā bhattaṁ vā khādanīyaṁ vā pacati, sāṭakaṁ vā veṭhanaṁ vā dhovati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she cooks congee, a meal, or fresh food for a householder, or she washes a wrap garment or a turban for them, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is a congee drink;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is a meal for the Sangha;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is to venerate a shrine;

segments.pali: attano veyyāvaccakarassa yāguṁ vā bhattaṁ vā khādanīyaṁ vā pacati, sāṭakaṁ vā veṭhanaṁ vā dhovati,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she cooks congee, a meal, or fresh food for her own service provider, or she washes a wrap garment or a turban for them;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on galleries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 45. Not resolving a legal issue

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a certain nun went to the nun Thullanandā and said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please come, venerable, and resolve this legal issue.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā agreed,

segments.pali: “sādhū”ti paṭissuṇitvā, neva vūpasameti na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: but then neither resolved it nor made any effort to do so.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā

segments.pali: ‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā, neva vūpasamessati na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: agree to resolve a legal issue, but then neither resolve it nor make any effort to do so?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā acted like this?”

segments.pali: ‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā, neva vūpasameti na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karotī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā act like this?

segments.pali: ‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā, neva vūpasamessati na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā—‘ehāyye, imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamehī’ti vuccamānā—‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva vūpasameyya na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If, when a nun is requested by a nun to resolve a legal issue, she agrees, but then neither resolves it nor makes any effort to resolve it, then, if there were no obstacles, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: By a nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: by another nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A legal issue:

segments.pali: cattāri adhikaraṇāni— vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are four kinds of legal issues: legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, legal issues arising from business.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: To resolve a legal issue:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: to make a decision on a legal issue.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, if there were no obstacles:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: when there is no obstacle.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Neither resolves it:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not resolve it herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nor makes any effort to resolve it:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not ask anyone else.

segments.pali: “Neva vūpasamessāmi na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karissāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, thinking, “I’ll neither resolve it nor make any effort to resolve it,” then, by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya upasampannasaññā adhikaraṇaṁ neva vūpasameti na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, and she perceives her as such, and she neither resolves the legal issue nor makes any effort to resolve it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya vematikā adhikaraṇaṁ neva vūpasameti, na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she neither resolves the legal issue nor makes any effort to resolve it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampannāya anupasampannasaññā adhikaraṇaṁ neva vūpasameti, na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is fully ordained, but she does not perceive her as such, and she neither resolves the legal issue nor makes any effort to resolve it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anupasampannāya adhikaraṇaṁ neva vūpasameti, na vūpasamāya ussukkaṁ karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she neither resolves the legal issue nor makes any effort to resolve it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the other person is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches, but is unable to find anyone to settle it;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on galleries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 46. Giving food to a non-monastic

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī naṭānampi naṭakānampi laṅghakānampi sokajjhāyikānampi kumbhathūṇikānampi sahatthā khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ deti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā was personally giving food to actors, dancers, acrobats, magicians, and musicians, saying,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please praise me in public.”

segments.pali: Naṭāpi naṭakāpi laṅghakāpi sokajjhāyikāpi kumbhathūṇikāpi thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā parisati vaṇṇaṁ bhāsanti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they did:

segments.pali: “ayyā thullanandā bahussutā bhāṇikā visāradā paṭṭā dhammiṁ kathaṁ kātuṁ;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable Thullanandā is a learned reciter; she’s confident and skilled at giving teachings.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Give to her; work for her!”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā agārikassa sahatthā khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ dassatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā personally give food to householders?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī agārikassa sahatthā khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ detī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā does this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī agārikassa sahatthā khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ dassati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī agārikassa vā paribbājakassa vā paribbājikāya vā sahatthā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā dadeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun personally gives fresh or cooked food to a householder or a male wanderer or a female wanderer, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A householder:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: anyone who lives at home.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A male wanderer:

segments.pali: bhikkhuñca sāmaṇerañca ṭhapetvā yo koci paribbājakasamāpanno.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any male wanderer apart from Buddhist monks and novice monks.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A female wanderer:

segments.pali: bhikkhuniñca sikkhamānañca sāmaṇeriñca ṭhapetvā yā kāci paribbājikasamāpannā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any female wanderer apart from Buddhist nuns, trainee nuns, and novice nuns.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Fresh food:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: apart from the five cooked foods

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and water and tooth cleaners, the rest is called “fresh food”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Cooked food:

segments.pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives:

segments.pali: kāyena vā kāyapaṭibaddhena vā nissaggiyena vā deti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives by body or by what is connected to the body or by releasing, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she gives water or tooth cleaners, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not give, but has it given;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives by placing it near the person;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives ointments for external use;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on galleries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 47. Not relinquishing a communal robe

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī āvasathacīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā did not relinquish the communal robe, but continued using it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Other menstruating nuns did not get to use it.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā āvasathacīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā not relinquish the communal robe, but continue using it?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī āvasathacīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī āvasathacīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī āvasathacīvaraṁ anissajjitvā paribhuñjeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun does not relinquish a communal robe, but continues to use it, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A communal robe:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it is given specifically for the use of menstruating nuns.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Does not relinquish, but continues to use it:

segments.pali: dvetisso rattiyo paribhuñjitvā catutthadivase dhovitvā bhikkhuniyā vā sikkhamānāya vā sāmaṇeriyā vā anissajjitvā paribhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses it for two or three days, washes it on the fourth day, and then uses it again without relinquishing it to a nun or a trainee nun or a novice nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been relinquished, and she perceives that it has not, and she uses it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been relinquished, but she is unsure of it, and she uses it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been relinquished, but she perceives that it has, and she uses it, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has been relinquished, but she perceives that it has not, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has been relinquished, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has been relinquished, and she perceives that it has, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she relinquishes it and then uses it;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses it again at the next turn;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there are no other menstruating nuns;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if her robe has been stolen;

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 48. Not relinquishing a lodging

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī āvasathaṁ anissajjitvā cārikaṁ pakkāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Thullanandā went wandering without first relinquishing her lodging.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā āvasatho ḍayhati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards her lodging caught fire.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Some nuns said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Come, venerables, let’s remove her things.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But others replied,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “No, let’s not.

segments.pali: Yaṁ kiñci naṭṭhaṁ sabbaṁ amhe abhiyuñjissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She’ll just make us responsible for anything that gets lost.”

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī punadeva taṁ āvasathaṁ paccāgantvā bhikkhuniyo pucchi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When Thullanandā returned to her lodging, she asked the nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, I hope you removed my things?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “No, we didn’t.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo āvasathe ḍayhamāne bhaṇḍakaṁ na nīharissantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns not remove the contents when a lodging is burning?”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā āvasathaṁ anissajjitvā cārikaṁ pakkamissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā go wandering without relinquishing her lodging?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī āvasathaṁ anissajjitvā cārikaṁ pakkamatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī āvasathaṁ anissajjitvā cārikaṁ pakkamissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī āvasathaṁ anissajjitvā cārikaṁ pakkameyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun goes wandering without relinquishing her lodging, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lodging:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one with a door is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Goes wandering without relinquishing:

segments.pali: bhikkhuniyā vā sikkhamānāya vā sāmaṇeriyā vā anissajjitvā parikkhittassa āvasathassa parikkhepaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she crosses the boundary of an enclosed lodging without first relinquishing it to a nun, a trainee nun, or a novice nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Aparikkhittassa āvasathassa upacāraṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she goes beyond the vicinity of an unenclosed lodging, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been relinquished, and she perceives that it has not, and she goes, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been relinquished, but she is unsure of it, and she goes, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has not been relinquished, but she perceives that it has, and she goes, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not relinquish a lodging without a door, and then goes, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has been relinquished, but she perceives that it has not, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has been relinquished, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it has been relinquished, and she perceives that it has, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she relinquishes it and then goes;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches, but is unable to find anyone to relinquish it to;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo tiracchānavijjaṁ pariyāpuṇanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns from the group of six were studying worldly subjects.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo tiracchānavijjaṁ pariyāpuṇissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns study worldly subjects? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo tiracchānavijjaṁ pariyāpuṇantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo tiracchānavijjaṁ pariyāpuṇissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun studies worldly subjects, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Worldly subjects:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whatever is external to the Buddha’s Teaching, not connected with the goal.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Learns:

segments.pali: padena pariyāpuṇāti, pade pade āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she learns by the line, then for every line she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she learns by the syllable, then for every syllable she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she learns writing;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she learns protective verses;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she learns verses for the purpose of protection;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth training rule is finished.
