english.author: brahmali
english.text: 51. Entering a monks’ monastery

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū gāmakāvāse ekacīvarā cīvarakammaṁ karonti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a number of monks dressed only in sarongs were making robes in a certain village monastery.

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisitvā yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkamiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nuns entered that monastery without asking permission and approached those monks.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The monks complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns enter a monastery without asking permission?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns did that?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do that?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī anāpucchā ārāmaṁ paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun enters a monastery without asking permission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena te bhikkhū tamhā āvāsā pakkamiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards those monks left that monastery.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But even though the nuns had heard that the monks had left, they did not go there.

segments.pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū punadeva taṁ āvāsaṁ paccāgacchiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon the monks returned.

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo—“ayyā āgatā”ti āpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisitvā yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkamiṁsu;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the nuns heard that this was the case, they asked permission and then entered that monastery.

segments.pali: upasaṅkamitvā te bhikkhū abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They approached the monks and bowed down to them.

segments.pali: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho tā bhikkhuniyo te bhikkhū etadavocuṁ—“kissa tumhe, bhaginiyo, ārāmaṁ neva sammajjittha na pānīyaṁ paribhojanīyaṁ upaṭṭhāpitthā”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The monks then said to them, “Sisters, why didn’t you sweep the monastery, or put out water for drinking or water for washing?”

segments.pali: “Bhagavatā, ayyā, sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—‘na anāpucchā ārāmo pavisitabbo’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “The Buddha has laid down a training rule that we may not enter a monastery without asking permission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That’s why we didn’t do it.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the Buddha. Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, santaṁ bhikkhuṁ āpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisituṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, when a monk is available, a nun should enter a monastery only after asking permission.

segments.pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun, when a monk is available, enters a monastery without asking permission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena te bhikkhū tamhā āvāsā pakkamitvā punadeva taṁ āvāsaṁ paccāgacchiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After leaving that monastery once more, the monks returned yet again.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thinking that the monks were still away, the nuns entered the monastery without asking permission.

segments.pali: Tāsaṁ kukkuccaṁ ahosi—“bhagavatā sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—‘na santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmo pavisitabbo’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns became anxious, thinking,

segments.pali: Mayañcamhā santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisimhā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “We have entered a monastery without asking permission, even though a monk was available. And the Buddha has laid down a training rule against this.

segments.pali: Kacci nu kho mayaṁ pācittiyaṁ āpattiṁ āpannā”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Could it be that we have committed an offense entailing confession?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the Buddha.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ sabhikkhukaṁ ārāmaṁ anāpucchā paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun, knowing that there are monks in a monastery, enters it without asking permission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Knowing:

segments.pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassā ārocenti, te vā ārocenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she knows it by herself or others have told her or they have told her.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are monks in a monastery:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: even where monks stay at the foot of a tree.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Enters it without asking permission:

segments.pali: bhikkhuṁ vā sāmaṇeraṁ vā ārāmikaṁ vā anāpucchā parikkhittassa ārāmassa parikkhepaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she crosses the boundary of an enclosed monastery without asking permission of a monk or a novice monk or a monastery worker, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Aparikkhittassa ārāmassa upacāraṁ okkamantiyā āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she enters the vicinity of an unenclosed monastery, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Sabhikkhuke sabhikkhukasaññā santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If there are monks in a monastery, and she perceives that there are, and she enters it without asking permission of an available monk, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Sabhikkhuke vematikā santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If there are monks in a monastery, but she is unsure of it, and she enters it without asking permission of an available monk, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Sabhikkhuke abhikkhukasaññā santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā ārāmaṁ pavisati, anāpatti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If there are monks in a monastery, but she does not perceive that there are, and she enters it without asking permission of an available monk, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If there are no monks in a monastery, but she perceives that there are, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If there are no monks in a monastery, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If there are no monks in a monastery, and she does not perceive that there are, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she enters after asking permission of an available monk;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, when there is no available monk, she enters without asking permission;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she goes while looking at the head of a nun in front of her;

segments.pali: yattha bhikkhuniyo sannipatitā honti tattha gacchati,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is going to where the nuns have gathered;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if a path goes through the monastery;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on monasteries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 52. Abusing a monk

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying  in the house with the peaked roof in the Great Wood near Vesālī,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato upālissa upajjhāyo āyasmā kappitako susāne viharati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerable Upāli’s preceptor, Venerable Kappita, was staying in a charnel ground.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ mahattarā bhikkhunī kālaṅkatā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the leader of the nuns from the group of six had just died.

segments.pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo taṁ bhikkhuniṁ nīharitvā āyasmato kappitakassa vihārassa avidūre jhāpetvā thūpaṁ katvā gantvā tasmiṁ thūpe rodanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They took her to that charnel ground, cremated her near Venerable Kappita’s dwelling, and made a stupa. They then went there to cry.

segments.pali: Atha kho āyasmā kappitako tena saddena ubbāḷho taṁ thūpaṁ bhinditvā pakiresi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerable Kappita was disturbed by the noise, and so he demolished the stupa and scattered the rubble.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns from the group of six thought,

segments.pali: “iminā kappitakena amhākaṁ ayyāya thūpo bhinno, handa naṁ ghātemā”ti mantesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Kappita has demolished our venerable’s stupa; let’s kill him,” and they laid a plan.

segments.pali: Aññatarā bhikkhunī āyasmato upālissa etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A certain nun told Venerable Upāli what was happening,

segments.pali: Āyasmā upāli āyasmato kappitakassa etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and he in turn told Venerable Kappita.

segments.pali: Atha kho āyasmā kappitako vihārā nikkhamitvā nilīno acchi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerable Kappita then left his dwelling and went into hiding.

segments.pali: Atha kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo yenāyasmato kappitakassa vihāro tenupasaṅkamiṁsu;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the nuns from the group of six went to his dwelling

segments.pali: upasaṅkamitvā āyasmato kappitakassa vihāraṁ pāsāṇehi ca leḍḍūhi ca ottharāpetvā, “mato kappitako”ti pakkamiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and crushed it with rocks and lumps of earth. When they thought he was dead, they left.

segments.pali: Atha kho āyasmā kappitako tassā rattiyā accayena pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya vesāliṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The following morning Venerable Kappita robed up, took his bowl and robe, and entered Vesālī for almsfood.

segments.pali: Addasaṁsu kho chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo āyasmantaṁ kappitakaṁ piṇḍāya carantaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the nuns from the group of six saw him,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they said,

segments.pali: “ayaṁ kappitako jīvati, ko nu kho amhākaṁ mantaṁ saṁharī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Kappita is alive! Who spoiled our plan?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When they heard

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it was Venerable Upāli,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they abused him,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayaṁ kāsāvaṭo malamajjano nihīnajacco amhākaṁ mantaṁ saṁharissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could this barber, this dirt remover of inferior caste, spoil our plan?”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo ayyaṁ upāliṁ akkosissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns from the group of six abuse Venerable Upāli?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo upāliṁ akkosantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo upāliṁ akkosissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuṁ akkoseyya vā paribhāseyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun abuses or reviles a monk, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monk:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who is fully ordained.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Abuses:

segments.pali: dasahi vā akkosavatthūhi akkosati etesaṁ vā aññatarena, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she abuses with the ten kinds of abuse or with any one of them, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Reviles:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she induces fear, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampanne upasampannasaññā akkosati vā paribhāsati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is fully ordained, and she perceives him as such, and she abuses or reviles him, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampanne vematikā akkosati vā paribhāsati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, and she abuses or reviles him, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Upasampanne anupasampannasaññā akkosati vā paribhāsati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is fully ordained, but she does not perceive him as such, and she abuses or reviles him, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anupasampannaṁ akkosati vā paribhāsati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she abuses or reviles someone who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is not fully ordained, but she perceives him as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is not fully ordained, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If he is not fully ordained, and she does not perceive him as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at something beneficial;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at giving a teaching;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at giving an instruction;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on monasteries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 53. Reviling the community

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī bhaṇḍanakārikā hoti kalahakārikā vivādakārikā bhassakārikā saṅghe adhikaraṇakārikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Caṇḍakāḷī was quarrelsome and argumentative, and she created legal issues in the Sangha.

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī tassā kamme karīyamāne paṭikkosati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But when a legal procedure was being done against her, the nun Thullanandā objected.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī gāmakaṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards when Thullanandā went to a village on some business,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the Sangha of nuns

segments.pali: “thullanandā bhikkhunī pakkantā”ti caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ āpattiyā adassane ukkhipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: took the opportunity to eject Caṇḍakāḷī for not recognizing an offense.

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī gāmake taṁ karaṇīyaṁ tīretvā punadeva sāvatthiṁ paccāgacchi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When Thullanandā had finished her business, she returned to Sāvatthī.

segments.pali: Caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā āgacchantiyā neva āsanaṁ paññapesi na pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakaṭhalikaṁ upanikkhipi;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: As she was approaching, Caṇḍakāḷī neither prepared a seat for her, nor set out a foot stool, a foot scraper, or water for washing the feet;

segments.pali: na paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi na pānīyena āpucchi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and she did not go out to meet her to receive her bowl and robe or to ask whether she wanted water to drink.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā asked her

segments.pali: “kissa tvaṁ, ayye, mayi āgacchantiyā neva āsanaṁ paññapesi na pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakaṭhalikaṁ upanikkhipi;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: why she was acting like this.

segments.pali: na paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi na pānīyena āpucchī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She replied, “That’s how it is, venerable, when one doesn’t have a protector.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “But how is it, venerable, that you don’t have a protector?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When these nuns knew

segments.pali: ‘ayaṁ anāthā appaññātā, natthi imissā kāci pativattā’ti, āpattiyā adassane ukkhipiṁsū”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: that no-one would speak up for me because I am not esteemed by them and I didn’t have a protector, they ejected me for not recognizing an offense.”

segments.pali: “bālā etā abyattā etā netā jānanti kammaṁ vā kammadosaṁ vā kammavipattiṁ vā kammasampattiṁ vā”ti, caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “They are incompetent fools! They don’t understand legal procedures, nor what makes them fail or succeed.” And being furious, she reviled the community.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāsissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā revile the community because she is furious?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāsatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāsissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī caṇḍīkatā gaṇaṁ paribhāseyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a furious nun reviles the community, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Furious:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: angry is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The community:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the Sangha of nuns is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Reviles:

segments.pali: “bālā etā abyattā etā netā jānanti kammaṁ vā kammadosaṁ vā kammavipattiṁ vā kammasampattiṁ vā”ti paribhāsati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she reviles them, saying, “They are incompetent fools. They don’t understand legal procedures, nor what makes them fail or succeed,” she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Sambahulā bhikkhuniyo vā ekaṁ bhikkhuniṁ vā anupasampannaṁ vā paribhāsati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she reviles several nuns, a single nun, or someone who is not fully ordained, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at something beneficial;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at giving a teaching;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is aiming at giving an instruction;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on monasteries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 54. Eating more after refusing an offer

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro brāhmaṇo bhikkhuniyo nimantetvā bhojesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a certain brahmin had invited the nuns for a meal.

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo bhuttāvī pavāritā ñātikulāni gantvā ekaccā bhuñjiṁsu ekaccā piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya agamaṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the nuns had finished and refused an offer to eat more, they went to their respective families, where some ate and others got almsfood and left.

segments.pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo paṭivissake etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards that brahmin said this to his neighbors,

segments.pali: “bhikkhuniyo mayā, ayyā, santappitā, etha tumhepi santappessāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I have satisfied the nuns. Come, and I’ll satisfy you, too.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could you satisfy us?

segments.pali: Yāpi tayā nimantitā tāpi amhākaṁ gharāni āgantvā ekaccā bhuñjiṁsu ekaccā piṇḍapātaṁ ādāya agamaṁsū”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those nuns who were invited by you came to our houses, where some ate and others got almsfood and left.”

segments.pali: Atha kho so brāhmaṇo ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That brahmin complained and criticized those nuns,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo amhākaṁ ghare bhuñjitvā aññatra bhuñjissanti, na cāhaṁ paṭibalo yāvadatthaṁ dātun”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could they eat in our house and afterwards eat elsewhere? Am I not able to give them as much as they want?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa brāhmaṇassa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of that brahmin,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns finish their meal, refuse an offer to eat more, and then eat elsewhere?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns act in this way?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī nimantitā vā pavāritā vā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun, who has been invited to a meal, refuses an offer to eat more, and then eats fresh or cooked food, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has been invited:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has been invited to eat any of the five cooked foods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Refuses an offer to eat more:

segments.pali: asanaṁ paññāyati, bhojanaṁ paññāyati, hatthapāse ṭhitā abhiharati, paṭikkhepo paññāyati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is eating; there is cooked food; they stand within arm’s reach; there is an offering; there is a refusal.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Fresh food:

segments.pali: pañca bhojanāni yāguṁ yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ ṭhapetvā

english.author: brahmali
english.text: apart from the five cooked foods, congee, the post-midday tonics, the seven-day tonics, and the lifetime tonics,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the rest is called “fresh food”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Cooked food:

segments.pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Nimantite nimantitasaññā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has been invited, and she perceives that she has, and she eats fresh or cooked food, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Nimantite vematikā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has been invited, but she is unsure of it, and she eats fresh or cooked food, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Nimantite animantitasaññā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā khādati vā bhuñjati vā, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has has been invited, but she does not perceive that she has, and she eats fresh or cooked food, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ āhāratthāya paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics for the purpose of food, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has been invited, but has not refused an offer to eat more;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she drinks congee;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she eats more after getting permission from the person who invited her;

segments.pali: yāmakālikaṁ sattāhakālikaṁ yāvajīvikaṁ sati paccaye paribhuñjati,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, when there is a reason, she uses post-midday tonics, seven-day tonics, or lifetime tonics;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on monasteries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 55. Being stingy with families

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī sāvatthiyaṁ aññatarissā visikhāya piṇḍāya caramānā yena aññataraṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a certain nun there was walking for almsfood along a street. She then went to a certain family where she sat down on the prepared seat.

segments.pali: Atha kho te manussā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ bhojetvā etadavocuṁ—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The people there gave her a meal and said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, other nuns may come too.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then that nun thought,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo nāgaccheyyun”ti, bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “What can I do so that other nuns don’t come?” And she went up to other nuns and said,

segments.pali: “amukasmiṁ, ayye, okāse vāḷā sunakhā caṇḍo balibaddo cikkhallo okāso.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, in such-and-such a place there are malicious dogs, a fierce ox, and it’s muddy.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Don’t go there.”

segments.pali: Aññatarāpi bhikkhunī tassā visikhāya piṇḍāya caramānā yena taṁ kulaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Another nun, too, went to that family while walking for almsfood along that street. She sat down on the prepared seat

segments.pali: Atha kho te manussā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ bhojetvā etadavocuṁ—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the people there gave her a meal. They then asked her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, why don’t the nuns come here?”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī tesaṁ manussānaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she told them what had happened.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can a nun keep a family to herself?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun acted like this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī kulaṁ maccharāyissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun act like this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun keeps a family to herself, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A family:

segments.pali: cattāri kulāni— khattiyakulaṁ, brāhmaṇakulaṁ, vessakulaṁ, suddakulaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are four kinds of families: the aristocratic family, the brahmin family, the merchant family, the worker family.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Keeps for herself:

segments.pali: “kathaṁ bhikkhuniyo nāgaccheyyun”ti bhikkhunīnaṁ santike kulassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she thinks, “What can I do so that other nuns don’t come?” and she then speaks disparagingly about that family to the nuns, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Kulassa vā santike bhikkhunīnaṁ avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she speaks disparagingly about that family to the nuns, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: kulaṁ na maccharāyantī santaṁyeva ādīnavaṁ ācikkhati,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is not keeping a family to herself, but describes the drawbacks as they are;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on monasteries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 56. Spending the rainy season apart from monks

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo gāmakāvāse vassaṁvuṭṭhā sāvatthiṁ agamaṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a number of nuns who had completed the rainy-season residence in a village monastery went to Sāvatthī.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns there asked them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, where did you spend the rains residence?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: We hope the instruction was effective?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “There were no monks there.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: So how could the instruction be effective?”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo abhikkhuke āvāse vassaṁ vasissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns spend the rains residence in a monastery without monks?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo abhikkhuke āvāse vassaṁ vasantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo abhikkhuke āvāse vassaṁ vasissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī abhikkhuke āvāse vassaṁ vaseyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun spends the rainy-season residence in a monastery without monks, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monastery without monks:

segments.pali: na sakkā hoti ovādāya vā saṁvāsāya vā gantuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is not able to go to the instruction or to a formal meeting of the community.

segments.pali: “Vassaṁ vasissāmī”ti senāsanaṁ paññapeti pānīyaṁ paribhojanīyaṁ upaṭṭhapeti pariveṇaṁ sammajjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I’ll stay here for the rainy-season residence,” and she prepares a dwelling, sets out water for drinking and water for washing, and sweeps the yard, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At dawn, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: vassupagatā bhikkhū pakkantā vā honti vibbhantā vā kālaṅkatā vā pakkhasaṅkantā vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if the monks who entered the rainy-season residence there depart or disrobe or die or join another religion or sect;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on monasteries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 57. Not doing the invitation ceremony

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo gāmakāvāse vassaṁvuṭṭhā sāvatthiṁ agamaṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a number of nuns who had completed the rainy-season residence in a village monastery went to Sāvatthī.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns there asked them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, where did you spend the rains residence?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Where did you invite the Sangha of monks for correction?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “We didn’t invite the Sangha of monks for correction.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ na pavāressantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns who have completed the rains residence not invite the Sangha of monks for correction?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ na pavārentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns didn’t do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ na pavāressanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns not do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī vassaṁvuṭṭhā ubhatosaṅghe tīhi ṭhānehi na pavāreyya diṭṭhena vā sutena vā parisaṅkāya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun who has completed the rainy-season residence does not invite correction from both Sanghas in regard to three things—what has been seen, heard, or suspected—she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has completed the rainy-season residence:

segments.pali: purimaṁ vā temāsaṁ pacchimaṁ vā temāsaṁ vuṭṭhā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has completed the first three or the last three months of the rainy-season residence.

segments.pali: Ubhatosaṅghe tīhi ṭhānehi na pavāressāmi diṭṭhena vā sutena vā parisaṅkāya vā”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I won’t invite correction from both Sanghas in regard to three things—what has been seen, heard, or suspected,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches, but does not find anyone to invite for correction;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on monasteries

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 58. Not going to the instruction

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying in the Sakyan country in the Banyan Tree Monastery at Kapilavatthu,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhunupassayaṁ upasaṅkamitvā chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo ovadanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the monks from the group of six went to the nunnery to instruct the nuns from the group of six.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards other nuns said to those nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Come, venerables, let’s go to the instruction.”

segments.pali: “Yampi mayaṁ, ayye, gaccheyyāma ovādassa kāraṇā, ayyā chabbaggiyā idheva āgantvā amhe ovadantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “There’s no need. The monks from the group of six came and instructed us right here.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo ovādaṁ na gacchissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns from the group of six not go to the instruction?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo ovādaṁ na gacchantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns didn’t do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo ovādaṁ na gacchissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns from the group of six not do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ovādāya vā saṁvāsāya vā na gaccheyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun does not go to the instruction or to a formal meeting of the community, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The instruction:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the eight important principles.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The community:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training.

segments.pali: Ovādāya vā saṁvāsāya vā na gacchissāmīti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I won’t go to the instruction or to formal meetings of the community,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches for a companion nun, but does not find one;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 59. Not seeking the instruction

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo uposathampi na pucchanti ovādampi na yācanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns did not enquire about the observance day nor ask for the instruction.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The monks complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo uposathampi na pucchissanti ovādampi na yācissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns not enquire about the observance day nor ask for the instruction?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo uposathampi na pucchanti ovādampi na yācantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nuns don’t do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo uposathampi na pucchissanti ovādampi na yācissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns not do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Anvaddhamāsaṁ bhikkhuniyā bhikkhusaṅghato dve dhammā paccāsīsitabbā—uposathapucchakañca ovādūpasaṅkamanañca. Taṁ atikkāmentiyā pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Every half-month a nun should seek two things from the Sangha of monks: asking it about the observance day and going to it for the instruction. If she lets the half-month pass, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Every half-month:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: every observance day.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The observance day:

segments.pali: dve uposathā— cātuddasiko ca pannarasiko ca.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are two observance days: the fourteenth and the fifteenth day of the lunar half-month.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The instruction:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the eight important principles.

segments.pali: “Uposathampi na pucchissāmi ovādampi na yācissāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I won’t enquire about the observance day, nor ask for the instruction,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches for a companion nun, but does not find one;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 60. Getting medical treatment from a man

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī pasākhe jātaṁ gaṇḍaṁ purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā bhedāpesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a certain nun was alone with a man, when she had him rupture an abscess growing on the lower part of her body.

segments.pali: Atha kho so puriso taṁ bhikkhuniṁ dūsetuṁ upakkami.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When he tried to rape her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she cried out.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns rushed up and asked her why.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she told them what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī pasākhe jātaṁ gaṇḍaṁ purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā bhedāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun be alone with a man and have him rupture an abscess growing on the lower part of her body?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī pasākhe jātaṁ gaṇḍaṁ purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā bhedāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī pasākhe jātaṁ gaṇḍaṁ purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā bhedāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī pasākhe jātaṁ gaṇḍaṁ vā rudhitaṁ vā anapaloketvā saṅghaṁ vā gaṇaṁ vā purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā bhedāpeyya vā phālāpeyya vā dhovāpeyya vā ālimpāpeyya vā bandhāpeyya vā mocāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun, alone with a man, without getting permission from the Sangha or a group, has an abscess or a wound situated on the lower part of her body ruptured by him, or split open, washed, anointed, bandaged, or unwrapped by him, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The lower part of the body:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: below the navel and above the knees.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Situated:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: situated there.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An abscess:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any kind of abscess.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A wound:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any kind of sore.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without getting permission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: without having asked permission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the Sangha of nuns is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A group:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: several nuns is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A man:

segments.pali: manussapuriso, na yakkho, na peto, na tiracchānagato, viññū paṭibalo dūsetuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of raping.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: With:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: together.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Alone:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: just the man and the nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she tells him, “Rupture it,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it has been ruptured, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she tells him, “Split it open,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it has been split open, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she tells him, “Wash it,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it has been washed, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she tells him, “Anoint it,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it has been anointed, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she tells him, “Bandage it,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it has been bandaged, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she tells him, “Unwrap it,” she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When it has been unwrapped, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: apaloketvā bhedāpeti vā phālāpeti vā dhovāpeti vā ālimpāpeti vā bandhāpeti vā mocāpeti vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets permission and then has it ruptured, split open, washed, anointed, bandaged, or unwrapped;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has a female companion who understands;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The tenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth subchapter on monasteries is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 61. Ordaining a pregnant woman

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo gabbhiniṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns gave the full admission to a pregnant woman.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she walked for alms,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: people said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Give almsfood to the venerable. She’s carrying a heavy burden.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns give the full admission to a pregnant woman?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns do this?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo gabbhiniṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a pregnant woman, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A pregnant woman:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a woman with child is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the woman is pregnant, and the nun perceives her as such, yet she gives her the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the woman is pregnant, but the nun is unsure of it, yet she gives her the full admission, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the woman is pregnant, but the nun does not perceive her as such, and she gives her the full admission, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the woman is not pregnant, but the nun perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the woman is not pregnant, but the nun is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the woman is not pregnant, and the nun does not perceive her as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a woman who is pregnant, but she  does not perceive her as such;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a woman who is not pregnant, and she does not perceive her as such;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a woman who is breastfeeding, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A woman who is breastfeeding:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is a mother or a wet-nurse.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.text: If the woman is not breastfeeding, but the nun is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.text: If the woman is not breastfeeding, and the nun does not perceive her as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 63. Ordaining an untrained candidate

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were giving the full admission to trainee nuns who had not trained for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Tā bālā honti abyattā na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were ignorant and incompetent, and did not know what was allowable and what was not.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns give the full admission to trainee nuns who haven’t trained for two years in the six rules?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After rebuking them …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ dātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, approval is required for a trainee nun to train for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And it should be given like this.

segments.pali: Tāya sikkhamānāya saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After approaching the Sangha of nuns, that trainee nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, I, so-and-so, am a trainee nun under venerable so-and-so.

segments.pali: Saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I ask the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she should ask a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should then inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya sikkhamānā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The trainee nun so-and-so is asking the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ saṅgho itthannāmāya sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ dadeyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should give approval to the trainee nun so-and-so to train for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya sikkhamānā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The trainee nun so-and-so is asking the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmāya sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ deti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha gives approval to the trainee nun so-and-so to train for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiyā dānaṁ, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of giving approval to the trainee nun so-and-so to train for two years in the six rules should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

segments.pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmāya sikkhamānāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammuti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has given approval to the trainee nun so-and-so to train for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That trainee nun should then be told to say this:

segments.pali: “pāṇātipātā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘I undertake to abstain from killing living beings for two years without transgression.

segments.pali: Adinnādānā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I undertake to abstain from stealing for two years without transgression.

segments.pali: Abrahmacariyā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I undertake to abstain from sexual activity for two years without transgression.

segments.pali: Musāvādā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I undertake to abstain from lying for two years without transgression.

segments.pali: Surāmerayamajjappamādaṭṭhānā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I undertake to abstain from alcohol, which causes heedlessness, for two years without transgression.

segments.pali: Vikālabhojanā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I undertake to abstain from eating at the wrong time for two years without transgression.’”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after rebuking those nuns in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a trainee nun who has not trained for two years in the six rules, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two years:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two twelve-month periods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not trained:

segments.pali: sikkhā vā na dinnā hoti, dinnā vā sikkhā kupitā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the training has not been given to her, or the training has been given to her, but she has failed in it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a trainee nun who has trained for two years in the six rules;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession
