english.author: brahmali
english.text: 64. Ordaining a trained candidate without approval

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were giving the full admission to trainee nuns who had trained for two years in the six rules, but who had not been approved by the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns said,

segments.pali: “etha, sikkhamānā, imaṁ jānātha, imaṁ detha, imaṁ āharatha, iminā attho, imaṁ kappiyaṁ karothā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Come, trainee nuns, find out about this,” “Give this,” “Bring this,” “There’s need for this,” or “Make this allowable.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But they replied,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, we’re not trainee nuns. We’re nuns.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns give the full admission to trainee nuns who have trained for two years in the six rules, but who haven’t been approved by the Sangha?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After rebuking them …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya sikkhamānāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ dātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, approval is required for the full admission of a trainee nun who has trained for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And it should be given like this.

segments.pali: Tāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya sikkhamānāya saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After approaching the Sangha of nuns, that trainee nun should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

segments.pali: ‘ahaṁ, ayye, itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā sikkhamānā saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, I, the trainee nun so-and-so, who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, ask the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she should ask a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should then inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā sikkhamānā saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The trainee nun so-and-so, who has trained under venerable so-and-so for two years in the six rules, is asking the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya sikkhamānāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ dadeyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should give approval for the trainee nun so-and-so, who has trained for two years in the six rules, to be fully admitted.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā sikkhamānā saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The trainee nun so-and-so, who has trained under venerable so-and-so for two years in the six rules, is asking the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya sikkhamānāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ deti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha gives approval for the trainee nun so-and-so, who has trained for two years in the six rules, to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya sikkhamānāya vuṭṭhānasammutiyā dānaṁ, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of giving approval for the trainee nun so-and-so, who has trained for two years in the six rules, to be fully admitted should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

segments.pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya sikkhamānāya vuṭṭhānasammuti;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has given approval for the trainee nun so-and-so, who has trained for two years in the six rules, to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after rebuking those nuns in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a trainee nun who has trained for two years in the six rules, but who has not been approved by the Sangha, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two years:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two twelve-month periods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has trained:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has trained in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not been approved:

segments.pali: ñattidutiyena kammena vuṭṭhānasammuti na dinnā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: approval to be fully admitted has not been given through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ sikkhamānaṁ saṅghena sammataṁ vuṭṭhāpeti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a trainee nun who has trained for two years in the six rules and who has been approved by the Sangha;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 65. Ordaining a married girl under twelve

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo ūnadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns were giving the full admission to married girls less than twelve years old.

segments.pali: Tā akkhamā honti sītassa uṇhassa jighacchāya pipāsāya ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ duruttānaṁ durāgatānaṁ vacanapathānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were unable to endure cold and heat; hunger and thirst; contact with horseflies, mosquitoes, wind, the burning sun, and creeping animals and insects; rude and unwelcome speech;

segments.pali: Uppannānaṁ sārīrikānaṁ vedanānaṁ dukkhānaṁ tibbānaṁ kharānaṁ kaṭukānaṁ asātānaṁ amanāpānaṁ pāṇaharānaṁ anadhivāsakajātikā honti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they were unable to bear up with bodily feelings that are painful, severe, sharp, and destructive of life.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns give the full admission to married girls less than twelve years old?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo ūnadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo ūnadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

segments.pali: Ūnadvādasavassā, bhikkhave, gihigatā akkhamā hoti sītassa uṇhassa jighacchāya pipāsāya ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ duruttānaṁ durāgatānaṁ vacanapathānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A married girl less than twelve years old is unable to endure cold and heat; hunger and thirst; contact with horseflies, mosquitoes, wind, the burning sun, and creeping animals and insects; rude and unwelcome speech;

segments.pali: Uppannānaṁ sārīrikānaṁ vedanānaṁ dukkhānaṁ tibbānaṁ kharānaṁ kaṭukānaṁ asātānaṁ amanāpānaṁ pāṇaharānaṁ anadhivāsakajātikā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and she’s unable to bear up with bodily feelings that are painful, severe, sharp, and destructive of life.

segments.pali: Dvādasavassāva kho, bhikkhave, gihigatā khamā hoti sītassa uṇhassa jighacchāya pipāsāya ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ duruttānaṁ durāgatānaṁ vacanapathānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But a married girl who is twelve years old is able to endure these things.

segments.pali: Uppannānaṁ sārīrikānaṁ vedanānaṁ dukkhānaṁ tibbānaṁ kharānaṁ kaṭukānaṁ asātānaṁ amanāpānaṁ pāṇaharānaṁ adhivāsakajātikā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ūnadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a married girl who is less than twelve years old, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is less than twelve years old:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has not reached twelve years of age.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A married girl:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who has gone to the place of a man is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is less than twelve years old, and the nun perceives her as less, yet she gives her the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is less than twelve years old, but the nun is unsure of it, yet she gives her the full admission, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is less than twelve years old, but the nun perceives her as more, and she gives her the full admission, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is more than twelve years old, but the nun perceives her as less, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is more than twelve years old, but the nun is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is more than twelve years old, and the nun perceives her as more, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a girl less than twelve years old, but she perceives her as more;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a girl more than twelve years old, and she perceives her as more;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 66. Ordaining an untrained married girl

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were giving the full admission to married girls who were more than twelve years old, but who had not trained for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Tā bālā honti abyattā na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were ignorant and incompetent, and they did not know what was allowable and what was not.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns give the full admission to married girls who are more than twelve years old, but who haven’t trained for two years in the six rules?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …”

segments.pali: vigarahitvā dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After rebuking them … he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ dātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, approval is required for a married girl who is more than twelve years old to train for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And the approval is to be given like this.

segments.pali: Tāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After approaching the Sangha of nuns, that married girl who is more than twelve years old should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

segments.pali: ‘ahaṁ, ayye, itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassā gihigatā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, I, so-and-so, a married girl who is more than twelve years old, am training under venerable so-and-so. I ask the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she should ask a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should then inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassā gihigatā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old, is asking the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ saṅgho itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ dadeyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should give approval to the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old, to train for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassā gihigatā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old, is asking the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ deti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha gives approval to the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old, to train for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiyā dānaṁ, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of giving approval to the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old, to train for two years in the six rules should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

segments.pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammuti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has given approval to the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old, to train for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’

segments.pali: Sā paripuṇṇadvādasavassā gihigatā evaṁ vadehīti vattabbā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That married girl, who is more than twelve years old, should be told to say this:

segments.pali: “pāṇātipātā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘I undertake to abstain from killing living beings for two years without transgression. …

segments.pali: vikālabhojanā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I undertake to abstain from eating at the wrong time for two years without transgression.’”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after rebuking those nuns in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a married girl who is more than twelve years old, but who has not trained for two years in the six rules, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is more than twelve years old:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has reached twelve years of age.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A married girl:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who has gone to the place of a man is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two years:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two twelve-month periods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not trained:

segments.pali: sikkhā vā na dinnā hoti, dinnā vā sikkhā kupitā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the training has not been given to her, or the training has been given to her, but she has failed in it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpeti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a married girl who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 67. Ordaining a trained married girl without approval

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were giving the full admission to married girls who were more than twelve years old and who had trained for two years in the six rules, but who had not been approved by the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns said,

segments.pali: “etha, sikkhamānā, imaṁ jānātha, imaṁ detha, imaṁ āharatha, iminā attho, imaṁ kappiyaṁ karothā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Come, trainee nuns, find out about this,” “Give this,” “Bring this,” “There’s need for this,” or “Make this allowable.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But they replied,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, we’re not trainee nuns. We’re nuns.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns give the full admission to married girls who are more than twelve years old and who have trained for two years in the six rules, but who haven’t been approved by the Sangha?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After rebuking them …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ dātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, approval is required for the full admission of a married girl who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And it should be given like this.

segments.pali: Tāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After approaching the Sangha of nuns, that married girl who is more than twelve years old should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

segments.pali: ‘ahaṁ, ayye, itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassā gihigatā dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā, saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, I, the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, ask the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she should ask a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should then inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassā gihigatā dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, is asking the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ dadeyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should give approval for the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules, to be fully admitted.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassā gihigatā dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, is asking the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ deti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha gives approval for the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules, to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammutiyā dānaṁ, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of giving approval for the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules, to be fully admitted should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

segments.pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya gihigatāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammuti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has given approval for the married girl so-and-so, who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules, to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after rebuking those nuns in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a married girl who is more than twelve years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules, but who has not been approved by the Sangha, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is more than twelve years old:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has reached twelve years of age.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A married girl:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who has gone to the place of a man is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two years:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two twelve-month periods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has trained:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has trained in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not been approved:

segments.pali: ñattidutiyena kammena vuṭṭhānasammuti na dinnā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: approval to be fully admitted has not been given through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: paripuṇṇadvādasavassaṁ gihigataṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena sammataṁ vuṭṭhāpeti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a married girl who is more than twelve years old, who has trained for two years in the six rules, and who has been approved by the Sangha;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh training rule is finished.

english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 68. Not guiding a disciple after ordination

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā dve vassāni neva anuggaṇhāti na anuggaṇhāpeti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā gave the full admission to a disciple, but then, for the next two years, neither guided her nor had her guided.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were ignorant and incompetent,

segments.pali: na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they did not know what was allowable and what was not.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā dve vassāni neva anuggaṇhissati na anuggaṇhāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can Venerable Thullanandā give the full admission to a disciple, and then neither guide her nor have her guided for two years?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā dve vassāni neva anuggaṇhāti na anuggaṇhāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā does this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā dve vassāni neva anuggaṇhissati na anuggaṇhāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā dve vassāni neva anuggaṇheyya na anuggaṇhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a disciple, and then, for the next two years, neither guides her nor has her guided, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A disciple:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a student is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two years:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two twelve-month periods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Neither guides her:

segments.pali: na sayaṁ anuggaṇheyya uddesena paripucchāya ovādena anusāsaniyā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not herself guide her—through recitation, through questioning, through instruction, through teaching.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nor has her guided:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not ask anyone else.

segments.pali: “dve vassāni neva anuggaṇhissāmi na anuggaṇhāpessāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I’ll neither guide her nor have her guided for two years,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches for someone to guide her, but cannot find anyone;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 69. Not following a mentor

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo vuṭṭhāpitaṁ pavattiniṁ dve vassāni nānubandhanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time nuns who had received the full admission did not follow their mentors for two years.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were ignorant and incompetent,

segments.pali: na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they did not know what was allowable and what was not.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo vuṭṭhāpitaṁ pavattiniṁ dve vassāni nānubandhissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can those nuns receive the full admission and then not follow their mentors for two years?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vuṭṭhāpitaṁ pavattiniṁ dve vassāni nānubandhantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns don’t do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo vuṭṭhāpitaṁ pavattiniṁ dve vassāni nānubandhissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns not do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī vuṭṭhāpitaṁ pavattiniṁ dve vassāni nānubandheyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun does not follow the mentor who gave her the full admission for two years, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who gave her the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who gave her the full ordination.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The mentor:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the preceptor is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two years:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two twelve-month periods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Does not follow:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: does not herself attend on her.

segments.pali: Dve vassāni nānubandhissāmīti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I won’t follow her for two years,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: upajjhāyā bālā vā hoti alajjinī vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if the preceptor is ignorant or shameless;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 70. Not sending away a new disciple

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā neva vūpakāseti na vūpakāsāpeti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā gave the full admission to a disciple, but then neither sent her away nor had her sent away.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And so her husband got hold of her.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā neva vūpakāsessati na vūpakāsāpessati, sāmiko aggahesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā give the full admission to a disciple, and then neither send her away nor have her sent away?

segments.pali: Sacāyaṁ bhikkhunī pakkantā assa, na ca sāmiko gaṇheyyā”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If this nun had gone away, her husband would not have gotten hold of her.” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā neva vūpakāseti na vūpakāsāpeti, sāmiko aggahesī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā didn’t do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā neva vūpakāsessati na vūpakāsāpessati, sāmiko aggahesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā not do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī sahajīviniṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā neva vūpakāseyya na vūpakāsāpeyya antamaso chappañcayojanānipi, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a disciple, and then neither sends her away nor has her sent away at least 65 to 80 kilometers, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Disciple:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: student is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Neither sends her away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not herself send her away.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nor has her sent away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not ask anyone else to send her away.

segments.pali: “Neva vūpakāsessāmi na vūpakāsāpessāmi antamaso chappañcayojanānipī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I’ll neither send her away nor have her sent away, not even 65 to 80 kilometers,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches for a companion nun for her, but cannot find anyone;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The tenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh subchapter on pregnant women is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 71. Ordaining an unmarried woman under twenty

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo ūnavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were giving the full admission to unmarried women less than twenty years old.

segments.pali: Tā akkhamā honti sītassa uṇhassa jighacchāya pipāsāya ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ duruttānaṁ durāgatānaṁ vacanapathānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were unable to endure cold and heat; hunger and thirst; contact with horseflies, mosquitoes, wind, the burning sun, and creeping animals and insects; rude and unwelcome speech;

segments.pali: Uppannānaṁ sārīrikānaṁ vedanānaṁ dukkhānaṁ tibbānaṁ kharānaṁ kaṭukānaṁ asātānaṁ amanāpānaṁ pāṇaharānaṁ anadhivāsakajātikā honti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they were unable to bear up with bodily feelings that are painful, severe, sharp, and destructive of life.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns give the full admission to unmarried women less than twenty years old?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo ūnavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo ūnavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

segments.pali: Ūnavīsativassā, bhikkhave, kumāribhūtā akkhamā hoti sītassa uṇhassa …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An unmarried woman less than twenty years old is unable to endure cold and heat; hunger and thirst; contact with horseflies, mosquitoes, wind, the burning sun, and creeping animals and insects; rude and unwelcome speech;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and she’s unable to bear up with bodily feelings that are painful, severe, sharp, and destructive of life.

segments.pali: Vīsativassāva kho, bhikkhave, kumāribhūtā khamā hoti sītassa uṇhassa …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But an unmarried woman who is twenty years old is able to endure these things.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ūnavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to an unmarried woman who is less than twenty years old, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is less than twenty years old:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has not reached twenty years of age.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An unmarried woman:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a novice nun is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is less than twenty years old, and the nun perceives her as less, yet she gives her the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is less than twenty years old, but the nun is unsure of it, yet she gives her the full admission, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is less than twenty years old, but the nun perceives her as more, and she gives her the full admission, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is more than twenty years old, but the nun perceives her as less, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is more than twenty years old, but the nun is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the girl is more than twenty years old, and the nun perceives her as more, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a girl less than twenty years old, but she perceives her as more;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to a girl more than twenty years old, and she perceives her as more;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on unmarried women

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 72. Ordaining an untrained unmarried woman

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were giving the full admission to unmarried women who were more than twenty years old, but who had not trained for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were ignorant and incompetent,

segments.pali: na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they did not know what was allowable and what was not.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns give the full admission to unmarried women who are more than twenty years old, but who haven’t trained for two years in the six rules?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After rebuking them …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, aṭṭhārasavassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ dātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, approval is required for an unmarried woman who is eighteen years old to train for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And the approval is to be given like this.

segments.pali: Tāya aṭṭhārasavassāya kumāribhūtāya saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After approaching the Sangha of nuns, that unmarried woman who is eighteen years old should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

segments.pali: ‘ahaṁ, ayye, itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya aṭṭhārasavassā kumāribhūtā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, I, so-and-so, an unmarried woman who is eighteen years old, am training under venerable so-and-so. I ask the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she should ask a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should then inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya aṭṭhārasavassā kumāribhūtā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The unmarried woman so-and-so, who is eighteen years old, is asking the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmāya aṭṭhārasavassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ dadeyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should give approval to the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is eighteen years old, to train for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya aṭṭhārasavassā kumāribhūtā saṅghaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The unmarried woman so-and-so, who is eighteen years old, is asking the Sangha for approval to train for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmāya aṭṭhārasavassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiṁ deti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha gives approval to the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is eighteen years old, to train for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya aṭṭhārasavassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammutiyā dānaṁ, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of giving approval to the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is eighteen years old, to train for two years in the six rules should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

segments.pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmāya aṭṭhārasavassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhāsammuti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has given approval to the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is eighteen years old, to train for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’

segments.pali: Sā aṭṭhārasavassā kumāribhūtā evaṁ vadehīti vattabbā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That unmarried woman who is eighteen years old should be told to say this:

segments.pali: “pāṇātipātā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmi …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘I undertake to abstain from killing living beings for two years without transgression. …

segments.pali: vikālabhojanā veramaṇiṁ dve vassāni avītikkamma samādānaṁ samādiyāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I undertake to abstain from eating at the wrong time for two years without transgression.’”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after rebuking those nuns in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu asikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to an unmarried woman who is more than twenty years old, but who has not trained for two years in the six rules, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is more than twenty years old:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has reached twenty years of age.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An unmarried woman:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a novice nun is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two years:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two twelve-month periods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not trained:

segments.pali: sikkhā vā na dinnā hoti, dinnā vā sikkhā kupitā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the training has not been given to her, or the training has been given to her, but she has failed in it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ vuṭṭhāpeti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to an unmarried woman who is more than twenty years old, and who has trained for two years in the six rules;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
