english.author: brahmali
english.text: 73. Ordaining a trained unmarried woman without approval

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were giving the full admission to unmarried women who were more than twenty years old and who had trained for two years in the six rules, but who had not been approved by the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns said,

segments.pali: “etha, sikkhamānā, imaṁ jānātha, imaṁ detha, imaṁ āharatha, iminā attho, imaṁ kappiyaṁ karothā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Come, trainee nuns, find out about this,” “Give this,” “Bring this,” “There’s need for this,” or “Make this allowable.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But they replied,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, we’re not trainee nuns. We’re nuns.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns give the full admission to unmarried women who are more than twenty years old and who have trained for two years in the six rules, but who haven’t been approved by the Sangha?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After rebuking them …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “anujānāmi, bhikkhave, paripuṇṇavīsativassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ dātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, approval is required for the full admission of an unmarried woman who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And it should be given like this.

segments.pali: Tāya paripuṇṇavīsativassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After approaching the Sangha of nuns, that unmarried woman who is more than twenty years old should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

segments.pali: ‘ahaṁ, ayye, itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇavīsativassā kumāribhūtā dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, I, the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, ask the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she should ask a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should then inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇavīsativassā kumāribhūtā dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The unmarried woman so-and-so, who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, is asking the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ saṅgho itthannāmāya paripuṇṇavīsativassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ dadeyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should give approval for the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, to be fully admitted.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā itthannāmāya ayyāya paripuṇṇavīsativassā kumāribhūtā dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhā saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The unmarried woman so-and-so, who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, is asking the Sangha for approval to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmāya paripuṇṇavīsativassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammutiṁ deti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha gives approval for the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya paripuṇṇavīsativassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammutiyā dānaṁ, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of giving approval for the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, to be fully admitted should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

segments.pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmāya paripuṇṇavīsativassāya kumāribhūtāya dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhāya vuṭṭhānasammuti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has given approval for the unmarried woman so-and-so, who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules under venerable so-and-so, to be fully admitted.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after rebuking those nuns in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena asammataṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to an unmarried woman who is more than twenty years old and who has trained for two years in the six rules, but who has not been approved by the Sangha, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is more than twenty years old:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has reached twenty years of age.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An unmarried woman:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a novice nun is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two years:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: two twelve-month periods.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has trained:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has trained in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not been approved:

segments.pali: ñattidutiyena kammena vuṭṭhānasammuti na dinnā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: approval to be fully admitted has not been given through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: paripuṇṇavīsativassaṁ kumāribhūtaṁ dve vassāni chasu dhammesu sikkhitasikkhaṁ saṅghena sammataṁ vuṭṭhāpeti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives the full admission to an unmarried woman who is more than twenty years old, who has trained for two years in the six rules, and who has been approved by the Sangha;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 74. Giving ordination while under twelve years’ seniority

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo ūnadvādasavassā vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time nuns who had less than twelve years of seniority were giving the full admission.

segments.pali: Tā bālā honti abyattā na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were ignorant and incompetent, and they did not know what was allowable and what was not.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And their students too were ignorant and incompetent,

segments.pali: na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they too did not know what was allowable and what was not.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns who have less than twelve years of seniority give the full admission?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo ūnadvādasavassā vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun who has less than twelve years of seniority gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has less than twelve years of seniority:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has not reached twelve years of seniority.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if one who has twelve years of seniority gives the full admission;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 75. Giving ordination without approval

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassā saṅghena asammatā vuṭṭhāpenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time nuns who had twelve years of seniority were giving the full admission without being approved by the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They were ignorant and incompetent,

segments.pali: na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they did not know what was allowable and what was not.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And their students too were ignorant and incompetent,

segments.pali: na jānanti kappiyaṁ vā akappiyaṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they too did not know what was allowable and what was not.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassā saṅghena asammatā vuṭṭhāpessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns who have twelve years of seniority give the full admission without being approved by the Sangha?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassā saṅghena asammatā vuṭṭhāpentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo paripuṇṇadvādasavassā saṅghena asammatā vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After rebuking them …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, approval is required for a nun who has twelve years of seniority to give the full admission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And the approval is to be given like this.

segments.pali: Tāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya bhikkhuniyā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After approaching the Sangha of nuns, that nun who has twelve years of seniority should arrange her upper robe over one shoulder and pay respect at the feet of the senior nuns. She should then squat on her heels, raise her joined palms, and say:

segments.pali: ‘ahaṁ, ayye, itthannāmā paripuṇṇadvādasavassā bhikkhunī saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ yācāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, I, the nun so-and-so who has twelve years of seniority, ask the Sangha for approval to give the full admission.’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she should ask a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

segments.pali: Sā bhikkhunī saṅghena paricchinditabbā— ‘byattāyaṁ bhikkhunī lajjinī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha should then decide whether that nun is competent and has a sense of conscience.

segments.pali: Sace bālā ca hoti alajjinī ca, na dātabbā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is ignorant and shameless, approval should not be given.

segments.pali: Sace bālā hoti lajjinī, na dātabbā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is ignorant but has a sense of conscience, approval should not be given.

segments.pali: Sace byattā hoti alajjinī, na dātabbā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is competent but shameless, approval should not be given.

segments.pali: Sace byattā ca hoti lajjinī ca, dātabbā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is competent and has a sense of conscience, approval should be given.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And it is to be given like this.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā paripuṇṇadvādasavassā bhikkhunī saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so, who has twelve years of seniority, is asking the Sangha for approval to give the full admission.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya bhikkhuniyā vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ dadeyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should give approval to the nun so-and-so, who has twelve years of seniority, to give the full admission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā paripuṇṇadvādasavassā bhikkhunī saṅghaṁ vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so, who has twelve years of seniority, is asking the Sangha for approval to give the full admission.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya bhikkhuniyā vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ deti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha gives approval to the nun so-and-so, who has twelve years of seniority, to give the full admission.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya bhikkhuniyā vuṭṭhāpanasammutiyā dānaṁ, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of giving approval to the nun so-and-so, who has twelve years of seniority, to give the full admission should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

segments.pali: Dinnā saṅghena itthannāmāya paripuṇṇadvādasavassāya bhikkhuniyā vuṭṭhāpanasammuti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has given approval to the nun so-and-so, who has twelve years of seniority, to give the full admission.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā tā bhikkhuniyo anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, after rebuking those nuns in many ways, the Buddha spoke in dispraise of being difficult to support …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī paripuṇṇadvādasavassā saṅghena asammatā vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun who has twelve years of seniority gives the full admission without approval from the Sangha, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has twelve years of seniority:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has reached twelve years of seniority.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without approval:

segments.pali: ñattidutiyena kammena vuṭṭhāpanasammuti na dinnā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: approval to give the full admission has not been given through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she gives the full admission, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has twelve years of seniority, has been approved by the Sangha, and then gives the full admission;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 76. Agreeing to stop ordaining, but then criticizing it

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ yācati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Caṇḍakāḷī approached the Sangha of nuns and asked for approval to give the full admission.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhikkhunisaṅgho caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ paricchinditvā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha of nuns

segments.pali: “alaṁ tāva te, ayye, vuṭṭhāpitenā”ti, vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ na adāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: decided that she should not,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and Caṇḍakāḷī consented.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhunisaṅgho aññāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ deti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the Sangha of nuns gave approval to other nuns to give the full admission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Caṇḍakāḷī complained and criticized it,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “So it seems I’m ignorant and shameless,

segments.pali: yaṁ saṅgho aññāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ vuṭṭhāpanasammutiṁ deti, mayhameva na detī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: since the Sangha gives approval to other nuns, but not to me.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Caṇḍakāḷī

segments.pali: ‘alaṁ tāva te, ayye, vuṭṭhāpitenā’ti vuccamānā ‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: consent to not being approved to give the full admission, and then criticize it afterwards?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī ‘alaṁ tāva te, ayye, vuṭṭhāpitenā’ti vuccamānā ‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Caṇḍakāḷī

segments.pali: ‘alaṁ tāva te, ayye, vuṭṭhāpitenā’ti vuccamānā ‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī—‘alaṁ tāva te, ayye, vuṭṭhāpitenā’ti vuccamānā ‘sādhū’ti paṭissuṇitvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun is told, “Venerable, you’ve given enough full admissions for now,” and she consents, saying, “Fine,” but then criticizes it afterwards, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, you’ve given enough full admissions for now”:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerable, you’ve given enough full ordinations for now.

segments.pali: “Sādhū”ti paṭissuṇitvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she consents, saying, “Fine,” but then criticizes it afterwards, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: pakatiyā chandā dosā mohā bhayā karontaṁ khiyyati,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she criticizes one who regularly acts out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, or fear;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 77. Not ordaining a trainee after promising

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā sikkhamānā thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ upasaṅkamitvā upasampadaṁ yāci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a trainee nun went to the nun Thullanandā and asked her for the full ordination.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā told her,

segments.pali: “sace me tvaṁ, ayye, cīvaraṁ dassasi evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti vatvā, neva vuṭṭhāpeti na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If you give me a robe, venerable, I’ll give you the full admission.” But she neither gave her the full admission nor made any effort to have her fully admitted.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā sikkhamānā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That trainee nun told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā say that to a trainee nun

segments.pali: ‘sace me tvaṁ, ayye, cīvaraṁ dassasi evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī’ti vatvā, neva vuṭṭhāpessati na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and then neither give her the full admission nor make any effort to have her fully admitted?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

segments.pali: ‘sace me tvaṁ, ayye, cīvaraṁ dassasi evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī’ti vatvā, neva vuṭṭhāpeti na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karotī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī sikkhamānaṁ—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā act in this way?

segments.pali: ‘sace me tvaṁ, ayye, cīvaraṁ dassasi evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī’ti vatvā, neva vuṭṭhāpessati na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī sikkhamānaṁ—‘sace me tvaṁ, ayye, cīvaraṁ dassasi, evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī’ti vatvā, sā pacchā anantarāyikinī neva vuṭṭhāpeyya na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ kareyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun tells a trainee nun, “If you give me a robe, venerable, I’ll give you the full admission,” but she then neither gives her the full admission nor makes any effort to have her fully admitted, then, if there were no obstacles, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A trainee nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who has trained for two years in the six rules.

segments.pali: Sace me tvaṁ, ayye, cīvaraṁ dassasi evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmīti

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If you give me a robe, venerable, I’ll give you the full admission”:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I’ll give you the full ordination.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, if there were no obstacles:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: when there is no obstacle.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She neither gives her the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: does not herself give her the full admission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nor makes any effort to have her fully admitted:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: does not ask anyone else to give her the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Neva vuṭṭhāpessāmi na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karissāmī”ti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she thinks, “I’ll neither give her the full admission nor make any effort to have her fully admitted,” then by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an obstacle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she searches for someone to give her the full ordination, but is unable to find anyone;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh training rule is finished.

english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 79. Ordaining one who is difficult to live with

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī purisasaṁsaṭṭhaṁ kumārakasaṁsaṭṭhaṁ caṇḍiṁ sokāvāsaṁ caṇḍakāḷiṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā gave the full admission to the trainee nun Caṇḍakāḷī, who was socializing with men and boys, and who was temperamental and difficult to live with.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī purisasaṁsaṭṭhaṁ kumārakasaṁsaṭṭhaṁ caṇḍiṁ sokāvāsaṁ caṇḍakāḷiṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī purisasaṁsaṭṭhaṁ kumārakasaṁsaṭṭhaṁ caṇḍiṁ sokāvāsaṁ caṇḍakāḷiṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī purisasaṁsaṭṭhaṁ kumārakasaṁsaṭṭhaṁ caṇḍiṁ sokāvāsaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a trainee nun who is socializing with men and boys and who is temperamental and difficult to live with, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Men:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: those who have reached twenty years of age.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Boys:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: those who have not reached twenty years of age.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Socializing:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she socializes with improper bodily and verbal actions.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Temperamental:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: angry is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Difficult to live with:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she causes suffering to others, and grieves herself.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A trainee nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who has trained for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives her the full admission without knowing what she is like;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 80. Ordaining without permission from family

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī mātāpitūhipi sāmikenapi ananuññātaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā gave the full admission to a trainee nun who had neither been given permission by her parents nor by her husband.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā amhehi ananuññātaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā give the full admission to that trainee nun without our permission?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo mātāpitūnampi sāmikassapi ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of the parents and the husband.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā mātāpitūhipi sāmikenapi ananuññātaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā give the full admission to a trainee nun who has neither permission from her parents nor her husband?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī, mātāpitūhipi sāmikenapi ananuññātaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī mātāpitūhipi sāmikenapi ananuññātaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī mātāpitūhi vā sāmikena vā ananuññātaṁ sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives the full admission to a trainee nun who has not been given permission by her parents or her husband, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Parents:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the biological parents is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Husband:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he who possesses her.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not been given permission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has not asked permission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A trainee nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who has trained for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives her the full admission without knowing;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives her the full admission after permission has been given;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The tenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 81. Ordaining after consent expires

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Thullanandā

segments.pali: “sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti there bhikkhū sannipātetvā pahūtaṁ khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ passitvā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: had gathered a group of senior monks, intending to give the full admission to a trainee nun. But after seeing much fresh and cooked food, she dismissed the senior monks, saying,

segments.pali: “na tāvāhaṁ, ayyā, sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti there bhikkhū uyyojetvā devadattaṁ kokālikaṁ kaṭamodakatissakaṁ khaṇḍadeviyā puttaṁ samuddadattaṁ sannipātetvā sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, I won’t give the full admission to the trainee nun just yet.” She then gathered Devadatta, Kokālika, Kaṭamodakatissaka, Khaṇḍadeviyāputta, and Samuddadatta, and gave the full admission to that trainee nun.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā pārivāsikachandadānena sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā give the full admission to a trainee nun when the given consent had expired?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī pārivāsikachandadānena sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī pārivāsikachandadānena sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī pārivāsikachandadānena sikkhamānaṁ vuṭṭhāpeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If, when a given consent has expired, a nun gives the full admission to a trainee nun, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When a given consent has expired:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: when the gathering has left.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A trainee nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who has trained for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives her the full admission while the gathering has not yet left;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eleventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 82. Ordaining every year

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo anuvassaṁ vuṭṭhāpenti, upassayo na sammati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns were giving full admission every year, and the nunnery did not have sufficient capacity.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo anuvassaṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti, upassayo na sammatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns give full admission every year, when the nunnery doesn’t have sufficient capacity?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns give full admission every year?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo anuvassaṁ vuṭṭhāpessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun gives full admission every year, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Every year:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: every twelve-month period.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives full admission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives full ordination.

segments.pali: “Vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing confession,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives full admission every other year;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The twelfth training rule is finished.

english.text: “How can nuns do this?

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In the same year:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in one twelve-month period.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Gives the full admission to two women:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: gives the full ordination to two women.

segments.pali: “Dve vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission to two women, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 84. Using sunshades and sandals

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo chattupāhanaṁ dhārenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns from the group of six used sunshades and sandals.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo chattupāhanaṁ dhāressanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns use sunshades and sandals? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six use sunshades and sandals?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo chattupāhanaṁ dhārentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo chattupāhanaṁ dhāressanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun uses a sunshade and sandals, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī gilānā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards there was a sick nun

segments.pali: Tassā vinā chattupāhanaṁ na phāsu hoti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who was not comfortable without a sunshade and sandals. …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the Buddha. The Buddha then had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, I allow a sick nun to use a sunshade and sandals.

segments.pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī agilānā chattupāhanaṁ dhāreyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun who is not sick uses a sunshade and sandals, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is not sick:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who is comfortable without a sunshade and sandals.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is sick:

segments.pali: yassā vinā chattupāhanaṁ na phāsu hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who is not comfortable without a sunshade and sandals.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A sunshade:

segments.pali: tīṇi chattāni— setacchattaṁ, kilañjacchattaṁ, paṇṇacchattaṁ maṇḍalabaddhaṁ salākabaddhaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are three kinds of sunshades: the white sunshade, the reed sunshade, the leaf sunshade. They are bound at the rim and bound at the ribs.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Uses:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses them even once, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Agilānā agilānasaññā chattupāhanaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, and she does not perceive herself as sick, and she uses a sunshade and sandals, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Agilānā vematikā chattupāhanaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, but she is unsure of it, and she uses a sunshade and sandals, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Agilānā gilānasaññā chattupāhanaṁ dhāreti, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, but she perceives herself as sick, and she uses a sunshade and sandals, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Chattaṁ dhāreti na upāhanaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she uses a sunshade, but not sandals, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Upāhanaṁ dhāreti na chattaṁ, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she uses sandals, but not a sunshade, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, but she does not perceive herself as sick, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, and she perceives herself as sick, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she uses them in a monastery or in the vicinity of a monastery;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule is finished.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo yānena yāyanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns from the group of six were traveling in vehicles.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo yānena yāyissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.text: “How can the nuns travel in a vehicle? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six travel in a vehicle?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo yānena yāyantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo yānena yāyissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun travels in a vehicle, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī gilānā hoti, na sakkoti padasā gantuṁ …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards there was a sick nun who was not able to travel on foot. …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the Buddha. The Buddha then had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, I allow a sick nun to use a vehicle.

segments.pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī agilānā yānena yāyeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun who is not sick travels in a vehicle, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is not sick:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who is able to travel on foot.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is sick:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who is unable to travel on foot.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A vehicle:

segments.pali: vayhaṁ ratho sakaṭaṁ sandamānikā sivikā pāṭaṅkī.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a wagon, a carriage, a cart, a chariot, a palanquin, a litter.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Travels:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she travels in a vehicle even once, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Agilānā agilānasaññā yānena yāyati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, and she does not perceive herself as sick, and she travels in a vehicle, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Agilānā vematikā yānena yāyati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali

segments.pali: Agilānā gilānasaññā yānena yāyati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, but she perceives herself as sick, and she travels in a vehicle, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, but she does not perceive herself as sick, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, and she perceives herself as sick, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 86. Wearing a hip ornament

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī aññatarissā itthiyā kulūpikā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there was a nun was associating with the family of a certain woman.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā itthī taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That woman said to that nun,

segments.pali: “handāyye, imaṁ saṅghāṇiṁ amukāya nāma itthiyā dehī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, please give this hip ornament to such-and-such a woman.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun thought,

segments.pali: “sacāhaṁ pattena ādāya gacchāmi vissaro me bhavissatī”ti paṭimuñcitvā agamāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If I carry it in my almsbowl, I’ll get into trouble,” and so she put it on and then left.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: While she was walking along a street the threads snapped and were scattered all over.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo saṅghāṇiṁ dhāressanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns wear hip ornaments? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun wear a hip ornament?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī saṅghāṇiṁ dhāressati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun wears a hip ornament, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A hip ornament:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whatever goes on the hip.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Wears:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she wears it even once, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she wears it because she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she wears a girdle;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third training rule is finished.

english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.text: Jewelry:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: what goes on the head, what goes around the neck, what goes on the hands, what goes on the feet, what goes around the hips.

english.author: brahmali

english.text: if she wears it even once, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo gandhavaṇṇakena nahāyissanti.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any kind of scent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Colors:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any kind of color.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Bathes:

segments.pali: nahāyati. Payoge dukkaṭaṁ, nahānapariyosāne āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.text: 89. Bathing with sesame paste

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo vāsitakena piññākena nahāyanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns from the group of six were bathing with scented sesame paste.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo vāsitakena piññākena nahāyissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns bathe with scented sesame paste? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo vāsitakena piññākena nahāyissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six bathe with scented sesame paste?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo vāsitakena piññākena nahāyantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo vāsitakena piññākena nahāyissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī vāsitakena piññākena nahāyeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun bathes with scented sesame paste, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Scented:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any kind of scent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sesame paste:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ground sesame is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Bathes:

segments.pali: nahāyati. Payoge dukkaṭaṁ, nahānapariyosāne āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: is bathing. For the effort there is an act of wrong conduct. At the end of the bath, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does it because she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she bathes with unscented sesame paste;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth training rule is finished.
