english.author: brahmali
english.text: 90. Getting a massage from a nun

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo bhikkhuniyā ummaddāpentipi parimaddāpentipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns were having a nun massage and rub them.

segments.pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo bhikkhuniyā ummaddāpessantipi parimaddāpessantipi, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns get a nun to massage and rub them? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns get a nun to massage and rub them?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhikkhuniyā ummaddāpentipi parimaddāpentipī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhikkhuniyā ummaddāpessantipi parimaddāpessantipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhuniyā ummaddāpeyya vā parimaddāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun has a nun massage her or rub her, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: another nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has a nun massage her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets her to massage her, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Or if she has a nun rub her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets her to rub her, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo sikkhamānāya ummaddāpentipi parimaddāpentipi …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns were having a trainee nun …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … a novice nun …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … a female householder massage and rub them.

segments.pali: Manussā vihāracārikaṁ āhiṇḍantā passitvā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.text: When people walking about the dwellings saw this, they complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo gihiniyā ummaddāpessantipi parimaddāpessantipi, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns get a female householder to massage and rub them? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe…  tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns get a female householder to massage and rub them?”

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo gihiniyā ummaddāpentipi parimaddāpentipī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo gihiniyā ummaddāpessantipi parimaddāpessantipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī (sikkhamānāya …pe…  sāmaṇeriyā …pe… ) gihiniyā ummaddāpeyya vā parimaddāpeyya vā, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun has (a trainee nun … a novice nun …) a female householder massage her or rub her, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A trainee nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a female who is training for two years in the six rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A novice nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a female training in the ten training rules.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A female householder:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a female who lives in a house is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has her massage her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets her to massage her, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has her rub her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets her to rub her, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The tenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 94. Sitting in front without permission

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo bhikkhussa purato anāpucchā āsane nisīdanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: nuns sat down on seats in front of a monk without asking permission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The monks complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo bhikkhussa purato anāpucchā āsane nisīdissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns sit down on seats in front of a monk without asking permission?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhikkhussa purato anāpucchā āsane nisīdantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo bhikkhussa purato anāpucchā āsane nisīdissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī bhikkhussa purato anāpucchā āsane nisīdeyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun sits down on a seat in front of a monk without asking permission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In front of a monk:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in front of one who is fully ordained.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without asking permission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: without getting permission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sits down on a seat:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: even if she sits down on the ground, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite anāpucchitasaññā āsane nisīdati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, and she does not perceive that she has, and she sits down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite vematikā āsane nisīdati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, but she is unsure of it, and she sits down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite āpucchitasaññā āsane nisīdati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, but she perceives that she has, and she sits down on a seat, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, but she does not perceive that she has, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, and she perceives that she has, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she sits down on the seat after asking permission;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eleventh training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 95. Asking a question without permission

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo anokāsakataṁ bhikkhuṁ pañhaṁ pucchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns asked questions of a monk who had not given them permission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The monks complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo anokāsakataṁ bhikkhuṁ pañhaṁ pucchissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns ask questions of a monk who hasn’t given them permission?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo anokāsakataṁ bhikkhuṁ pañhaṁ pucchantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo anokāsakataṁ bhikkhuṁ pañhaṁ pucchissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī anokāsakataṁ bhikkhuṁ pañhaṁ puccheyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun asks a question of a monk who has not given her permission, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not given her permission:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: without asking permission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monk:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one who is fully ordained.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Asks a question:

segments.pali: suttante okāsaṁ kārāpetvā vinayaṁ vā abhidhammaṁ vā pucchati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets permission to ask about the discourses, but she asks about the Monastic Law or philosophy, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Vinaye okāsaṁ kārāpetvā suttantaṁ vā abhidhammaṁ vā pucchati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she gets permission to ask about the Monastic Law, but she asks about the discourses or philosophy, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Abhidhamme okāsaṁ kārāpetvā suttantaṁ vā vinayaṁ vā pucchati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she gets permission to ask about philosophy, but she asks about the discourses or the Monastic Law, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite anāpucchitasaññā pañhaṁ pucchati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, and she does not perceive that she has, and she asks a question, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite vematikā pañhaṁ pucchati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, but she is unsure of it, and she asks a question, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Anāpucchite āpucchitasaññā pañhaṁ pucchati, āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has not asked permission, but she perceives that she has, and she asks a question, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, but she does not perceive that she has, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she has asked permission, and she perceives that she has, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asks after getting permission;

segments.pali: anodissa okāsaṁ kārāpetvā yattha katthaci pucchati,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gets permission, but not in regard to a specific subject, and she then asks about any subject;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The twelfth training rule is finished.

english.text: The subchapter on sunshades and sandals

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 96. Not wearing a chest wrap

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī asaṅkaccikā gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun had gone to the village for alms without wearing her chest wrap.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: While she was walking along a street, a whirlwind lifted up her upper robes.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People shouted out,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “She has beautiful breasts and belly!”

segments.pali: Sā bhikkhunī tehi manussehi uppaṇḍiyamānā maṅku ahosi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because she was teased by those people, she felt humiliated.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī upassayaṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After returning to the nunnery, she told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun enter an inhabited area without wearing her chest wrap?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī asaṅkaccikā gāmaṁ pāvisī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī asaṅkaccikā gāmaṁ pavisissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī asaṅkaccikā gāmaṁ paviseyya, pācittiyan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun enters an inhabited area without wearing her chest wrap, she commits an offense entailing confession.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without wearing her chest wrap:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: not wearing her chest wrap.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Chest wrap:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it is for the purpose of concealing the body below the collar bone and above the navel.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Enters an inhabited area:

segments.pali: parikkhittassa gāmassa parikkhepaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she crosses the boundary of an enclosed inhabited area, she commits an offense entailing confession.

segments.pali: Aparikkhittassa gāmassa upacāraṁ okkamantiyā āpatti pācittiyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she enters the vicinity of an unenclosed inhabited area, she commits an offense entailing confession.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if her robe is stolen;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if her robe is lost;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is not mindful;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not know;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The thirteenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The ninth subchapter on sunshades and sandals is finished.

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, chasaṭṭhisatā pācittiyā dhammā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, the one hundred and sixty-six rules on confession have been recited.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The section on minor rules has been completed.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing confession in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing acknowledgment

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 1. The training rule on asking for ghee

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo aṭṭha pāṭidesanīyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these eight rules on acknowledgment come up for recitation.

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns from the group of six were eating ghee that they had asked for.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns eat ghee that they have asked for?

segments.pali: Kassa sampannaṁ na manāpaṁ, kassa sāduṁ na ruccatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who doesn’t like nice food? Who doesn’t prefer tasty food?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjissantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six eat ghee that they have asked for?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā, bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjantī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, paṭidesetabbaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun asks for ghee and then eats it, she must acknowledge it:

segments.pali: ‘gārayhaṁ, ayye, dhammaṁ āpajjiṁ asappāyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ, taṁ paṭidesemī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I have done a blameworthy and unsuitable thing that is to be acknowledged. I acknowledge it.”’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhuniyo gilānā honti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards a number of nuns were sick.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns who were looking after them asked,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I hope you’re bearing up? I hope you’re getting better?”

segments.pali: “Pubbe mayaṁ, ayye, sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjāma, tena no phāsu hoti;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Previously we ate ghee that we had asked for, and then we were comfortable.

segments.pali: idāni pana ‘bhagavatā paṭikkhittan’ti kukkuccāyantā na viññāpema, tena no na phāsu hotī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But now that the Buddha has prohibited this, we don’t ask because we’re afraid of wrongdoing. And because of that we’re not comfortable.” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the Buddha. Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānāya bhikkhuniyā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjituṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, I allow a sick nun to eat ghee that she has asked for.

segments.pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī agilānā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, paṭidesetabbaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun who is not sick asks for ghee and then eats it, she must acknowledge it:

segments.pali: ‘gārayhaṁ, ayye, dhammaṁ āpajjiṁ asappāyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ taṁ paṭidesemī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I have done a blameworthy and unsuitable thing that is to be acknowledged. I acknowledge it.”’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is not sick:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who is comfortable without ghee.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is sick:

segments.pali: yassā vinā sappinā na phāsu hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who is not comfortable without ghee.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Ghee:

segments.pali: gosappi vā ajikāsappi vā mahiṁsasappi vā. Yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati tesaṁ sappi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ghee from cows, ghee from goats, ghee from buffaloes, or ghee from whatever animal whose meat is allowable.

segments.pali: Agilānā attano atthāya viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick and she asks for herself, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment.

segments.pali: Agilānā agilānasaññā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, and she does not perceive herself as sick, and she eats ghee that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment.

segments.pali: Agilānā vematikā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, but she is unsure of it, and she eats ghee that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment.

segments.pali: Agilānā gilānasaññā sappiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, but she perceives herself as sick, and she eats ghee that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, but she does not perceive herself as sick, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, and she perceives herself as sick, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asked for it when she was sick, but eats it when she is no longer sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she eats the leftovers from one who is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is from relatives;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is from those who have given an invitation;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is for the benefit of someone else;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is by means of her own property;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first training rule on acknowledgment is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

segments.pali: dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjeyya, paṭidesetabbaṁ tāya bhikkhuniyā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: curd and then eats it, she must acknowledge it:

segments.pali: ‘gārayhaṁ, ayye, dhammaṁ āpajjiṁ asappāyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ, taṁ paṭidesemī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I have done a blameworthy and unsuitable thing that is to be acknowledged. I acknowledge it.”’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

segments.pali: … pe … ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is not sick:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who is comfortable without curd.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is sick:

segments.pali: yassā vinā dadhinā na phāsu hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who is not comfortable without curd.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Oil:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: sesame oil, mustard oil, honey tree oil, castor oil, oil from tallow.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Honey:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: honey from bees.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Syrup:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: from sugarcane.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Fish:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: what lives in water is what is meant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Meat:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the meat of those animals whose meat is allowable.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Milk:

segments.pali: gokhīraṁ vā ajikākhīraṁ vā mahiṁsakhīraṁ vā yesaṁ maṁsaṁ kappati tesaṁ khīraṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: milk from cows, milk from goats, milk from buffaloes, or milk from whatever animal whose meat is allowable.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Curd:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: curd from those same animals.

segments.pali: Agilānā attano atthāya viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick and she asks for herself, then for the effort there is an act of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment.

segments.pali: Agilānā agilānasaññā dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, and she does not perceive herself as sick, and she eats curd that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment.

segments.pali: Agilānā vematikā dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, but she is unsure of it, and she eats curd that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment.

segments.pali: Agilānā gilānasaññā dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not sick, but she perceives herself as sick, and she eats curd that she has asked for, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, but she does not perceive herself as sick, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is sick, and she perceives herself as sick, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she asked for it when she was sick, but eats it when she is no longer sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she eats the leftovers from one who is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is from relatives;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is from those who have given an invitation;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is for the benefit of someone else;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is by means of her own property;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth training rule entailing acknowledgment is finished.

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, aṭṭha pāṭidesanīyā dhammā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, the eight rules on acknowledgment have been recited.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing acknowledgment in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 5. The training rule on physical contact with a man

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Homage to the Buddha, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāḷho migāranattā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihāraṁ kattukāmo hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Migāra’s grandson Sāḷha wanted to build a dwelling for the Sangha of nuns.

segments.pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He went to the nuns and said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, I wish to build a dwelling for the Sangha of nuns.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please get me the nun in charge of building work.”

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena catasso bhaginiyo bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā honti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time four sisters had gone forth as nuns:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nandā, Nandavatī, Sundarīnandā, and Thullanandā.

segments.pali: Tāsu sundarīnandā bhikkhunī taruṇapabbajitā abhirūpā hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā paṇḍitā byattā medhāvinī dakkhā analasā, tatrupāyāya vīmaṁsāya samannāgatā, alaṁ kātuṁ alaṁ saṁvidhātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Of these, the nun Sundarīnandā had gone forth when she was young, and she was beautiful, intelligent, skilled, and diligent, and she possessed good judgment in regard to doing and arranging things well.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhikkhunisaṅgho sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ sammannitvā sāḷhassa migāranattuno navakammikaṁ adāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After appointing Sundarīnandā to be in charge of building work, the Sangha made her work with Sāḷha.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī sāḷhassa migāranattuno nivesanaṁ abhikkhaṇaṁ gacchati—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: As a consequence of this, Sundarīnandā would often go to Sāḷha’s house to ask for tools,

segments.pali: “vāsiṁ detha, parasuṁ detha, kuṭhāriṁ detha, kudālaṁ detha, nikhādanaṁ dethā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whether a machete, a hatchet, an ax, a spade, or a chisel.

segments.pali: Sāḷhopi migāranattā bhikkhunupassayaṁ abhikkhaṇaṁ gacchati katākataṁ jānituṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And Sāḷha would often go to the nunnery to find out about the progress of the building work.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And because they saw each other frequently, they fell in love.

segments.pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dūsetuṁ okāsaṁ alabhamāno etadevaatthāya bhikkhunisaṅghassa bhattaṁ akāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But because Sāḷha could not find any opportunity to be intimate with Sundarīnandā, he invited the Sangha of nuns for a meal.

segments.pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā bhattagge āsanaṁ paññapento “ettakā bhikkhuniyo ayyāya sundarīnandāya vuḍḍhatarā”ti—ekamantaṁ āsanaṁ paññapesi “ettakā navakatarā”ti—ekamantaṁ āsanaṁ paññapesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When preparing the seats in the dining hall, he counted the number of nuns senior to Venerable Sundarīnandā and placed their seats to one side, and he counted the number of nuns junior to her and placed their seats on the other side.

segments.pali: Paṭicchanne okāse nikūṭe sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā āsanaṁ paññapesi, yathā therā bhikkhuniyo jāneyyuṁ—“navakānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ santike nisinnā”ti; navakāpi bhikkhuniyo jāneyyuṁ—“therānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ santike nisinnā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He then placed Sundarīnandā’s seat in a concealed spot in a corner. In this way the senior nuns would think she was seated close to the junior ones and the junior nuns would think she was seated close to the senior ones.

segments.pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā bhikkhunisaṅghassa kālaṁ ārocāpesi—“kālo, ayye, niṭṭhitaṁ bhattan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards he informed the Sangha that the meal was ready.

segments.pali: Sundarīnandā bhikkhunī sallakkhetvā—“na bahukato sāḷho migāranattā bhikkhunisaṅghassa bhattaṁ akāsi; maṁ so dūsetukāmo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sundarīnandā thought, “Sāḷha hasn’t prepared a meal for the Sangha as a service, but because he wants to be intimate with me.

segments.pali: Sacāhaṁ gamissāmi vissaro me bhavissatī”ti, antevāsiniṁ bhikkhuniṁ āṇāpesi—“gaccha me piṇḍapātaṁ nīhara.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If I go, I will get into trouble.” She then told a nun who was her pupil, “Go and bring back almsfood for me.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If anyone asks about me, tell them I’m sick.”

segments.pali: “Evaṁ, ayye”ti kho sā bhikkhunī sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā paccassosi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Yes, venerable,” she replied.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sāḷho migāranattā bahidvārakoṭṭhake ṭhito hoti sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ paṭipucchanto—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards Sāḷha was standing outside his gatehouse repeatedly inquiring after Sundarīnandā:

segments.pali: “kahaṁ, ayye, ayyā sundarīnandā? Kahaṁ, ayye, ayyā sundarīnandā”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, where’s Venerable Sundarīnandā?”

segments.pali: Evaṁ vutte, sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā antevāsinī bhikkhunī sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ etadavoca—“gilānāvuso; piṇḍapātaṁ nīharissāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun who was Sundarīnandā’s pupil told him: “She’s sick. I’m bringing her almsfood.”

segments.pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā—“yampāhaṁ atthāya bhikkhunisaṅghassa bhattaṁ akāsiṁ ayyāya sundarīnandāya kāraṇā”ti manusse āṇāpetvā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sāḷha thought, “I invited the Sangha of nuns because of Sundarīnandā,”

segments.pali: “bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ bhattena parivisathā”ti vatvā yena bhikkhunupassayo tenupasaṅkami.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and after telling the people there to serve the meal to the Sangha of nuns, he left for the nunnery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī bahārāmakoṭṭhake ṭhitā hoti sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ patimānentī.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then Sundarīnandā was standing outside the monastery gatehouse longing for Sāḷha.

segments.pali: Addasā kho sundarīnandā bhikkhunī sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ. Disvāna upassayaṁ pavisitvā sasīsaṁ pārupitvā mañcake nipajji.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she saw him coming, she entered the nunnery, put on her upper robe so that it covered her head, and lay down on her bed.

segments.pali: Atha kho sāḷho migāranattā yena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca—“kiṁ te, ayye, aphāsu, kissa nipannāsī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sāḷha approached her and said, “Venerable, what’s wrong with you? Why are you lying down?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “That’s what happens when you desire someone who doesn’t desire you in return.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “What do you mean I don’t desire you?

segments.pali: Api cāhaṁ okāsaṁ na labhāmi taṁ dūsetun”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: I just couldn’t find an opportunity to be intimate with you.”

segments.pali: Avassuto avassutāya sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajji.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And both having lust, he made physical contact with Sundarīnandā.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarā bhikkhunī jarādubbalā caraṇagilānā sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā avidūre nipannā hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then a nun who was weak from old age and had problems with her feet was lying down not far from Sundarīnandā.

segments.pali: Addasā kho sā bhikkhunī sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ avassutaṁ avassutāya sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjantaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She saw how Sāḷha made physical contact with Sundarīnandā while both of them had lust.

segments.pali: Disvāna ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—“kathañhi nāma ayyā sundarīnandā avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She complained and criticized her, “How could Venerable Sundarīnandā consent to a man making physical contact with her, when they both had lust?”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun then told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā santuṭṭhā lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires, who had a sense of conscience, and who were contented, afraid of wrongdoing, and fond of the training, complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā sundarīnandā avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Sundarīnandā consent to a man making physical contact with her, when they both had lust?”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ. Ye te bhikkhū appicchā santuṭṭhā lajjino kukkuccakā sikkhākāmā te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—“kathañhi nāma sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those nuns then told the monks. And the monks of few desires, who had a sense of conscience, and who were contented, afraid of wrongdoing, and fond of the training, criticized her in the same way.

segments.pali: Atha kho te bhikkhū sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After rebuking the nun Sundarīnandā in many ways, they told the Buddha.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā bhikkhū paṭipucchi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks:

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Sundarīnandā acted like this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her,

segments.pali: “ananucchavikaṁ, bhikkhave, sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s not suitable, monks, it’s not proper for the nun Sundarīnandā, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done.

segments.pali: Kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: How could Sundarīnandā consent to a man making physical contact with her, when they both had lust?

segments.pali: Netaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānaṁ vā pasādāya pasannānaṁ vā bhiyyobhāvāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence,

segments.pali: Atha khvetaṁ, bhikkhave, appasannānañceva appasādāya pasannānañca ekaccānaṁ aññathattāyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and cause some to lose it.”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya dupposatāya mahicchatāya asantuṭṭhitāya saṅgaṇikāya kosajjassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitvā, anekapariyāyena subharatāya suposatāya appicchassa santuṭṭhassa sallekhassa dhutassa pāsādikassa apacayassa vīriyārambhassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitvā, bhikkhūnaṁ tadanucchavikaṁ tadanulomikaṁ dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And the Buddha spoke in many ways in dispraise of being difficult to support and maintain, in dispraise of great desires, discontent, socializing, and laziness; but he spoke in many ways in praise of being easy to support and maintain, of fewness of wishes, contentment, self-effacement, ascetic practices, serenity, reduction in things, and being energetic. After giving a teaching on what is right and proper, he addressed the monks:

segments.pali: “tena hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmi dasa atthavase paṭicca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Well then, monks, I will lay down a training rule for the following ten reasons:

segments.pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya, dummaṅkūnaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya, samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya, appasannānaṁ pasādāya, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā vinayānuggahāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad nuns, for the ease of good nuns, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training.

segments.pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ āmasanaṁ vā parāmasanaṁ vā gahaṇaṁ vā chupanaṁ vā paṭipīḷanaṁ vā sādiyeyya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalikā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a lustful nun consents to a lustful man making physical contact with her, to touching her, to taking hold of her, to contacting her, or to squeezing her, anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on above the knees.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

segments.pali: yā yādisā yathāyuttā yathājaccā yathānāmā yathāgottā yathāsīlā yathāvihārinī yathāgocarā therā vā navā vā majjhimā vā, esā vuccati yā panāti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever, of such a kind, of such activity, of such caste, of such name, of such family, of such conduct, of such behavior, of such association, who is senior, who is junior, or who is of middle standing—this is called “a”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is a nun because she lives on alms;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun because she has gone over to living on alms;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun because she wears a patchwork cloth;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun by convention;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun on account of her claim;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a “come, nun” nun;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun given the full ordination by taking the three refuges;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a good nun;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun of substance;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a trainee nun;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a fully trained nun;

segments.pali: samaggena ubhatosaṅghena ñatticatutthena kammena akuppena ṭhānārahena upasampannāti bhikkhunī.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand.

segments.pali: Tatra yāyaṁ bhikkhunī samaggena ubhatosaṅghena ñatticatutthena kammena akuppena ṭhānārahena upasampannā, ayaṁ imasmiṁ atthe adhippetā bhikkhunīti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lustful:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: having lust, longing for, in love with.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lustful:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: having lust, longing for, in love with.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Man:

segments.pali: manussapuriso na yakkho na peto na tiracchānagato viññū paṭibalo kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjituṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of making physical contact.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Below the collar bone:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: down from the collar bone.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Above the knees:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: up from the knees.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Making physical contact:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: making mere physical contact.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Touching:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: touching here and there.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Taking hold of:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the mere taking hold of.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Contacting:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: mere contacting.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Consents to squeezing:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: consents to the taking hold of a bodily part and then pressing.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Is expelled:

segments.pali: seyyathāpi nāma puriso sīsacchinno abhabbo tena sarīrabandhanena jīvituṁ; evameva bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ āmasanaṁ vā parāmasanaṁ vā gahaṇaṁ vā chupanaṁ vā paṭipīḷanaṁ vā sādiyantī assamaṇī hoti asakyadhītā. Tena vuccati pārājikā hotīti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: just as a man with his head cut off is unable to continue living by reconnecting it to the body, so is a lustful nun who consents to a lustful man making physical contact with her, to touching her, to taking hold of her, to contacting her, or to squeezing her, anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, not a monastic, not a daughter of the Sakyan. Therefore it is said “she is expelled.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Excluded from the community:

segments.pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā— eso saṁvāso nāma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”.

segments.pali: So tāya saddhiṁ natthi, tena vuccati asaṁvāsāti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”.

segments.pali: Ubhatoavassute adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti pārājikassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Physical contact below the collar bone but above the knees, both having lust: (1) If he, with his own body,  makes physical contact with her body, she commits an offense entailing expulsion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (2) If he, with his own body, makes physical contact with something connected to her body, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (3) If he, with something connected to his own body, makes physical contact with her body, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (4) If he, with something connected to his own body, makes physical contact with something connected to her body, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (5) If he, with something released by him, makes physical contact with her body, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (6) If he, with something released by him, makes physical contact with something connected to her body, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (7) If he, with something released by him, makes physical contact with something released by her, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Ubbhakkhakaṁ adhojāṇumaṇḍalaṁ kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is unintentional;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is overwhelmed by pain;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth offense entailing expulsion is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 6. The training rule on concealing an expulsion offense

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī sāḷhena migāranattunā gabbhinī hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Sundarīnandā was pregnant by Migāra’s grandson Sāḷha.

segments.pali: Yāva gabbho taruṇo ahosi tāva chādesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the fetus got large, she concealed her condition.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And when the fetus was fully grown, she disrobed and gave birth.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns said to the nun Thullanandā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, Sundarīnandā gave birth shortly after disrobing.”

segments.pali: Kacci no sā bhikkhunīyeva samānā gabbhinī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Could it be that she was pregnant while she was still a nun?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Yes, venerables.”

segments.pali: “Kissa pana tvaṁ, ayye, jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodesi na gaṇassa ārocesī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “But, venerable, when you knew that a nun had committed an offense entailing expulsion, why didn’t you either confront her yourself or tell the community?”

segments.pali: “Yo etissā avaṇṇo mayheso avaṇṇo, yā etissā akitti mayhesā akitti, yo etissā ayaso mayheso ayaso, yo etissā alābho mayheso alābho.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Her disrepute is my disrepute, her infamy is my infamy, her notoriety is my notoriety, her loss is my loss.

segments.pali: Kyāhaṁ, ayye, attano avaṇṇaṁ attano akittiṁ attano ayasaṁ attano alābhaṁ paresaṁ ārocessāmī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Why would I tell others of my own disrepute, infamy, notoriety, and loss?”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodessati na gaṇassa ārocessatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā, knowing that a nun had committed an offense entailing expulsion, neither confront her herself nor tell the community?”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then those nuns told the monks what had happened,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the monks in turn told the Buddha.

segments.pali: Atha kho bhagavā etasmiṁ nidāne etasmiṁ pakaraṇe bhikkhusaṅghaṁ sannipātāpetvā bhikkhū paṭipucchi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks:

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodeti na gaṇassa ārocetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā acted like this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodessati na gaṇassa ārocessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā, knowing that a nun had committed an offense entailing expulsion, neither confront her herself nor tell the community?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodeyya na gaṇassa āroceyya, yadā ca sā ṭhitā vā assa cutā vā nāsitā vā avassaṭā vā, sā pacchā evaṁ vadeyya—‘pubbevāhaṁ, ayye, aññāsiṁ etaṁ bhikkhuniṁ evarūpā ca evarūpā ca sā bhaginīti, no ca kho attanā paṭicodessaṁ na gaṇassa ārocessan’ti, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā vajjappaṭicchādikā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun knows that a nun has committed an offense entailing expulsion, but she neither confronts her herself nor tells the community, and afterward—whether that nun remains or has died or has been expelled or has converted—she says, “Venerables, although I previously knew that this nun was like this, I thought, ‘I will neither confront her myself nor tell the community,’” she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on those who conceal offenses.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Knows:

segments.pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti, aññe vā tassā ārocenti, sā vā āroceti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she knows by herself or others have told her or she has told her.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has committed an offense entailing expulsion:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she has committed any one of the eight offenses entailing expulsion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She neither confronts her herself:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not herself accuse her.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nor tells the community:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she does not tell other nuns.

segments.pali: Yadā ca sā ṭhitā vā assa cutā vāti

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Whether that nun remains or has died:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Remains: what is meant is that she remains as a nun.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has died: what is meant is that she has passed away.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has been expelled:

segments.pali: sayaṁ vā vibbhantā hoti aññehi vā nāsitā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she has either disrobed herself or been expelled by others.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Has converted:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: what is meant is that she has joined another religious community.

segments.pali: Sā pacchā evaṁ vadeyya— “pubbevāhaṁ, ayye, aññāsiṁ etaṁ bhikkhuniṁ evarūpā ca evarūpā ca sā bhaginī”ti. No ca kho attanā paṭicodessanti

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Afterward she says, “Venerables, although I previously knew that this nun was like this, I thought, ‘I will neither confront her myself’”:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I won’t accuse her myself.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Nor tell the community”:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Nor tell other nuns.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Is expelled:

segments.pali: seyyathāpi nāma paṇḍupalāso bandhanā pamutto abhabbo haritatthāya, evameva bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodessāmi na gaṇassa ārocessāmīti dhuraṁ nikkhittamatte assamaṇī hoti asakyadhītā. Tena vuccati pārājikā hotīti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: just as a fallen, withered leaf is incapable of becoming green again, so is a nun who knows that a nun has committed an offense entailing expulsion, but who thinks, “I will neither confront her myself nor tell the community,” by the mere fact of abandoning her duty, not a monastic, not a daughter of the Sakyan. Therefore it is said “she is expelled.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Excluded from the community:

segments.pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā. Eso saṁvāso nāma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”.

segments.pali: So tāya saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati asaṁvāsāti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: “saṅghassa bhaṇḍanaṁ vā kalaho vā viggaho vā vivādo vā bhavissatī”ti nāroceti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not tell because she thinks there will be quarrels or disputes in the Sangha;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not tell because she thinks there will be a schism or fracture in the Sangha;

segments.pali: “ayaṁ kakkhaḷā pharusā jīvitantarāyaṁ vā brahmacariyantarāyaṁ vā karissatī”ti nāroceti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not tell because she thinks the person she is telling about is cruel and harsh and that she might become a threat to life or the monastic life;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not tell because she does not see any suitable nuns;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not tell, but not because she wants to conceal;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not tell because she thinks the other person will be known through her own actions;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is deranged;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is overwhelmed by pain;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth offense entailing expulsion is finished.
