english.author: brahmali
english.text: 7. The training rule on siding with an ejected monk

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ ariṭṭhaṁ bhikkhuṁ gaddhabādhipubbaṁ anuvattati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Thullanandā was taking sides with the monk Ariṭṭha, the ex-vulture-hunter, who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ ariṭṭhaṁ bhikkhuṁ gaddhabādhipubbaṁ anuvattissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can Venerable Thullanandā take sides with the monk Ariṭṭha who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ ariṭṭhaṁ bhikkhuṁ gaddhabādhipubbaṁ anuvattatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā is doing this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ ariṭṭhaṁ bhikkhuṁ gaddhabādhipubbaṁ anuvattissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nun Thullanandā take sides with the monk Ariṭṭha who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaraṁ appaṭikāraṁ akatasahāyaṁ tamanuvatteyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi evamassa vacanīyā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun takes sides with a monk who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha—in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—and who is disrespectful, who has not made amends, and who has not made friends, the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: ‘eso kho, ayye, bhikkhu samaggena saṅghena ukkhitto dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaro appaṭikāro akatasahāyo, māyye, etaṁ bhikkhuṁ anuvattī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, this monk has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. He’s disrespectful, hasn’t made amends, and hasn’t made friends. Venerable, don’t take sides with this monk.”

segments.pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsiyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then stops, all is well.

segments.pali: No ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā ukkhittānuvattikā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule on taking sides with one who has been ejected.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A unanimous Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: those belonging to the same Buddhist sect and staying within the same monastery zone.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has been ejected:

segments.pali: āpattiyā adassane vā appaṭikamme vā appaṭinissagge vā ukkhitto.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has been ejected for not recognizing an offense, for not making amends, or for not giving up a bad view.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in accordance with that Teaching, in accordance with that Monastic Law.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In accordance with the Teacher’s instruction:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in accordance with the Victor’s instruction, in accordance with the Buddha’s instruction.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who is disrespectful:

segments.pali: saṅghaṁ vā gaṇaṁ vā puggalaṁ vā kammaṁ vā nādiyati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he does not heed the Sangha, groups of monks, individual monks, or legal procedures.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not made amends:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: he has been ejected and not readmitted.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has not made friends:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: monks belonging to the same Buddhist sect is what is meant by “friends”.

segments.pali: So tehi saddhiṁ natthi, tena vuccati akatasahāyoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He is not together with them—therefore it is called “who has not made friends”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Takes sides with:

segments.pali: yaṁdiṭṭhiko so hoti yaṅkhantiko yaṁruciko, sāpi taṁdiṭṭhikā hoti taṅkhantikā taṁrucikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she has the same view, the same belief, the same persuasion as he does.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: that nun who supports one who has been ejected.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns:

segments.pali: aññāhi bhikkhunīhi. Yā passanti yā suṇanti tāhi vattabbā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct her like this:

segments.pali: “eso kho, ayye, bhikkhu samaggena saṅghena ukkhitto dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaro appaṭikāro akatasahāyo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, this monk has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. He’s disrespectful, hasn’t made amends, and hasn’t made friends.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerable, don’t take sides with this monk.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they should correct her a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun, even if she has to be pulled into the midst of the Sangha, should be corrected like this:

segments.pali: “eso kho, ayye, bhikkhu samaggena saṅghena ukkhitto dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaro appaṭikāro akatasahāyo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, this monk has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. He’s disrespectful, hasn’t made amends, and hasn’t made friends.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerable, don’t take sides with this monk.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They should correct her a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Should press her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, she should be pressed like this.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaraṁ appaṭikāraṁ akatasahāyaṁ tamanuvattati, sā taṁ vatthuṁ na paṭinissajjati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so is taking sides with a monk who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha—in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—and who is disrespectful, who has not made amends, and who has not made friends. And she keeps on doing it.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anādaraṁ appaṭikāraṁ akatasahāyaṁ tamanuvattati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so is taking sides with a monk who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha—in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—and who is disrespectful, who has not made amends, and who has not made friends.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she keeps on doing it.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

segments.pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing expulsion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Is expelled:

segments.pali: seyyathāpi nāma puthusilā dvedhā bhinnā appaṭisandhikā hoti, evameva bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjantī assamaṇī hoti asakyadhītā. Tena vuccati pārājikā hotīti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: just as an ordinary stone that has broken in half cannot be put together again, so is a nun who does not stop when pressed three times not a monastic, not a daughter of the Sakyan. Therefore it is said “she is expelled.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Excluded from the community:

segments.pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā. Eso saṁvāso nāma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”.

segments.pali: So tāya saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati asaṁvāsāti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pārājikassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, but she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing expulsion.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pārājikassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing expulsion.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pārājikassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing expulsion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has not been pressed;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stops;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is deranged;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is overwhelmed by pain;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh offense entailing expulsion is finished.

english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law
