english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 8. The training rule on behaving inappropriately in eight ways

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthaggahaṇampi sādiyanti, saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇampi sādiyanti, santiṭṭhantipi, sallapantipi, saṅketampi gacchanti, purisassapi abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyanti, channampi anupavisanti, kāyampi tadatthāya upasaṁharanti etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns from the group of six, being lustful and aiming to indulge in inappropriate sexual conduct, consented to lustful men holding their hands and the edge of their robes, and they stood with them, chatted with them, went to rendezvous with them, consented to men coming to them, entered covered places with them, and disposed their bodies for them for that purpose.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthaggahaṇampi sādiyissanti, saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇampi sādiyissanti, santiṭṭhissantipi, sallapissantipi, saṅketampi gacchissanti, purisassapi abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyissanti, channampi anupavisissanti, kāyampi tadatthāya upasaṁharissanti etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāyā”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do such things?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthaggahaṇampi sādiyanti, saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇampi sādiyanti, santiṭṭhantipi, sallapantipi, saṅketampi gacchanti, purisassapi abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyanti, channampi anupavisanti, kāyampi tadatthāya upasaṁharanti etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāyā”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do these things?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthaggahaṇampi sādiyissanti, saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇampi sādiyissanti, santiṭṭhissantipi, sallapissantipi, saṅketampi gacchissanti, purisassapi abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyissanti, channampi anupavisissanti, kāyampi tadatthāya upasaṁharissanti etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six, being lustful and aiming to indulge in inappropriate sexual conduct, consent to lustful men holding their hands and the edge of their robes, and how can they stand with them, chat with them, go to rendezvous with them, consent to men coming to them, enter covered places with them, and dispose their bodies for them for that purpose?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthaggahaṇaṁ vā sādiyeyya, saṅghāṭikaṇṇaggahaṇaṁ vā sādiyeyya, santiṭṭheyya vā, sallapeyya vā, saṅketaṁ vā gaccheyya, purisassa vā abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyeyya, channaṁ vā anupaviseyya, kāyaṁ vā tadatthāya upasaṁhareyya etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya, ayampi pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā aṭṭhavatthukā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, a lustful nun consents to a lustful man holding her hand and the edge of her robe, and she stands with him and chats with him and goes to a rendezvous with him and consents to him coming to her and enters a covered place with him and disposes her body for him for that purpose, she too is expelled and excluded from the community. The training rule having eight parts.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lustful:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: having lust, longing for, in love with.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lustful:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: having lust, longing for, in love with.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Man:

segments.pali: manussapuriso, na yakkho na peto na tiracchānagato viññū paṭibalo kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjituṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of making physical contact.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Consents to holding her hand:

segments.pali: hattho nāma kapparaṁ upādāya yāva agganakhā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: hand: from the elbow to the tip of the nails.

segments.pali: Etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya ubbhakkhakaṁ adhojāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she consents to him holding her above the collar bone or below the knees, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And the edge of her robe:

segments.pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya nivatthaṁ vā pārutaṁ vā gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she consents to him holding her sarong or upper robe, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And stands with him:

segments.pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisassa hatthapāse tiṭṭhati, āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she stands within arm’s reach of a man, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And chats with him:

segments.pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisassa hatthapāse ṭhitā sallapati, āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she stands within arm’s reach of a man, chatting with him, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And goes to a rendezvous with him:

segments.pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisena— “itthannāmaṁ okāsaṁ āgacchā”ti—vuttā gacchati. Pade pade āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she goes to such-and-such a place when told by a man to do so, then for every step, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For entering within arm’s reach of the man, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And consents to him coming to her:

segments.pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisassa abbhāgamanaṁ sādiyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she consents to a man coming to her, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When he enters within arm’s reach, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And enters a covered place with him:

segments.pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya yena kenaci paṭicchannaṁ okāsaṁ paviṭṭhamatte āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she enters a concealed place with any man, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And disposes her body for him for that purpose:

segments.pali: etassa asaddhammassa paṭisevanatthāya purisassa hatthapāse ṭhitā kāyaṁ upasaṁharati, āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, for the purpose of indulging in inappropriate sexual conduct, she disposes her body for a man while standing within arm’s reach of him, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Is expelled:

segments.pali: seyyathāpi nāma tālo matthakacchinno abhabbo puna viruḷhiyā evameva bhikkhunī aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūrentī assamaṇī hoti asakyadhītā. Tena vuccati pārājikā hotīti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: just as a palm tree with its crown cut off is incapable of further growth, so is a nun who fulfills the eight parts not a monastic, not a daughter of the Sakyan. Therefore it is said “she is expelled.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Excluded from the community:

segments.pali: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā. Eso saṁvāso nāma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”.

segments.pali: So tāya saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati asaṁvāsāti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is unintentional;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is not mindful;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not know;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not consent;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is deranged;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is overwhelmed by pain;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eighth offense entailing expulsion is finished.

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, aṭṭha pārājikā dhammā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, the eight rules on expulsion have been recited.

segments.pali: Yesaṁ bhikkhunī aññataraṁ vā aññataraṁ vā āpajjitvā na labhati bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁvāsaṁ, yathā pure tathā pacchā, pārājikā hoti asaṁvāsā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun commits any one of them, she is no longer part of the community of nuns. As before, so after: she’s expelled and excluded from the community.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on training

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on evenly all around

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 1. Not wearing the sarong evenly all around

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo sekhiyā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these rules of training come up for recitation.

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo puratopi pacchatopi olambentī nivāsenti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns from the group of six wore their sarongs hanging down in front and behind.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo puratopi pacchatopi olambentī nivāsessanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns wear their sarongs hanging down in front and behind? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo puratopi pacchatopi olambentī nivāsessantī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six wear their sarongs hanging down in front and behind?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo puratopi pacchatopi olambentī nivāsentī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo puratopi pacchatopi olambentī nivāsessanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘“I will wear my sarong evenly all around,” this is how you should train.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: One should wear one’s sarong evenly all around, covering the navel and the knees.

segments.pali: Yā anādariyaṁ paṭicca purato vā pacchato vā olambentī nivāseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun, out of disrespect, wears her sarong hanging down in front or behind, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is unintentional;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is not mindful;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not know;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: (Contracted.)

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on training

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The subchapter on shoes

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 75. Defecating in water

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi karonti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nuns from the group of six were defecating, urinating, and spitting in water.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi karissanti, seyyathāpi gihiniyo kāmabhoginiyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns defecate, urinate, and spit in water? They’re just like householders who indulge in worldly pleasures!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā, tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi karissantī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six defecate, urinate, and spit in water?”

segments.pali: Atha kho bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns told the monks,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who in turn told the Buddha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards the Buddha had the Sangha gathered

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and questioned the monks:

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi karontī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that those nuns do this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi karissanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nuns from the group of six do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Na udake uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā karissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘“I will not defecate, urinate, or spit in water,” this is how you should train.’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena gilānā bhikkhuniyo udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi kātuṁ kukkuccāyanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards, being afraid of wrongdoing, sick nuns did not defecate, urinate, or spit in water.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the Buddha. He then had the Sangha gathered and addressed the monks:

segments.pali: anujānāmi, bhikkhave, gilānāya bhikkhuniyā udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi kātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Monks, I allow a sick nun to defecate, urinate, and spit in water.

segments.pali: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Na udake agilānā uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā karissāmīti sikkhā karaṇīyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘“When not sick, I will not defecate, urinate, or spit in water,” this is how you should train.’”

segments.pali: Na udake agilānāya uccāro vā passāvo vā kheḷo vā kātabbo.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If one is not sick, one should not defecate, urinate, or spit in water.

segments.pali: Yā anādariyaṁ paṭicca udake agilānā uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā karoti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, out of disrespect, a nun who is not sick defecates, urinates, or spits in water, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if it is unintentional;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is not mindful;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does not know;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is sick;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she does it on dry ground, but it then spreads to water;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is deranged;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is overwhelmed by pain;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifteenth training rule is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The seventh subchapter on shoes is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, the rules of training have been recited.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on training is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing suspension

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 1. The training rule on taking legal action

segments.pali: Ime kho panāyyāyo sattarasa saṅghādisesā dhammā uddesaṁ āgacchanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Venerables, these seventeen rules on suspension come up for recitation.

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro upāsako bhikkhunisaṅghassa udositaṁ datvā kālaṅkato hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time a lay follower who had given a storehouse to the Sangha of nuns had died.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He had two sons,

segments.pali: eko assaddho appasanno, eko saddho pasanno.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one with and one without faith and confidence,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they divided their father’s property between them.

segments.pali: Atha kho so assaddho appasanno taṁ saddhaṁ pasannaṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then the one without faith said to the other,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “The storehouse is ours; let’s allocate it to one of us.”

segments.pali: Evaṁ vutte, so saddho pasanno taṁ assaddhaṁ appasannaṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But the one with faith responded,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “No,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: our father gave it to the Sangha of nuns.”

segments.pali: Dutiyampi kho so assaddho appasanno taṁ saddhaṁ pasannaṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time they both said the same thing,

segments.pali: Atha kho so saddho pasanno taṁ assaddhaṁ appasannaṁ etadavoca—
Tatiyampi kho so assaddho appasanno taṁ saddhaṁ pasannaṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time the one without faith repeated his proposal.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The one with faith then thought,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If I get it, I too would give it to the Sangha of nuns,”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and he said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Alright, let’s allocate it.”

segments.pali: Atha kho so udosito tehi bhājīyamāno tassa assaddhassa appasannassa pāpuṇāti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But when they allocated it, it fell to the one without faith.

segments.pali: Atha kho so assaddho appasanno bhikkhuniyo upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He then went to the nuns and said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please leave, venerables, this storehouse is mine.”

segments.pali: Evaṁ vutte thullanandā bhikkhunī taṁ purisaṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun Thullanandā said to him,

segments.pali: “māyyo, evaṁ avaca, tumhākaṁ pitunā bhikkhunisaṅghassa dinno”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “No, your father gave it to the Sangha of nuns.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because they were unable to agree, they asked judges to decide on the matter.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, who knows that it was given to the Sangha of nuns?”

segments.pali: Evaṁ vutte, thullanandā bhikkhunī te mahāmatte etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā replied,

segments.pali: “api nāyyā tumhehi diṭṭhaṁ vā sutaṁ vā sakkhiṁ ṭhapayitvā dānaṁ diyyamānan”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “But sirs, didn’t you appoint a witness who saw or heard the giving of the gift?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Saying,

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kho ayyā āhā”ti taṁ udositaṁ bhikkhunisaṅghassa akaṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true what the venerable says,” the judges made the storehouse the property of the Sangha of nuns.

segments.pali: Atha kho so puriso parājito ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The defeated man complained and criticized the nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “They’re not monastics, these shaven-headed sluts.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: How could they take my storehouse?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā told the judges of this

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and they punished him.

segments.pali: Atha kho so puriso daṇḍito bhikkhunupassayassa avidūre ājīvakaseyyaṁ kārāpetvā ājīvake uyyojesi—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That man then made a dwelling place for Ājīvaka ascetics near the nunnery,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: inciting them to drown out the nuns.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Once again Thullanandā told the judges

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the judges jailed him.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People then complained and criticized those nuns,

segments.pali: “paṭhamaṁ bhikkhuniyo udositaṁ acchindāpesuṁ, dutiyaṁ daṇḍāpesuṁ, tatiyaṁ bandhāpesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “First the nuns take his storehouse, then they have him punished, and then they have him jailed.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Next they’ll have him executed!”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tesaṁ manussānaṁ ujjhāyantānaṁ khiyyantānaṁ vipācentānaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nuns heard the complaints of those people,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā take legal action?”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then those nuns told the monks …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī ussayavādikā viharatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā is taking legal action?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī ussayavādikā viharissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā take legal action?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ussayavādikā vihareyya gahapatinā vā gahapatiputtena vā dāsena vā kammakārena vā antamaso samaṇaparibbājakenāpi, ayaṁ bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun takes legal action against a householder or a householder’s offspring or a slave or a worker or even toward a monastic or a wanderer, then that nun has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Takes legal action:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: what is meant is that she is the initiator of a lawsuit.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A householder:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: anyone who lives at home.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A householder’s offspring:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever is an offspring or a sibling.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A slave:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: one born in the household, one who has been bought, one who has been brought back as a captive.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A worker:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a paid worker, a servant.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A monastic or a wanderer:

segments.pali: bhikkhuñca bhikkhuniñca sikkhamānañca sāmaṇerañca sāmaṇeriñca ṭhapetvā yo koci paribbājakasamāpanno.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: anyone who is a wanderer apart from Buddhist monks, nuns, trainee nuns, novice monks, and novice nuns.

segments.pali: Aḍḍaṁ karissāmīti dutiyaṁ vā pariyesati gacchati vā, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, thinking, “I’ll initiate a lawsuit,” she looks for a companion or just goes there herself, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she tells one other person, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she tells a second person, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At the end of the lawsuit, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An immediate offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

segments.pali: saṅghova tassā āpattiyā mānattaṁ deti mūlāya paṭikassati abbheti, na sambahulā na ekā bhikkhunī. Tena vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: only the Sangha gives the trial period for that offense, sends back to the beginning, and rehabilitates—not several nuns, not an individual nun. Therefore it is called an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Tasseva āpattinikāyassa nāmakammaṁ adhivacanaṁ. Tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the name and designation of this class of offense. Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she goes there because people pull her;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is asking for protection;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she tells without specifying a person;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is deranged;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is overwhelmed by pain;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The first offense entailing suspension is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing suspension

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 2. Ordaining a criminal

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena vesāliyaṁ aññatarassa licchavissa pajāpati aticārinī hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time in Vesālī the wife of a certain Licchavī man was unfaithful.

segments.pali: Atha kho so licchavi taṁ itthiṁ etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: He said to her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Please stop. If you don’t, I’ll punish you.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But she did not listen.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena vesāliyaṁ licchavigaṇo sannipatito hoti kenacideva karaṇīyena.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Just then in Vesālī the Licchavī clan had gathered on some business.

segments.pali: Atha kho so licchavi te licchavayo etadavoca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That Licchavī man said to them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sirs, please give me permission in regard to one of my wives.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “What is it with her?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “She’s unfaithful. I wish to kill her.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “You may go ahead.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When his wife heard

english.author: brahmali
english.text: that her husband wanted to kill her,

segments.pali: Varabhaṇḍaṁ ādāya sāvatthiṁ gantvā titthiye upasaṅkamitvā pabbajjaṁ yāci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she took their most valuable possessions and went to Sāvatthī. There she went to the monastics of other religions and asked for the going forth,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: but they refused.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She then went to the Buddhist nuns and again asked for the going forth,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: but they too refused.

segments.pali: Thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ upasaṅkamitvā bhaṇḍakaṁ dassetvā pabbajjaṁ yāci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She then went to the nun Thullanandā, showed her the goods, and once again asked for the going forth.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā took the goods and gave her the going forth.

segments.pali: Atha kho so licchavi taṁ itthiṁ gavesanto sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhunīsu pabbajitaṁ disvāna yena rājā pasenadi kosalo tenupasaṅkami;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That Licchavī man then went to Sāvatthī in search of his wife. When he saw that she had been given the going forth as a nun, he went to King Pasenadi of Kosala

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and said,

segments.pali: “pajāpati me, deva, varabhaṇḍaṁ ādāya sāvatthiṁ anuppattā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sir, my wife took my most valuable possessions and came to Sāvatthī.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please permit me to deal with her.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Well then, find her and then inform me.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I’ve seen her. She’s gone forth as a nun.”

segments.pali: “Sace, bhaṇe, bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā, na sā labbhā kiñci kātuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “If she’s gone forth as a nun, there’s nothing that can be done.

segments.pali: Svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo, caratu brahmacariyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Teaching of the Buddha is well-proclaimed. Let her practice the spiritual life for the full ending of suffering.”

segments.pali: Atha kho so licchavi ujjhāyati khiyyati vipāceti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then that Licchavī man complained and criticized the nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nuns give the going forth to a criminal?”

segments.pali: Assosuṁ kho bhikkhuniyo tassa licchavissa ujjhāyantassa khiyyantassa vipācentassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns heard the complaints of that Licchavī man,

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā give the going forth to a criminal?”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns told the monks. …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī coriṁ pabbājetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī coriṁ pabbājessati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā give the going forth to a criminal?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī jānaṁ coriṁ vajjhaṁ viditaṁ anapaloketvā rājānaṁ vā saṅghaṁ vā gaṇaṁ vā pūgaṁ vā seṇiṁ vā aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun, without getting permission from the king or the Sangha or a community or an association or a society, knowingly gives the full admission to a female criminal who is known as sentenced to death, then, except when it is allowable, that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She knows:

segments.pali: sāmaṁ vā jānāti aññe vā tassā ārocenti, sā vā āroceti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she knows by herself or others have told her or she has told her.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A female criminal:

segments.pali: yā pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, esā corī nāma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: any female who has stolen anything worth five māsaka coins or more is called “a female criminal”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sentenced to death:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she has been sentenced to death because of her action.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Is known:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: it is known to other people that she has been sentenced to death.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without getting permission from:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: without having asked permission.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The king:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: where a king reigns, permission should be obtained from the king.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: what is meant is the Sangha of nuns, and permission should be obtained from that Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A community:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: where a community governs, permission should be obtained from that community.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An association:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: where an association governs, permission should be obtained from that association.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A society:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: where a society governs, permission should be obtained from that society.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Except when it is allowable:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: unless it is allowable.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Allowable:

segments.pali: dve kappāni— titthiyesu vā pabbajitā hoti aññāsu vā bhikkhunīsu pabbajitā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are two allowable situations: she has gone forth with monastics of another religion or she has gone forth with other Buddhist nuns.

segments.pali: Aññatra kappā “vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā ācariniṁ vā pattaṁ vā cīvaraṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to give the full admission, she searches for a group, a teacher, a bowl, or a robe, or she establishes a monastery zone, then, except when it is allowable, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, the preceptor commits an offense entailing suspension,

segments.pali: Gaṇassa ca ācariniyā ca āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the group and the teacher commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An immediate offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Coriyā corisaññā aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is a criminal, and the nun perceives her as such, and she gives her the full admission, except when it is allowable, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Coriyā vematikā aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is a criminal, but the nun is unsure of it, and she gives her the full admission, except when it is allowable, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Coriyā acorisaññā aññatra kappā vuṭṭhāpeti, anāpatti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is a criminal, but the nun does not perceive her as such, and she gives her the full admission, except when it is allowable, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not a criminal, but the nun perceives her as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not a criminal, but the nun is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she is not a criminal, and the nun does not perceive her as such, there is no offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives her the full admission without knowing that she is a criminal;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives her the full admission after getting permission;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she gives her the full admission when it is allowable;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The second offense entailing suspension is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing suspension

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 3. Being alone

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena bhaddāya kāpilāniyā antevāsinī bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ bhaṇḍitvā gāmakaṁ ñātikulaṁ agamāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a nun who was a pupil of Bhaddā Kāpilānī had an argument with the nuns and then went to her relatives’ village.

segments.pali: Bhaddā kāpilānī taṁ bhikkhuniṁ apassantī bhikkhuniyo pucchi—“kahaṁ itthannāmā, na dissatī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Not seeing her pupil anywhere, Bhaddā Kāpilānī asked the nuns, “Where’s so-and-so? She’s disappeared.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “She disappeared, venerable, after arguing with the nuns.”

segments.pali: “Ammā, amukasmiṁ gāmake etissā ñātikulaṁ. Tattha gantvā vicinathā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “My dears, her relatives live in such-and-such a village. Go there and look for her.”

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo tattha gantvā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ passitvā etadavocuṁ—“kissa tvaṁ, ayye, ekikā āgatā, kaccisi appadhaṁsitā”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns went there, and when they saw her, they said to her, “Why did you go alone, venerable? We hope you weren’t assaulted?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I wasn’t.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun walk to the next village by herself?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā gāmantaraṁ gacchatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā gāmantaraṁ gacchissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena dve bhikkhuniyo sāketā sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On one occasion two nuns were traveling from Sāketa to Sāvatthī.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On the way they had to cross a river.

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo nāvike upasaṅkamitvā etadavocuṁ—“sādhu no, āvuso, tārethā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They went to a boatman and said, “Please take us across.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I’m not able, venerables, to take both of you across at the same time.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And so they crossed individually, alone with the boatman.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When he had crossed with the first nun, he raped her.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And after returning to the first bank, he raped the other nun as well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Later, when they were reunited, they asked each other,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, I hope you weren’t assaulted?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I was.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And you, venerable, were you assaulted?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I was, too.”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo sāvatthiṁ gantvā bhikkhunīnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They then continued on to Sāvatthī and told the nuns there what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun cross a river by herself?”

segments.pali: Atha kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They told the monks,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who in turn told the Buddha. Soon afterwards he had the Sangha gathered and questioned the monks:

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā nadīpāraṁ gacchatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā nadīpāraṁ gacchissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā vā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā nadīpāraṁ gaccheyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself or crosses a river by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ gacchantā sāyaṁ aññataraṁ gāmaṁ upagacchiṁsu.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On one occasion a number of nuns were walking through the Kosalan country on their way to Sāvatthī, when one evening they arrived at a certain village.

segments.pali: Tattha aññatarā bhikkhunī abhirūpā hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā. Aññataro puriso tassā bhikkhuniyā saha dassanena paṭibaddhacitto hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: One of the nuns was beautiful and graceful, and a certain man fell in love with her as soon as he saw her.

segments.pali: Atha kho so puriso tāsaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ seyyaṁ paññapento tassā bhikkhuniyā seyyaṁ ekamantaṁ paññāpesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, as he was preparing sleeping places for those nuns, he prepared hers to one side.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī sallakkhetvā—“pariyuṭṭhito ayaṁ puriso; sace rattiṁ āgacchissati, vissaro me bhavissatī”ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And that nun thought, “This man is obsessed with me. If I go there for the night, I’ll get into trouble.”

segments.pali: bhikkhuniyo anāpucchā aññataraṁ kulaṁ gantvā seyyaṁ kappesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Then, without informing the nuns, she went to a certain family and slept there.

segments.pali: Atha kho so puriso rattiṁ āgantvā taṁ bhikkhuniṁ gavesanto bhikkhuniyo ghaṭṭesi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When night arrived, that man went searching for that nun, and as he did so he bumped into the other nuns.

segments.pali: Bhikkhuniyo taṁ bhikkhuniṁ apassantiyo evamāhaṁsu—“nissaṁsayaṁ kho sā bhikkhunī purisena saddhiṁ nikkhantā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Not seeing that nun anywhere, the nuns said, “No doubt she has left with a man.”

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī tassā rattiyā accayena yena tā bhikkhuniyo tenupasaṅkami. Bhikkhuniyo taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—“kissa tvaṁ, ayye, purisena saddhiṁ nikkhantā”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The following morning that nun returned to the nuns, and they said to her, “Venerable, why did you leave with a man?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I didn’t leave with a man, venerables.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She then told the nuns what had happened.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun spend the night apart by herself?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā rattiṁ vippavasatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā rattiṁ vippavasissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā vā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā nadīpāraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā rattiṁ vippavaseyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself or crosses a river by herself or spends the night apart by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

segments.pali: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the nuns.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhuniyo kosalesu janapade sāvatthiṁ addhānamaggappaṭipannā honti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On one occasion a number of nuns were traveling through the Kosalan country on their way to Sāvatthī.

segments.pali: Tattha aññatarā bhikkhunī vaccena pīḷitā ekikā ohīyitvā pacchā agamāsi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: One of the nuns, needing to defecate, stayed behind by herself, and then followed behind the others.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: People saw her and raped her.

segments.pali: Atha kho sā bhikkhunī yena tā bhikkhuniyo tenupasaṅkami. Bhikkhuniyo taṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavocuṁ—“kissa tvaṁ, ayye, ekikā ohīnā, kaccisi appadhaṁsitā”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She then went to the other nuns, and they said to her, “Why did you stay behind by yourself, venerable? We hope you weren’t assaulted?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I was.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma bhikkhunī ekā gaṇamhā ohīyissatīti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun lag behind her companions by herself?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā gaṇamhā ohīyatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī ekā gaṇamhā ohīyissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun do this?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And so, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī ekā vā gāmantaraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā nadīpāraṁ gaccheyya, ekā vā rattiṁ vippavaseyya, ekā vā gaṇamhā ohīyeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun walks to the next inhabited area by herself or crosses a river by herself or spends the night apart by herself or lags behind her companions by herself, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Walks to the next inhabited area by herself:

segments.pali: parikkhittassa gāmassa parikkhepaṁ paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti thullaccayassa,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she crosses the boundary of an enclosed inhabited area with her first foot, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then crosses it with her second foot, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Aparikkhittassa gāmassa upacāraṁ paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmentiyā āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she enters the vicinity of an unenclosed inhabited area with her first foot, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then enters it with her second foot, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Or crosses a river by herself:

segments.pali: nadī nāma timaṇḍalaṁ paṭicchādetvā yattha katthaci uttarantiyā bhikkhuniyā antaravāsako temiyati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A river: wherever, after covering the three circles, the sarong gets wet when the nun is crossing.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she has crossed with the first foot, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she has crossed with the second foot, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Or spends the night apart by herself:

segments.pali: saha aruṇuggamanā dutiyikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthapāsaṁ vijahantiyā āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, at dawn, she is in the process of going beyond arm’s reach of her companion nun, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she has gone beyond, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Or lags behind her companions by herself:

segments.pali: agāmake araññe dutiyikāya bhikkhuniyā dassanūpacāraṁ vā savanūpacāraṁ vā vijahantiyā āpatti thullaccayassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if, in an uninhabited area, in the wilderness, she is in the process of going beyond the range of sight or the range of hearing of her companion nun, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When she has gone beyond, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An immediate offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

segments.pali: dutiyikā bhikkhunī pakkantā vā hoti vibbhantā vā kālaṅkatā vā pakkhasaṅkantā vā,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if her companion nun has left or disrobed or died or joined another religion or sect;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if there is an emergency;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The third offense entailing suspension is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Theravāda Collection on Monastic Law

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Nuns’ Analysis

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing suspension

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 4. Readmitting an ejected nun

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī bhaṇḍanakārikā hoti kalahakārikā vivādakārikā bhassakārikā saṅghe adhikaraṇakārikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Caṇḍakāḷī was quarrelsome and argumentative, and she created legal issues in the Sangha.

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī tassā kamme karīyamāne paṭikkosati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But when a legal procedure was being done against her, the nun Thullanandā objected.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī gāmakaṁ agamāsi kenacideva karaṇīyena.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Soon afterwards Thullanandā went to a village on some business.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha of nuns

segments.pali: “thullanandā bhikkhunī pakkantā”ti, caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ āpattiyā adassane ukkhipi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: took the opportunity to eject Caṇḍakāḷī for not recognizing an offense.

segments.pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī gāmake taṁ karaṇīyaṁ tīretvā punadeva sāvatthiṁ paccāgacchi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When Thullanandā had finished her business in that village, she returned to Sāvatthī.

segments.pali: Caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā āgacchantiyā neva āsanaṁ paññapesi na pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakaṭhalikaṁ upanikkhipi na paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi na pānīyena āpucchi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: On her return, Caṇḍakāḷī neither prepared a seat for her, nor set out a foot stool, a foot scraper, or water for washing the feet; and she did not go out to meet her to receive her bowl and robe, nor ask whether she wanted water to drink.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Thullanandā asked her

segments.pali: “kissa tvaṁ, ayye, mayi āgacchantiyā neva āsanaṁ paññapesi na pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakaṭhalikaṁ upanikkhipi na paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi na pānīyena āpucchī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: why she was acting like this.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: She replied, “That’s how it is, venerable, when you don’t have a protector.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “But how is it, venerable, that you don’t have a protector?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “When the nuns knew

segments.pali: ‘ayaṁ anāthā appaññātā, natthi imissā kāci paṭivattā’ti, āpattiyā adassane ukkhipiṁsū”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: that no-one would speak up for me because I am not esteemed by them and I didn’t have a protector, they ejected me for not recognizing an offense.”

segments.pali: “bālā etā abyattā etā, neva jānanti kammaṁ vā kammadosaṁ vā kammavipattiṁ vā kammasampattiṁ vā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “They are incompetent fools. They don’t know about legal procedures or their flaws, nor what makes them fail or succeed.

segments.pali: Mayaṁ kho jānāma kammampi kammadosampi kammavipattimpi kammasampattimpi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: But we know all these things.

segments.pali: Mayaṁ kho akataṁ vā kammaṁ kāreyyāma kataṁ vā kammaṁ kopeyyāmā”ti, lahuṁ lahuṁ bhikkhunisaṅghaṁ sannipātetvā caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ osāresi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: We can get legal procedures done that haven’t been done, and we can get procedures that have been done overturned.” And she quickly gathered a sangha of nuns and readmitted the nun Caṇḍakāḷī.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāressatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā readmit a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāretī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Thullanandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāressati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā readmit a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāreyya, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun, without getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community, readmits a nun who has been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching and the Monastic Law and the Teacher’s instruction, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A unanimous Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: those belonging to the same Buddhist sect and staying within the same monastery zone.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Who has been ejected:

segments.pali: āpattiyā adassane vā appaṭikamme vā appaṭinissagge vā ukkhittā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: who has been ejected for not recognizing an offense, for not making amends, or for not giving up a bad view.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In accordance with the Teaching and the Monastic Law:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in accordance with that Teaching, in accordance with that Monastic Law.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In accordance with the Teacher’s instruction:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: in accordance with the Victor’s instruction, in accordance with the Buddha’s instruction.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: without having asked permission of the Sangha that did the legal procedure of ejection.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Without the consent of the community:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: without being aware of any consent from the community.

segments.pali: “Osāressāmī”ti gaṇaṁ vā pariyesati, sīmaṁ vā sammannati, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, intending to readmit her, she searches for a group or establishes a monastery zone, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā, kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense. When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An immediate offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she readmits her, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she readmits her, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she readmits her, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she readmits her after getting permission from the Sangha that did the procedure;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she readmits her with the consent of the community;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she readmits one who is behaving properly;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she readmits her when the Sangha that did the procedure is unavailable;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fourth offense entailing suspension is finished.
