english.author: brahmali
english.text: 5. Receiving food with lust

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī abhirūpā hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time there was a beautiful nun called Sundarīnandā.

segments.pali: Manussā bhattagge sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ passitvā avassutā avassutāya sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā aggamaggāni bhojanāni denti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When people saw her in the dining hall, they gave her the best food, both the donors and the recipient having lust.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Sundarīnandā ate as much as she liked,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: but not so the other nuns.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā sundarīnandā avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khādissati bhuñjissatī”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Sundarīnandā, being lustful, eat either fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khādati bhuñjatī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Sundarīnandā did this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khādissati bhuñjissati.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Sundarīnandā, being lustful, eat either fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khādeyya vā bhuñjeyya vā, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a lustful nun eats fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man, then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lustful:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: having lust, longing for, in love with.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Lustful:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: having lust, longing for, in love with.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Man:

segments.pali: manussapuriso, na yakkho na peto na tiracchānagato, viññū paṭibalo sārajjituṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a human male, not a male spirit, not a male ghost, not a male animal. He understands and is capable of having lust.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Fresh food:

segments.pali: pañca bhojanāni udakadantaponaṁ ṭhapetvā avasesaṁ khādanīyaṁ nāma.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: apart from the five cooked foods, water, and tooth cleaners, the rest is called “fresh food”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Cooked food:

segments.pali: pañca bhojanāni—odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are five kinds of cooked food: cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An immediate offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If only the man has lust, and she receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Ubhatoavassute yakkhassa vā petassa vā paṇḍakassa vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahassa vā hatthato “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If both of them have lust, and she receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it directly from a male spirit, a male ghost, a paṇḍaka, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If only the male being has lust, and she receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if both are without lust;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she receives, knowing that the man has no lust;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The fifth offense entailing suspension is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 6. Encouraging a nun to receive food from a man with lust

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena sundarīnandā bhikkhunī abhirūpā hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time there was a beautiful nun called Sundarīnandā.

segments.pali: Manussā bhattagge sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ passitvā avassutā sundarīnandāya bhikkhuniyā aggamaggāni bhojanāni denti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When people saw her in the dining hall, they were affected by lust, and they gave her the best food.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Being afraid of wrongdoing, Sundarīnandā did not receive it.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun next to her said,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Why didn’t you receive it, venerable?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Because they have lust.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “But do you have lust?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “No.”

segments.pali: “Kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “What can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, when you’re without it?

segments.pali: Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā, bhuñja vā”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands, and eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun say,

segments.pali: ‘kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘What can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, when you’re without it?

segments.pali: Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā’”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands, and eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you’?” …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that a nun said this?”

segments.pali: ‘kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā yato tvaṁ anavassutā.
Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā’”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī evaṁ vakkhati—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could a nun say this?”

segments.pali: ‘kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā yato tvaṁ anavassutā;
iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī evaṁ vadeyya— ‘kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā. Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā’ti, ayampi bhikkhunī paṭhamāpattikaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun says, “Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you,” then that nun too has committed an immediate offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Says:

segments.pali: “kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā. Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she urges her on, saying, “Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you,” then she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Tassā vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, because of her statement, the other nun receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At the end of the meal, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: An immediate offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense as soon as the misconduct is committed, and no pressing is required.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she urges her on to receive water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Tassā vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, because of her statement, the other nun receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Ekatoavassute yakkhassa vā petassa vā paṇḍakassa vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggahassa vā hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā “khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti uyyojeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, when only the male being has lust, she urges her on to eat fresh or cooked food received directly from a male spirit, a male ghost, a paṇḍaka, or a male animal in human form, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Tassā vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, because of her statement, the other nun receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At the end of the meal, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she urges her on to receive water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

segments.pali: Tassā vacanena “khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If, because of her statement, the other nun receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she urges her on, knowing that he has no lust;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she urges her on, thinking that she is not receiving because of anger;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she urges her on, thinking that she is not receiving because of compassion for the family;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The sixth offense entailing suspension is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 10. Renouncing the training in anger

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time when the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery,

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ bhaṇḍitvā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: the nun Caṇḍakāḷī had argued with the nuns. In anger she said this:

segments.pali: “buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training!

segments.pali: Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro, santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā caṇḍakāḷī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vakkhati—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Caṇḍakāḷī say this in anger?”

segments.pali: Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro, santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’”ti …pe…
“saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī said this in anger?”

segments.pali: Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro, santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vakkhati—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Caṇḍakāḷī say this in anger?

segments.pali: Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro, santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeyya—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun says in anger,

segments.pali: ‘buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi. Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro. Santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them,”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: ‘māyye, kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca— buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi. Kinnumāva samaṇiyo yā samaṇiyo sakyadhītaro. Santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā, tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmīti, abhiramāyye, svākkhāto dhammo; cara brahmacariyaṁ sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, don’t say such things in anger, ‘I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training! The Sakyan daughters are not the only monastics. There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.’ Take delight, venerable; the Teaching is well proclaimed. Practice the spiritual life for the complete ending of suffering.”

segments.pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya iccetaṁ kusalaṁ;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then stops, all is well.

segments.pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī yāvatatiyakaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, then after the third announcement that nun too has committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

There are other monastics who have a sense of conscience, who are afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training. I’ll practice the spiritual life with them.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so says this in anger:

segments.pali: ‘buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmi.

segments.pali: Santaññāpi samaṇiyo lajjiniyo kukkuccikā sikkhākāmā tāsāhaṁ santike brahmacariyaṁ carissāmī’ti.

english.author: brahmali

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

segments.pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantiyā ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For one who commits the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the third announcement:

segments.pali: yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya āpajjati, na saha vatthujjhācārā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense when she has been pressed for the third time, not as soon as the misconduct has been committed.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has not been pressed;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stops;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The tenth offense entailing suspension is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 11. Accusing nuns of acting from bias

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Caṇḍakāḷī was angry that she had lost a legal case, saying,

segments.pali: “chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā caṇḍakāḷī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vakkhati—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can Venerable Caṇḍakāḷī say such things just because she’s angry that she has lost a legal case?”

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that the nun Caṇḍakāḷī says this because she’s angry?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vakkhati—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can the nun Caṇḍakāḷī say such things just because she’s angry that she has lost a legal case?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Yā pana bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeyya—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun is angry because she has lost a legal case, saying,

segments.pali: ‘chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo’ti,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear,”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

segments.pali: ‘māyye, kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, just because you’re angry that you’ve lost a legal case, don’t say,

segments.pali: chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyoti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.’

segments.pali: Ayyā kho chandāpi gaccheyya, dosāpi gaccheyya, mohāpi gaccheyya, bhayāpi gaccheyyā’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Perhaps it’s you who are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.”

segments.pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali

segments.pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ;

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, then after the third announcement that nun too has committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A legal issue:

segments.pali: adhikaraṇaṁ nāma cattāri adhikaraṇāni— vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there are four kinds of legal issues: legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, legal issues arising from business.

english.author: brahmali

segments.pali: “māyye, kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.’

segments.pali: Ayyā kho chandāpi gaccheyya …pe… bhayāpi gaccheyyā”ti.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha:

segments.pali: ‘Suṇātu me, ayye, saṅgho. Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. The nun so-and-so, because she’s angry that she has lost a legal case, says this:

segments.pali: “chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo… pe… bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she keeps on saying it.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so, because she’s angry that she has lost a legal case, says this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she keeps on saying it.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

segments.pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For one who commits the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and the serious offenses are annulled.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the third announcement:

segments.pali: yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya āpajjati, na saha vatthujjhācārā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense when she has been pressed for the third time, not as soon as the misconduct has been committed.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has not been pressed;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stops;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The eleventh offense entailing suspension is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 12. Inappropriate socializing

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandāya bhikkhuniyā antevāsikā bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nuns who were the pupils of the nun Thullanandā were socializing and behaving badly, had a bad reputation, and were harassing the Sangha of nuns and hiding each other’s offenses.

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized them,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharissanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā”ti …pe…

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns behave in this way?” …

segments.pali: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that nuns are behaving like this?”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked them …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharissanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How can nuns behave in this way?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

segments.pali: “Bhikkhuniyo paneva saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If nuns socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the nuns should correct them like this:

segments.pali: ‘bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”

segments.pali: Evañca tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyyuṁ, tā bhikkhuniyo bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If those nuns continue as before, the nuns should press them up to three times to make them stop.

segments.pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyyuṁ, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they then stop, all is well.

segments.pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyyuṁ, imāpi bhikkhuniyo yāvatatiyakaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they do not stop, then after the third announcement those nuns too have committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nuns:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: what is meant is that they are fully ordained.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Socialize:

segments.pali: saṁsaṭṭhā nāma ananulomikena kāyikavācasikena saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they socialize with improper bodily and verbal action.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Behave badly:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: having bad behavior.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Have a bad reputation:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: a bad reputation has spread about them.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Are notorious:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they make a living by means of a bad and wrong livelihood.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Harass the Sangha of nuns:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they object on each other’s behalf when a legal procedure is being carried out against them.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Hide each other’s offenses:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they hide one another’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Them:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: those nuns who socialize.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns:

segments.pali: aññāhi bhikkhunīhi. Yā passanti yā suṇanti tāhi vattabbā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct them like this:

segments.pali: “bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they should correct them a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they stop, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they do not stop, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those nuns, even if they have to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this:

segments.pali: “bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They should correct them a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they stop, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If they do not stop, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Should press them:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, they should be pressed like this.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Itthannāmā ca itthannāmā ca bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns so-and-so and so-and-so socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they keep on doing it.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhuniyo samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nuns so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Itthannāmā ca itthannāmā ca bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns so-and-so and so-and-so socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they keep on doing it.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhuniyo samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha presses the nuns so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya ca itthannāmāya ca bhikkhunīnaṁ samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of pressing the nuns so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

segments.pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena, itthannāmā ca itthannāmā ca bhikkhuniyo tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has pressed the nuns so-and-so and so-and-so to make them stop.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, they commit a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, they commit an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantīnaṁ ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For those who commit the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled.

segments.pali: Dve tisso ekato samanubhāsitabbā. Tatuttari na samanubhāsitabbā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Two or three may be pressed together, but not more than that.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Those nuns too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the third announcement:

segments.pali: yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya āpajjanti, na saha vatthujjhācārā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense when they have been pressed for the third time, not as soon as the misconduct has been committed.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: they are sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and they perceive it as such, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but they are unsure of it, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but they perceive it as illegitimate, and they do not stop, they commit an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but they perceive it as legitimate, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but they are unsure of it, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and they perceive it as such, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they have not been pressed;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they stop;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they are insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if they are the first offenders.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The twelfth offense entailing suspension is finished.

english.author: brahmali

english.author: brahmali
english.text: 13. Encouraging inappropriate socializing

segments.pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.

segments.pali: Tena kho pana samayena thullanandā bhikkhunī saṅghena samanubhaṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: At that time the nun Thullanandā had been pressed by the Sangha. She then said to the nuns,

segments.pali: “saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately.

segments.pali: Santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Tā saṅgho na kiñci āha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety,  and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. The Sangha says nothing to them.

segments.pali: Tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha— ‘bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It’s because of disrespect, contempt, impatience, and slander, and because you are weak that the Sangha says to you, ‘Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.’”

segments.pali: Yā tā bhikkhuniyo appicchā …pe… tā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns of few desires complained and criticized her,

segments.pali: “kathañhi nāma ayyā thullanandā saṅghena samanubhaṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo evaṁ vakkhati—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could Venerable Thullanandā, after being pressed by the Sangha, say this to the nuns?”

segments.pali: saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.
“saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī saṅghena samanubhaṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Is it true, monks, that after being pressed by the Sangha, the nun Thullanandā said this to the nuns?”

segments.pali: saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.
Santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Tā saṅgho na kiñci āha.

Tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha— bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī”ti?

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “It’s true, sir.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Buddha rebuked her …

segments.pali: kathañhi nāma, bhikkhave, thullanandā bhikkhunī saṅghena samanubhaṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo evaṁ vakkhati—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “How could the nun Thullanandā, after being pressed by the Sangha, say this to the nuns?

segments.pali: saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This will affect people’s confidence …” …

segments.pali: evañca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuniyo imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddisantu—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, the nuns should recite this training rule like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘If a nun says,

segments.pali: ‘saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately.

segments.pali: Santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Tā saṅgho na kiñci āha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. The Sangha says nothing to them.

segments.pali: Tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha— bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It’s because of disrespect, contempt, impatience, and slander, and because you are weak that the Sangha says to you, ‘Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters,’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: then the nuns should correct her like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, don’t say such things:

segments.pali: saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately.

segments.pali: Santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Tā saṅgho na kiñci āha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. The Sangha says nothing to them.

segments.pali: Tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha— bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Viviccathāyye. Vivekaññeva bhaginīnaṁ saṅgho vaṇṇetī’ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It’s because of disrespect, contempt, impatience, and slander, and because you are weak that the Sangha says to you, “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses. Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”’”

segments.pali: Evañca sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi vuccamānā tatheva paggaṇheyya, sā bhikkhunī bhikkhunīhi yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsitabbā tassa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun continues as before, the nuns should press her up to three times to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yāvatatiyañce samanubhāsīyamānā taṁ paṭinissajjeyya, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she then stops, all is well.

segments.pali: no ce paṭinissajjeyya, ayampi bhikkhunī yāvatatiyakaṁ dhammaṁ āpannā nissāraṇīyaṁ saṅghādisesan”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, then after the third announcement that nun too has committed an offense entailing sending away and suspension.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: whoever …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Nun:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: … The nun who has been given the full ordination in unanimity by both Sanghas through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of nun is meant in this case.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Says:

segments.pali: “saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately.

segments.pali: Santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā. Tā saṅgho na kiñci āha”.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses. The Sangha says nothing to them.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It’s because of disrespect that the Sangha … to you:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: because of despising.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because of contempt:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: because of contemptuousness.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because of impatience:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: because of irritation.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because of slander:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: because of slandering.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Because … weak:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: because of lack of supporters.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Says:

segments.pali: “bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: that nun who speaks thus.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nuns:

segments.pali: aññāhi bhikkhunīhi. Yā passanti yā suṇanti tāhi vattabbā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: other nuns who see it or hear about it. They should correct her like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, don’t say such things:

segments.pali: ‘saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “… Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And they should correct her a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If those who hear about it do not say anything, they commit an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That nun, even if she has to be pulled into the Sangha, should be corrected like this:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerable, don’t say such things:

segments.pali: ‘saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharittha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “… Be secluded, venerables. The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.”’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: They should correct her a second

english.author: brahmali
english.text: and a third time.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she stops, all is well.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If she does not stop, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Should press her:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “And, monks, she should be pressed like this.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A competent and capable nun should inform the Sangha:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī saṅghena samanubhaṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so, after being pressed by the Sangha, says this to the nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, you should socialize.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Don’t live separately.

segments.pali: Santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha says nothing to them.

segments.pali: Tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It’s because of disrespect, contempt, impatience, and slander, and because you are weak that the Sangha says to you,

segments.pali: bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerables.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she keeps on saying it.

segments.pali: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If the Sangha is ready, it should press the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the motion.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

segments.pali: Ayaṁ itthannāmā bhikkhunī saṅghena samanubhaṭṭhā bhikkhuniyo evaṁ vadeti—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The nun so-and-so, after being pressed by the Sangha, says this to the nuns,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, you should socialize.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Don’t live separately.

segments.pali: Santi saṅghe aññāpi bhikkhuniyo evācārā evaṁsaddā evaṁsilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There are other nuns in the Sangha who have such behavior, reputation, and notoriety, and who harass the Sangha of nuns and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha says nothing to them.

segments.pali: Tumhaññeva saṅgho uññāya paribhavena akkhantiyā vebhassiyā dubbalyā evamāha—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: It’s because of disrespect, contempt, impatience, and slander, and because you are weak that the Sangha says to you,

segments.pali: bhaginiyo kho saṁsaṭṭhā viharanti pāpācārā pāpasaddā pāpasilokā, bhikkhunisaṅghassa vihesikā, aññamaññissā vajjappaṭicchādikā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Sisters, you socialize, behave badly, have a bad reputation, are notorious, harass the Sangha of nuns, and hide each other’s offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Be secluded, venerables.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha praises seclusion for the sisters.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: And she keeps on saying it.

segments.pali: Saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuniṁ samanubhāsati tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha presses the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Yassā ayyāya khamati itthannāmāya bhikkhuniyā samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya, sā tuṇhassa;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who approves of pressing the nun so-and-so to make her stop should remain silent.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Any nun who doesn’t approve should speak up.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

segments.pali: Samanubhaṭṭhā saṅghena itthannāmā bhikkhunī tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha has pressed the nun so-and-so to make her stop.

segments.pali: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I will remember it thus.’”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjantiyā ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ, dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā paṭippassambhanti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: For one who commits the offense entailing suspension, the offense of wrong conduct and the serious offenses are annulled.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: That too:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: After the third announcement:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: there is an offense when she has been pressed for the third time, not as soon as the misconduct has been committed.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Entailing sending away:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: she is sent away from the Sangha.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: Suspension:

segments.pali: saṅghova tassā āpattiyā mānattaṁ deti, mūlāya paṭikassati, abbheti, na sambahulā na ekā bhikkhunī. Tena vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: only the Sangha gives the trial period for that offense, sends back to the beginning, and rehabilitates—not several nuns, not an individual nun. Therefore it is called an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Tasseva āpattinikāyassa nāmakammaṁ adhivacanaṁ, tenapi vuccati “saṅghādiseso”ti.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: This is the name and designation of this class of offense. Therefore, too, it is called an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme dhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme vematikā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

segments.pali: Dhammakamme adhammakammasaññā na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is a legitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as illegitimate, and she does not stop, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she perceives it as legitimate, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, but she is unsure of it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If it is an illegitimate legal procedure, and she perceives it as such, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: There is no offense:

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she has not been pressed;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she stops;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is insane;

english.author: brahmali
english.text: if she is the first offender.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The thirteenth offense entailing suspension is finished.

segments.pali: Uddiṭṭhā kho, ayyāyo, sattarasa saṅghādisesā dhammā—

english.author: brahmali
english.text: “Venerables, the seventeen rules on suspension have been recited,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: nine being immediate offenses, eight after the third announcement.

segments.pali: Yesaṁ bhikkhunī aññataraṁ vā aññataraṁ vā āpajjati, tāya bhikkhuniyā ubhatosaṅghe pakkhamānattaṁ caritabbaṁ.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If a nun commits any one of them, she must undertake the trial period for a half-month toward both Sanghas.

segments.pali: Ciṇṇamānattā bhikkhunī yattha siyā vīsatigaṇo bhikkhunisaṅgho tattha sā bhikkhunī abbhetabbā.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: When this is completed, she is to be rehabilitated wherever there is a sangha of at least twenty nuns.

segments.pali: Ekāyapi ce ūno vīsatigaṇo bhikkhunisaṅgho taṁ bhikkhuniṁ abbheyya.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: If that nun is rehabilitated by a sangha of nuns of even one less than twenty,

segments.pali: Sā ca bhikkhunī anabbhitā, tā ca bhikkhuniyo gārayhā, ayaṁ tattha sāmīci.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: that nun is not rehabilitated and those nuns are at fault. This is the proper procedure.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: In regard to this I ask you,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A second time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: A third time I ask,

english.author: brahmali
english.text: ‘Are you pure in this?’

english.author: brahmali
english.text: You are pure in this and therefore silent. I will remember it thus.”

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The group of seventeen is finished.

english.author: brahmali
english.text: The chapter on offenses entailing suspension in the Nuns’ Analysis is finished.
